purpose: A summonable neuron mesh over the RAPP ecosystem: one rapp-neuron/1.0 card per public spec/HTML surface, understood word-level. Re-instantiate a neuron from its card + live file to answer any drift/audit question; fan the relevant neurons out in a loop for all-encompassing coverage. Built by the build-ecosystem-neuron-mesh workflow.
repos: RAPP Network Specification — specs/SPEC.md (the god spec)
installer/index.html — RAPP v1 frozen digital twin (2026-04-17)
kody-w/RAPP — network protocol + doorman pattern documentation
kody-w/rapp-egg-hub — Public hub documentation, specifications, and distribution mechanism for portable digital twins (organisms with identity, voice, memory, lineage, and cartridges)
rapp_brainstem/README.md — Brainstem server documentation including request flow, agent system design, auth chain, import shims, and memory agent handling
specs/SPEC.md — RAPP Protocol — The Network Spec

contracts: Rappid is the global address: derivable by string parsing alone from the format rappid:@<owner>/<slug>:<64-hex-no-dashes>
The rappid string is NEVER re-versioned—consolidates all prior forms and is locked in Eternity form
Every consumer MUST use door_from_rappid() from tools/door_address.py; per-consumer reimplementation is forbidden
The nine canonical URLs (repo, front, identity, holocard, holo_md, avatar, summon_qr, members, facets) are derivable from rappid by parsing alone—no API, no lookup
Estate is recomputable from public data given only the operator's GitHub handle (disaster recovery property)
Every planted door MUST set parent_rappid to the planter's personal rappid in rappid.json—enforced constitutionally
Spec says what is true; the network has no fallbacks—invalid rappids raise errors, never silently fixed
Discovery is pure raw fetch across four substrates (GitHub raw, LAN Bonjour, AirDrop/egg, sneakernet file://) all using the same protocol
Federation is a graph, not a tree—any beacon can be a starting point; the seed is convenient but not authoritative
Two-tier estate mandatory: public for discovery (rapp-estate) and private for real work (rapp-estate-private)
Private estate uses URL opacity (content-addressed or HMAC'd paths) revealing nothing to unauthorized viewers
Slot delimiters (|||VOICE|||, |||TWIN|||) are fixed forever—never repurposed, overloaded, or removed
discovery.indexable consent flag honored uniformly across all substrates (robots.txt-style opt-out)
canonical_phrases: The rappid encodes the address. The address encodes the door. The door encodes the contract. There is no other map.
Spec says what's true; the network has no fallbacks.
The string is NEVER re-versioned.
Consumers MUST NOT silently fix up identity in place.
Every consumer MUST use this one function (or a faithful port). Per-consumer reimplementation is forbidden.
The network has no registry. Becoming part of the federation = publishing your estate per spec.
No centralized substrate is load-bearing for federation—peers find you on whatever substrate you publish to.
One protocol on every substrate.
Strictness is one-time; laxity is permanent.
Federation is a graph, not a tree.

neurons.summary: The RAPP network protocol spec defines federation, addressing, and discovery across public infrastructure: the rappid (globally-resolvable address derivable by string parsing alone, locked in consolidated Eternity form 2026-06-03) is the sole identity construct; every door publishes nine canonical files fetchable from raw.githubusercontent.com URLs; the estate catalogs owned and joined doors and is recomputable from public data; discovery walks a graph of beacons via pure raw fetch across four substrate tiers (GitHub, LAN, egg, sneakernet) with zero centralized infrastructure required; and all primitives enforce strictness-is-final semantics (invalid rappids error rather than silently fix, derived fields never stored, slots frozen forever) backed by constitutional articles.

pattern.gap: Cubby-neighborhood pattern is referenced in user context (NEIGHBORHOOD_PROTOCOL §19, ECOSYSTEM_MAP §5 PR #59, schemas rapp-cubby/1.0, rapp-super-rar/1.0, rapp-payphone-dial/1.0, brainstem-egg/2.3-cubby) but not explicitly detailed in this spec—the spec defines the foundational protocol layers (rappid, estate, federation) upon which neighborhood patterns and dark-door collaborator-gated mechanisms operate.
pattern.change: Private estate two-tier model with URL opacity (XLVIII.6) and private extension beacon fields newly introduced to the spec—this is the infrastructure layer that supports cubby-neighborhood private collaboration patterns, though the neighborhood-specific governance mechanics live in SUBMISSION_PROTOCOL.md.

neurons.summary: Action-oriented runbook for network citizenship: six-step procedure for any AI to mint a personal rappid, plant a front door, emit a two-tier estate, summon existing doors, join gates, publish, and optionally federate over LAN when GitHub is unavailable. The network reduces to fetch/write/shell primitives with no servers, no auth tokens, no infrastructure beyond GitHub Pages + raw.githubusercontent.com.
contracts: A personal rappid is required identity for every network participant; it anchors every door created and proves membership in every gate joined.
Every front door publishes exactly nine canonical URLs (identity/card/avatar/summon_qr/holo_md/members/facets/repo/front) derivable by string parsing alone from the rappid.

Estate entries store ONLY {rappid, added_at, via}; all other fields (owner, repo, kind, door_type, URLs) must be derived at read-time via door_from_rappid() — storing derived fields is forbidden (SPEC §4.2).

Per Article XLVIII, estate publication is atomically two-tier: creates BOTH public rapp-estate AND private rapp-estate-private repos simultaneously; a public-only estate is legacy mode (toy), not recommended for real work involving identifiable parties.

The network operates on GitHub as default substrate (via raw.githubusercontent.com) but is substrate-agnostic: Article XLVII.5 + XLVII.5.1 provide LAN-federation equivalents using identical JSON shapes, door_from_rappid() parser, and beacon schema, routing through Bonjour multicast + local HTTP when GitHub is unavailable.
Every consumer MUST use the canonical door_from_rappid() function (from tools/door_address.py or faithful port); per-consumer reimplementation is forbidden.

Antipatterns (verbatim from SPEC §11): Don't reinvent the rappid parser. Don't store derived fields. Don't patch invalid rappids (reissue them). Don't auto-execute destructive actions. Don't fall back to RAPP branding (speak in door's identity). Don't ship half-features. Don't break local-first.
canonical_phrases: You are reading the network-participation runbook. Feed me to any AI. After reading me they can participate in the global RAPP network as a 1st-class citizen.

The whole network reduces to six actions. Each one is a fetch, a write, or a shell command — no servers to provision, no auth tokens to manage, no infrastructure beyond GitHub Pages + raw.githubusercontent.com.
Your personal rappid is your identity passport. It anchors every door you create and proves your membership in every gate you join.
Your front door is a public chat surface anyone can summon. It's a GitHub repo with a fixed canonical file set at predictable URLs (SPEC §3).

Your estate has two sides per Article XLVIII (mandatory two-tier from minute 1): Public side — <your-handle>/rapp-estate — discovery surface. Private side — <your-handle>/rapp-estate-private (PRIVATE GitHub repo) — substance surface (PII, contacts, mailbox content, conversation history).

Each entry stores ONLY {rappid, added_at, via}. All other fields (owner, repo, kind, door_type, summon URL, holocard URL) are DERIVED at read time via door_from_rappid(). Storing derived fields is forbidden.

GitHub is the default substrate but not the only one. Article XLVII.5 + XLVII.5.1 give you a substrate-equivalent LAN federation that uses the SAME JSON shapes, the SAME door_from_rappid() parser, the SAME beacon schema.
You are now indistinguishable from any other 1st-class citizen. The network is a graph; you are a node. Other nodes can summon you; you can summon them.
The protocol is the spec. The skill is what you do with the spec.

pattern.gap: The file explicitly positions itself as action-oriented network-layer runbook (six steps to citizenship) and references the cubby-neighborhood / dark-door pattern via Article XLVII.5 LAN-federation semantics (payphone handshake, substrate-agnostic addressing, local-first substrate), but does NOT directly name nor implement the full neighborhood pattern schemas (rapp-cubby/1.0, rapp-super-rar/1.0, rapp-payphone-dial/1.0, brainstem-egg/2.3-cubby, rapp-ecosystem-graph/1.0, rapp-canon/1.0, rapp-drift-report/1.0, rapp-drift-issue/1.0, rapp-rar-steward/1.0). The pattern is IMPLICITLY covered through the two-tier estate model, bilateral-channel mechanics (SMB-mounted shared folder / HTTP POST between LAN brainstems), and substrate-agnosticism, but NOT EXPLICITLY declared.
pattern.change: Gap closure would require either: (A) explicit reference to the schema set {rapp-cubby/1.0, rapp-super-rar/1.0, rapp-payphone-dial/1.0, brainstem-egg/2.3-cubby, rapp-ecosystem-graph/1.0, rapp-canon/1.0, rapp-drift-report/1.0, rapp-drift-issue/1.0, rapp-rar-steward/1.0} in a new section on private-cubby-neighborhood mechanics and agent names (@rapp/rapp, @rapp/drift, @rapp/drift_watcher, @rapp/rar_steward), OR (B) an explicit pointer from this file to a sibling NEIGHBORHOOD_PATTERN.md that spells out the dark-door / payphone / egg-roundtrip / stream-don't-commit mechanisms at network layer.

neurons.summary: Frozen RAPP v1 specification: single-file agent contract (BasicAgent, name, metadata, perform()), three-tier runtime (brainstem local, swarm cloud, copilot studio), sacred wire (/chat), data-sloshing context, and agent registry (RAR). Post-v1 addenda formalize brainstem-kernel boundary, egg cartridge family (organism/rapplication/session/neighborhood/estate), vBrainstem multi-participant sessions, and twin lifecycle management. Version frozen 2026-04-17; 18.13 addendum 2026-05-19.
contracts: Sacred Tenet: single-file *_agent.py IS the agent IS the documentation IS the contract (§0)

Wire is forever: /chat envelope unchanged forever via additive-only schema evolution, both response+assistant_response keys always emitted, DEFAULT_USER_GUID='c0p110t0-aaaa-bbbb-cccc-123456789abc' constant, no handshake/peer mode (§0.1)
Agent contract: BasicAgent subclass, self.name string, self.metadata OpenAI-style JSON schema dict, perform(**kwargs)->str method, no build step, no sibling imports (§5)

Three tiers, all runnable independently, same single-file agent on all three: Tier 1 local brainstem (Python 3.11+, port 7071, GitHub Copilot), Tier 2 cloud (Azure Functions), Tier 3 enterprise (Copilot Studio) (§4)

Brainstem sacred: brainstem.py is platform-layer sacred companion to agent-file tenet; only legitimate edits are bug fixes or new top-level output-slot delimiters like |||VOICE||| and |||TWIN|||; no inline features, no silent contract changes (§18.1)
Agent discovery: any file matching agents/*_agent.py or *Agent.ts is loaded; one file=one agent; no multi-agent files (§5.7)

RAR manifest is cost of admission to registry, not cost of being RAPP agent: plain RAPP agents need only BasicAgent+name+metadata+perform(), RAR-registered agents additionally carry __manifest__ with schema='rapp-agent/1.0', semver version, category, tags (§12.0–§12.2)

Egg cartridge family: single sneakernet primitive across ecosystem, identified by manifest.schema and manifest.type/scale; five kinds (organism, rapplication, session, neighborhood, estate) with schema versions brainstem-egg/2.2–2.3 and rapp-egg/2.0; introspection-based routing in egg_hatcher_agent.py, never guesses, refuses unknown kinds (§18.10–§18.10.5)

Tenancy model: one tenant = one soul.md + one agents/ directory + one storage; Tier 2 adds per-user_guid memory isolation without contradicting §10 single-brainstem constraint; multi-tenancy achieved by deploying multiple instances (§10, §18.4)

Twin lifecycle states: active (default ~/.rapp/twins/<hash>/), archived (reversible, ~/.rapp/twins/.archive/<hash>/), purged (irreversible tombstone, ~/.rapp/twins/.purged/<hash>.json); transitions active→archived→purged; scanner skips dotdir-prefixed children of ~/.rapp/twins/ (§18.13)

v1 is frozen: no breaking changes to §0 (Sacred Tenet), §5 (Agent Contract), §8 (HTTP Surface), §9 (Config), §14 (Versioning); future versions must remain backwards-compatible or require v2 major bump (§14, §18.1)

vBrainstem: browser-tab multi-participant session surface at pages/vbrainstem.html, QR+WebRTC pairing, host/guest roles, session as brainstem-egg/2.3-session cartridge, three LLM backends (localhost:7071, custom brainstem, GitHub Copilot Pyodide), Coordinator persona for autonomous workflow drive (§18.11)
canonical_phrases: The single-file *_agent.py is sacred.
The file IS the agent IS the documentation IS the contract.
/chat is the only wire. It is time-travel safe by construction.
A v0 brainstem unearthed from a backup, a probe, or a frozen Docker image still talks to the latest brainstem without a single code change on either side.
The agent is the API. The service is a view.
An agent that requires more than a single Python (or TypeScript) file is not a RAPP agent.
Nothing else is required to be a RAPP agent. No __manifest__, no @publisher/slug, no version, no category, no registry membership.
The manifest is the cost of admission to RAR. It is not the cost of being a RAPP agent.
brainstem.py is sacred at the platform layer.
The single-file agent stays the unit — whether that file started life as a single hand-authored agent or as the converged output of a workshop.
The egg cartridge is the single sneakernet primitive across the RAPP ecosystem.
The hatcher never guesses. Unknown cartridge kinds get a clear reply; never a silent or destructive fallback.
Rappid of a purged twin remains globally addressable; purging removes the local body, not the substrate identity.

neurons.summary: ESTATE_SPEC locks the rappid format as the global address for all doors (twins and neighborhoods) across the RAPP network, with pure string-parsing derivation to canonical URLs, mandatory estate.json schema storing only rappid+provenance, and disaster-recovery recomputation from public GitHub data. Covers single-operator estates, neighborhood-scale federations, substrate-portable identity cartridges, and public/private boundary enforcement.

pattern.gap: The ESTATE_SPEC does not name a private instance; it establishes the generic pattern: rappid-as-global-address, pure-fetch discovery, substrate-agnostic federation. The private RAPP instances (e.g., Bots in Blazers, AIBAST) are mentioned only as worked examples of neighborhood-scale cartridges and federation handoff in §7.5.3 and §6.5. The public/private boundary (Article XLVIII) treats private repos as opaque URLs served over the same canonical URL set; the spec enforces the PATTERN without naming private deployment details.
pattern.change: Schema ratification (2026-06-02 Amendment to XLVI.2) extended single-presence and gate kinds to reflect de-facto shipped instances (added memorial, pre-founder, mirror, experiment, custom, ant-farm, braintrust, workspace, hatched, rapplication, place). This formalized a drift in practice: kinds had shipped before being canonicalized. The fix: CONSTITUTION enforcement + one canonical parser (door_address.py) imported by all consumers. No private instance is involved in this pattern — it's a generic governance correction.

neurons.summary: Wire-level specification for the neighborhood-scale rapp-egg cartridge (rapp-egg/2.0 scale=neighborhood), defining the ZIP format, member topology, snapshot options, carrier protocols, hatch targets (in-place and local-simulate), and safety model for capturing and restoring federated brainstem neighborhoods across multiple substrates.
contracts: The egg is a ZIP file (ZIP_DEFLATED) with .egg extension; peer workspaces are gzipped tarballs; local workspaces are individual files
manifest.json is required and declares schema, scale, snapshot timestamp, members, and options (with partial snapshot flags for audit)
members.local and members.peers carry distinct metadata: local includes loaded_agents/model/files_captured; peers include reachability/health/ssh_ok status and captured_path_in_egg pointers

Carrier selection via peers.json coordinate fields: LAN-SSH (ssh_user+ssh_host), GitHub-neighborhood (github_neighborhood field), Tailscale (SSH-implicit), HTTPS-with-auth (planned), file:// import (planned)
GitHub-neighborhood carrier uses opt-in PR mode with github_write_enabled/dry_run flags; never writes main directly; clones shallow, applies tarball, branches, commits, pushes, opens PR
Hatch target=in-place restores to canonical paths and invokes carrier write half for each peer; target=local-simulate extracts peer tarballs to ~/.rapp/simulated/<peer>/twins/<hash>/ without SSH
Global state follows strict allowlist (rappid.json, estate.json, self_healing_cron_state.json, peers/ directory) and denylist (private-estate-secret, keys/, logs/, venv/, src/)

Hatch defaults to non-destructive gap-filling; per-category overwrite flags (overwrite_agents, overwrite_core, overwrite_data, overwrite_global_state, overwrite_twins, overwrite_peer_twins) gate destructive writes
Hatchers MUST support plan action (dry-run) and report per-category created/overwritten/skipped counts and per-peer breakdowns
Peer twin entry carries captured:bool; if false, reason field ('oversize', 'tar_failed', etc); tarball path format is peers/<peer>/twins/<hash>.tar.gz
canonical_phrases: A neighborhood is a group of organisms that recognize each other; members can live on any substrate (LAN, GitHub, Tailscale, HTTPS-with-auth)
The egg's format does not care about carrier; only the carrier (method to move workspace bytes) varies by substrate
The format and dispatch rules are uniform regardless of carrier
If you are writing a snapshot agent, a hatcher, an inspector, a federation backup tool, or any code that reads or writes a scale: neighborhood egg — this is the contract
Hatch MUST default to non-destructive (gap-filling) behavior
Eggs are not signed (yet); treat them as you would any .tar.gz
Eggs are NOT for sharing publicly; they contain twin memory contents and brainstem-loaded agent source — operator-private state
The snapshot uses a strict allowlist (files + recursive directories) and denylist (never travels, regardless)

canonical_phrases: State is determined by disk location. There is no separate registry. The filesystem is the source of truth.
There is no direct `active → purged` transition. `purge` only operates on an already-archived twin.
The `.purged/<hash>.json` ledger entry IS that tombstone at the local brainstem layer.

contracts: Twin lifecycle has exactly three states: active, archived, purged — determined by filesystem location, not registry.
Purge is irreversible and requires prior archival; single-twin only; requires explicit confirm=true.
Unarchive restores body but does not auto-boot; preserves archived.json inside workspace for history.
Archive can operate in bulk (filter-based, dry-run default) or single; stops running twins before moving workspace.
Fleet.archive_estate fans out via SSH to all peers; per-peer results aggregated; no atomic rollback; archive is reversible so partial state recoverable.
Snapshots and hatches support lifecycle-aware restoration: include_archived/restore_archived flags follow opt-in additive pattern.

contracts: Twin-chat (§6) is the base layer; all social apps (commons, rappterbook, forum) are channels + message kinds on top
Brainstem is a pure controller that hatches isolated twins (own process, workspace, identity, memory) and drives them by twin-chat; it never joins or posts
Every envelope may be sealed with AES-256-GCM (rapp-sealed/1.0); key derived from PBKDF2-SHA256 (210,000 iterations, salt 'rapp-neighborhood-5a/1') from out-of-band pairing secret
The §6 envelope is byte-identical on local ≡ kited ≡ cloud; relay is interchangeable
A kited twin shows the kite mark (§2) when actively hosting and is swarm-capable ('v' prefix); on-device (no 'v') is the single-machine form
Console operations (kind:'console') only happen over the sealed channel (§8); plaintext 'say' may be open
A dark-door neighborhood (§19) has no public front door; membership/existence are private; 404 is the guard
Collaborator-gated sealed key (§19): the live room runs §8 sealed channel end-to-end; the key is derived from a secret only collaborators can read; speaking sealed IS the collaborator proof
A kited host relay (lights) is a well-known peer id claimed by the first vTwin; on host drop, next-in-line guest claims it; when last vTwin leaves, id frees and lights go dark
Each cubby member gets an isolated directory (rapp-cubby/1.0) housing a slice of their full rapp estate; members write only in their own cubby, reading is open to all collaborators
Stream, don't commit: members can stream agents into brainstem and register in host repo's .git/info/exclude (rapp-*-loadout/1.0); they run but are invisible to git
A cloud neighborhood (§14) is permanent (Azure Function), speaks the same §6 envelope and §8 seal, serves sealed twin-chat over HTTPS (channel 5a-cloud)

The spec owns the vocabulary (vTwin, Kited, Sealed, Doorman, etc.), the kite mark visual identity, and the §19 dark-door pattern for private collaborator-gated cubbies where members work in-repo with sealed end-to-end relay and replicated append-only boards.

neurons.summary: CONSTITUTION.md is the authoring discipline that keeps the RAPP wire contract (SPEC.md) shippable for a decade. It articulates 51 articles (Articles 0–LI) governing kernel architecture, agent portability, delimited slots, identity via rappid, variant species lineage, ecosystem federation, and the operator-centric discipline required for decentralized substrate management. The file establishes that capabilities live in single-file agents, the brainstem stays structurally light, rappid forms the permanent genealogy tree of all brainstems ever born, material changes require proposal traceability, and the substrate must be safe for sensitive work through mandatory two-tier privacy separation.

The neighborhood pattern is COVERED at the specification level. neurons.summary: ECOSYSTEM.md is the authoritative architecture-level companion to HERO_USECASE.md, defining the complete end-to-end layout of a RAPP organism: identity stack (rappid-driven), lifecycle (plant → hatch → live → mutate → reassimilate), file layout, two surfaces (front door + doorman), three-tier memory system, MMR calculation, PR-driven evolution, egg cartridges (five kinds), integrity verification, Dream Catcher reassimilation, three network modes, external integrations, component inventory, and cross-references to federation patterns including dark-door neighborhoods and super-RAR.
contracts: A RAPP organism IS a public GitHub repository with specific file layout; rappid (UUIDv4 or v2 format) is the immutable identity
Kernel (brainstem.py + VERSION + basic_agent.py) is frozen at v0.6.0; capabilities grow exclusively via agent.py PRs
Identity derives entirely from rappid via computed refraction: sigil, card, stream_id, deep-verify anchor, parent_rappid chain

MMR is single global rating computed client-side from public signals; formula includes base (memory + mutations + agents + age + forks) + activity decay (1.0 active → 0.45 stasis) + lineage gift (30% parent surplus outside decay)
Every egg seals with multi-layer integrity: per-file SHA-256 + manifest hash + origin commit SHA + state-at-seal snapshot; verification is client-side
Memory is three concentric tiers: device-local (localStorage), public (.brainstem_data/memory.json), per-user private (GitHub Issues with private-memory label)
Dream Catcher folds parallel-dimension frames into canonical trunk via UTC-first conflict resolution; non-contradicting frames layer on
Network operates in three modes: Online (live APIs), Airplane (24h localStorage cache with stale flag), Hatched offline (egg state_at_seal as source of truth)
Egg cartridges are five-kind family under brainstem-egg/2.x; hatcher introspects manifest.scale/kind and refuses unknown kinds

canonical_phrases: The repo IS the organism — its identity (rappid), its voice (soul.md), its memory (.brainstem_data/), its body (agents/), and its skin (index.html, doorman/) all live as committed files. There is no server.

The lifecycle is non-linear: an organism can be hatched many times in parallel (each device = one parallel dimension), each accumulates its own mutations, and the Dream Catcher reassimilates them via PR.

Re-hatching is the cross-substrate migration step. Egg re-hatching is the substrate lifecycle: an .egg cartridge is the organism in suspended animation, and re-anchoring it on a new brainstem revives the organism in place without disturbing its rappid, its cross-signed device chain, or its frame log.
The kernel (brainstem.py + VERSION + basic_agent.py) is frozen at the v0.6.0 grail. It is never edited. Capabilities grow exclusively through agent.py files merged into /agents/.
The rappid IS the organism, every visual is a refraction of it.
Frames are the unit of mutation. Every meaningful event becomes a frame.
Eggs are zip archives matching the brainstem-egg family schema. Two tiers (doorman exports doorman; doorman exports ascended).
Integrity is a multi-layer envelope: per-file SHA-256, manifest hash, origin commit SHA, state-at-seal snapshot.
The 🔬 Verify pane recomputes everything client-side. Three verdicts: ✓ envelope intact, ⚠ partial, ✗ tampered.
Deep-verify against live repo re-fetches every file from raw.githubusercontent.com and recomputes hashes. If matches: provably authentic, since only the seed's owner can push.

Conflict-resolution doctrine: UTC-first canon — whichever frame hit the UTC first is canonical. Non-contradicting later frames layer on. Contradicting frames are preserved as alternate-dimension data.

Trust anchor matrix: Online = raw.githubusercontent.com (only operator can push), Airplane = localStorage stamped with last-sync date, Hatched offline = egg manifest + provenance, Phase 2 = ed25519 publisher signatures.
Chat Is The Only Wire: an MCP client over stdio is just another Layer-2 caller of /chat, sending the same envelope and getting the same envelope back — it adds no new abstraction.
What the visitor never has to do: open a terminal, read documentation, know what a brainstem is, know what a rappid is, understand the egg format.

neurons.summary: Constitutional specification (Article XLVII.5 + subsections) for substrate-agnostic federation across four connectivity tiers (GitHub raw, LAN HTTP+Bonjour, AirDrop eggs, sneakernet file://) that form a resilience ladder where no single substrate is load-bearing. One protocol shape, four transports; the federation walks whatever lights up.
contracts: Four substrates form a decentralized resilience ladder; no centralized substrate is load-bearing.
Same rapp-network-beacon/1.1 JSON shape and same door_from_rappid() parser (Article XLVI.5) operate uniformly across all substrates.
discovery.indexable consent flag is honored equally on every substrate; sniffers MUST respect opt-out on github-raw, lan-http, file://, and other transports.
The egg cartridge (organism/rapplication/session/neighborhood/estate family) is substrate-agnostic transport unit; same contents move via GitHub raw, LAN-SSH, AirDrop, or file://.
tools/sniff_network.py::_resolve_node() is the single normalizer; substrate label (github-raw, lan-http, file, http, https) surfaces in each record so consumers distinguish snapshot vs. live data.
--include-private flag in sniff_network.py is constitutionally reserved for operator self-audit only, never for crawling others.
Each operator record carries compliance field (xlviii/partial/legacy) indicating Article XLVIII adherence level.
LAN-SSH carrier (§3.1) for neighborhood-egg snapshot/hatch requires SSH with BatchMode=yes, StrictHostKeyChecking=accept-new, key-only auth; no agent on peer required.
Egg imports via import_peer_egg.py synthesize beacon at file:// if not carried; add {github, beacon_url: file://..., estate_url: file://...} entry to local network-seed.json.
WebRTC tether (Substrate #5) carries live multi-participant sessions; portable as brainstem-egg/2.3-session; drops onto Substrate #3 (AirDrop) or #4 (file://) for offline replay.

No centralized substrate is load-bearing.
The federation walks whatever lights up.
Publishing IS the signal.
The egg IS a federation packet.

The network is a graph, not a tree.

The platform's local-first promise survives every centralized-substrate failure mode.

declares: Article XLVI (Estate Spec, rappid is URL)
contracts: Every Article XLVIII compliant operator has THREE tiers from first install: public estate (discovery, zero PII), private estate (bones—workflow + sanitized structure, collaborator-gated, no PII by default), local on-device (substance—PII/customer data/real content).

URL opacity is load-bearing: private repo paths carry zero semantic information; only two well-known paths (meta.json, README.md) are exempt; all other content lives at opaque paths: objects/<sha256>.json (content-addressed) or kinds/<HMAC(secret,kind)>/<HMAC(secret,id)>.json.

PII is canonical to operator's local device (~/.brainstem/workbenches/<slug>/ or per-neighborhood ~/.brainstem/neighborhoods/<slug>/<handle>/customers/<slug>/); lives in private repo only via explicit operator override (§1.6: per-file, per-engagement, repo-wide, or future encrypted-at-rest).

Cloning private repo gives fully runnable digital organism (agents, rapplications, holocards, specs, rar, soul, senses, organs, identity, constitution, governance) minus operator-specific PII; each collaborator's substance stays on their device only.

Workbench is canonical local-tier primitive (§1.7): one twin's working area for one ongoing thing; stored at ~/.brainstem/workbenches/<workbench-slug>/ with meta.json (owner_rappid, twin_rappids[], type, created, namespace?, peers_visible); supports sibling-peek (default permissive, local-only, cross-twin collaboration via filesystem read).

Publish flow is constitutionally atomic: auto-create private estate (step 0), verify existence, compute commitment hash (sha256 of normalized JSON tree), audit URL opacity (refuse publish if paths violate regex), write public estate + beacon, set rapp-estate topic on public repo.

Conformance for active contributors: MUST keep customer/project PII in local workbenches, MUST NOT git add customer-specific PII unless exercising explicit override §1.6, MUST honor .gitignore exclusion of .brainstem//.bwat-data/, SHOULD periodically audit git diff before pushing; enforcement is per-contributor honor + structural defaults, not platform policing.

The private repo CANNOT contain: internal paths beyond meta.json/README.md, recipient handles/contact names/topic strings, fields surfacing operator's private collaborators, fields characterizing what's inside; these are constitutionally enforced per Article XLVIII.5 + XLVIII.6.
canonical_phrases: The boundary is not optional. The URLs are not metadata. The private repo is not for advanced users. The operator's secret is not for the network. From day one — every operator, every estate, every URL — the substrate for real work is in place.
Cloning the private repo gives you a fully runnable digital organism. It has every anatomical layer the organism needs to function — just no operator-specific PII.
The version that actually works has three tiers. Each addresses a distinct threat model.
The private repo IS the neighborhood-organism's distributable shape.

URL opacity load-bearing constraint; every path outside meta.json/README.md is opaque (content-addressed or HMAC'd). Cloning private repo yields fully runnable organism minus substance. Workbench is canonical local primitive (sibling-peek collaborative). Publish flow atomic, auto-creates estates, audits opacity. Conformance per-contributor honor + structural defaults (gitignore, sanitized projects.json, explicit overrides §1.6).

contracts: Every planted neighborhood front gate MUST display a QR code on first paint; non-conforming gates flagged by federation roll-up lint pass

Every tether claiming to dial neighborhoods MUST handle scanned QR payloads: egg URL (fetch JSON read neighborhood_json), neighborhood.json URL (load directly), or bare URL with query params (?neighborhood= or ?egg=)
Snippet auto-detects deploy URL via location.origin + location.pathname; same code works in any neighborhood without hardcoding
Graceful degradation: if QRious unavailable (offline/no CDN), canvas replaced with plaintext URL for copy/paste into tether's Paste mode
QR size 256px minimum recommended for reliable phone-camera reads; error level M (15% redundancy) for ~60-80 ASCII char URLs
canonical_phrases: Every neighborhood's planted front gate (index.html at the repo root, served via GitHub Pages or any other substrate) MUST display a tether QR code on first paint. The QR is the neighborhood's phone number — any tether scans it to dial in.

All three resolve to the same rappid. The QR is for casual visitors with cameras; the holocard is for recognition and provenance; the incantation is the hero use case — memorize 7 words, summon anywhere.
The egg is a brainstem-egg/2.3-neighborhood JSON invite carrying rappid + canonical URLs (neighborhood_json, tether_url, homepage).
The tether is universal (any deployment, any repo). The neighborhood is the destination. The QR is the visual phone number connecting them.
The snippet auto-detects which deploy it's running in, so the same HTML file works in any neighborhood that drops it into their index.html. No hardcoding required.

neurons.summary: The Rapplication SDK specifies the agent-first architecture for installable RAPP units—single or two-file compositions (agent.py + optional service.py) where the agent is always the complete interface via perform() and the service is a purely optional HTTP view layer. The spec mandates zero-config portability, shared storage semantics, and enforces the sneakernet-portable invariant: exactly two files (agent.py + .egg) with only drag-and-drop and single chat command required for setup.
contracts: Agent file (*_agent.py) extends BasicAgent with metadata dict (OpenAI schema) and perform(**kwargs) returning JSON string—is the complete interface; service is always optional

Service file (*_service.py) is URL-namespace view layer only, contains module-level name string and handle(method, path, body) returning (dict, status_code) tuple, invisible to LLM, reads/writes same .brainstem_data/{name}.json
Agent MUST work fully through perform() alone; removing service must not break agent
Single-file constraint: one agent = one .py file, one service = one .py file, no multi-file rapplications
Zero-config rule: no .env, registration, build steps, restart, pip install, shell commands, config edits, environment variables, or additional tools required
Agent docstring IS the readme (sacred)—no separate README.md, INSTRUCTIONS.md, or SETUP.md files permitted in sneakernet payload

Sneakernet-portable invariant: EXACTLY two files (agent.py + .egg), receiver uses only drag-and-drop + one chat command; bootstrap agent handles all setup: unpacking, sha256 verification, agent installation, rappid minting, workspace provisioning, subscription recording
Storage pattern: agent (agents/) and service (services/) both access same .brainstem_data/{name}.json—agent is source of truth
Portability across tiers: same agent runs in Tier 1 (local brainstem), Tier 2 (Azure Functions), Tier 3 (Copilot Studio) without modification
JSON-only I/O: perform() returns JSON string, handle() returns (dict, int) tuple, no exceptions
Best practice—rapplication UI should call perform() directly with static predetermined inputs bypassing LLM for deterministic zero-cost hydration
Discovery: both agents and services auto-discovered on every request with no registration required
canonical_phrases: The agent is the API. The service is a view.
Every rapplication MUST work fully through perform() alone.
The sneakernet portability invariant: A portable artifact shared between operators consists of EXACTLY two files: one agent.py + one .egg.
Anything else is not portable.
The bootstrap agent is responsible for everything past the drag-and-drop.
No separate README.md, README.txt, INSTRUCTIONS.md, SETUP.md, or any other accompanying documentation file in a sneakernet payload.
Agent-first: works through any LLM with no UI required.
The same *_agent.py files that the brainstem hot-loads for /chat should be the ONLY data source the rapplication's UI uses to hydrate.

When you're about to add a doc here, ask: Is this a contract someone will check, or a story someone will read? Will this file be referenced by name by code, tests, or other docs? Is the audience a human contributor, or end-user prospects?

contracts: The kernel (Flask process) is sacred and frozen — no edits after initial deployment; brainstem.py, basic_agent.py, and VERSION are immutable.
Agents are the only unit of extension; no synonyms (skills, plugins, routines, loops, cassettes, capabilities) permitted — one file, one class, one perform() method.
The repo IS the organism — its identity (rappid), voice (soul.md), body (agents/), memory (.brainstem_data/), and skin (index.html + doorman/) are all committed files.

.egg cartridges are universal, self-describing, SHA-256-sealed, and portable across kernels; five kinds (organism, rapplication, session, neighborhood, estate) dispatched by manifest scale field via generic egg_hatcher_agent.py.
The substrate is GitHub alone — no central server, registry, marketplace, or PKI beyond gh auth + PeerJS-handshake + Cloudflare auth-proxy.
Private/public split enforced: gate is anonymous-friendly (identity, trade card, lineage); companion is auth-gated (roster, agents, decisions) anchored to GitHub collaborator status.

Four modes of AI travel are load-bearing: Cold (full cartridge), Warm (remote agents + home state), Soul (parallel hatched dimensions + Dream Catcher reconciliation), Message (WebRTC live or GitHub Issue async).
User is in the loop async, not synchronous — agents work across zones on behalf of operator; results land in estate inbox; network self-heals when members are offline.
Any proposal breaking Part 1 §1-5 or Part Deux §3-5 is rejected; feature flags and backwards-compat shims prohibited — schema bumps required for clean shipping.
Master Plan is append-only — extensions added, existing items never repurposed; breaking changes ship as Part Trois.

egg format reverts from rapp-egg/2.0 or loses scale field dispatch — breaks §1.4 cartridge contract

Any existing item in the ten first principles is repurposed rather than extended
neurons.summary: RAPP Master Plan is the constitutional north-star for a decentralized AI platform where operators run local brainstems, share organisms as encrypted eggs, and mesh through shared neighborhoods using GitHub as the sole substrate. The plan locks ten first principles: kernel sacred, agents-only extension, repo-as-organism, eggs as travel cartridges, GitHub as infrastructure, async user loops, and local-first resilience. Breaking changes require Part Trois; the plan is append-only.

neurons.summary: ECOSYSTEM_MAP.md is the single canonical synthesis of the RAPP ecosystem—the append-only decision table and implementation inventory that unifies 40+ concept docs, 9 schema families, and 100+ code sites. It formalizes authority order (MASTER_PLAN > CONSTITUTION > specs), defines the five fractal primitives (rappid, door, card, tether, trust scope), catalogs every schema with its emitter and definer, maps implementation to spec, surfaces the "before you do X" workflow table with 39 decision points, locks 5 antipatterns as law, documents 11 drift gaps (mostly resolved), lists live planted state, and condenses lexicon + trademark scope. Authority: it is derivative—if it disagrees with MASTER_PLAN/CONSTITUTION/spec docs, those win and the map is wrong; fix the map. Maintained at repo root as peer of MASTER_PLAN.md, append-only.
contracts: Authority order is absolute: MASTER_PLAN.md wins on strategy; CONSTITUTION.md governs repo structure (38+ articles); spec docs execute the plan; pages/vault/ essays provide rationale; code comments last because code rots.

Five universal primitives are recursively fractaled at every scale (agent → twin → neighborhood → estate → metropolis): rappid (UUIDv4, never regenerated), door (public URL surface), card (trade-card introduction), tether (4 channels: WebRTC, Issues, PRs, raw fetch), trust scope (Personal/Neighborhood/Public).

The five antipatterns are locked law and append-only: (1) call agents ONLY agents (never skill/plugin/routine/loop); (2) frozen kernel at brainstem.py + basic_agent.py + doorman tier; (3) no backwards-compat shims for half-released schemas—bump version and migrate cleanly; (4) soul.md Identity block is non-negotiable, forbids RAPP/AI-assistant fallback; (5) network calls must have local-first cache fallback (HERO_USECASE offline).

Every rappid is <owner>/<repo>-format derivable via tools/door_address.py::door_from_rappid(). Per-consumer rappid parsers are forbidden (CONSTITUTION Art. XLVI.5). Nine canonical files live at GitHub raw.githubusercontent.com/<owner>/<repo>/main/{rappid.json,card.json,holo.*,members.json,facets.json} — no auth, no rate limit.

Estate is a cache; the network is the source of truth. Local ~/.brainstem/estate.json stores ONLY {rappid, added_at, via}. Article XLVI formalizes rappid-as-global-address; XLVII governs decentralized discovery via .well-known/ beacons and BFS federation hints; XLVIII mandates two-tier (public + opaque-pathed private) for PII compliance.

§13 drift is append-only; entries added when found, removed only when spec and code reconciled (PR closes row). Severity: P0 wire-incompatible, P1 schema/field mismatch, P2 doc/naming, P3 undocumented schema in code. As of 2026-05-08, 11 drift gaps documented; all but one fully resolved.

Ecosystem_map/1.0 is append-only and derivative. If it disagrees with MASTER_PLAN/CONSTITUTION/spec docs, the spec wins and the map is wrong—fix the map. §13 drift is the only mutable section. Add a row to §11 the first time you see drift on a trigger.

Three-tier surface: Tier 1 (Flask :7071, local), Tier 2 (Azure Functions /api/*, cloud), Tier 3 (Microsoft Copilot Studio). Same /chat envelope across tiers; identical agent contract rapp-agent/1.0; differ only in storage backend + auth + surface.

§11 decision table (39 rows) routes workflows: orient / add agent / add organ / tether call / schema use / plant / hot-load / graft / dock / bond pulse / discover door / discover estate / rebuild estate / verify private / audit compliance / forbid rappid parser / federate / touch kernel / touch install URL / add network call / write doc / add endpoint / bump schema / forbid terminology / brand fallback / probe private / defer to phase 2 / user-facing copy. Each row pre-checks (≤30s) → deep-read spec.

The lexicon is locked: humans call it **estate** (not swarm), **soul**/**soul-key**/**rappid**, **agent** (never skill/plugin/routine/loop), **brainstem** (no rename), **rapplication** (brand), **mitosis** (birth), **dreamcatcher** (merge), **species tree** (lineage).

neurons.summary: The RAPP network protocol specification defining federation, rappid identity (globally-resolvable door addresses), the 9-URL canonical door set, estate management (public+private two-tier), holocard derivation, soul persistence, agent contracts, and hardcoded antipatterns. Schema-of-record is rapp-protocol/1.0; constitutional anchor is Articles I–XLVI.
contracts: Every door MUST publish 9 canonical URLs derivable by string parsing from rappid alone via door_from_rappid().
Rappid format NEVER re-versions; consolidates three prior forms; every legacy form read forever and canonicalized.
Estate entry stores ONLY {rappid, added_at, via}; derived fields forbidden; recomputable from network via parent_rappid chain.
Two-tier estate (public+private) mandatory from day one; beacon carries private_estate_pointer, private_estate_commitment, private_door_count.
rapp-network-beacon/1.1 canonical TXT-record schema: rappid, github, beacon_path, estate_path, schema, spec_version, indexable flag.
Slots (|||VOICE|||, |||TWIN|||) are fixed forever; never repurposed or overloaded (Article II/XXV); new sub-capabilities use TAGS inside.
No fallbacks—spec says what's true; missing canonical files trigger non-compliance, planter emits missing files.
Nine door URL set (§3): repo, front, identity (rappid.json), holocard (card.json), holo_md, avatar, summon_qr, members (gates only), facets.
canonical_phrases: One file. Read this and you can participate in the global RAPP network. The only requirement is a GitHub account.
RAPP is a global mesh of AI doors hosted on free GitHub infrastructure.
The rappid encodes the address. The address encodes the door. The door encodes the contract. There is no other map.
The string is NEVER re-versioned.
Every consumer MUST use this one function (or a faithful port). Per-consumer reimplementation is forbidden.
Strictness is one-time; laxity is permanent.
The operator's HMAC secret lives at ~/.brainstem/private-estate-secret (file mode 0600). NEVER appears in any committed file.
No centralized substrate is load-bearing for federation—peers find you on whatever substrate you publish to.

contracts: This file overrides specs/WORKSPACE_PROTOCOL.md on access + joining for the private-cubby-neighborhood neighborhood
A cubby is one member's isolated housing for their entire rapp estate — the same environment their local computer provides
You write only inside your own cubby, plus append-only zones: events/, members.json entry, cubbies/index.json
Cross-cubby changes ride pull requests that the cubby owner merges
.github/CODEOWNERS maps each cubby to its owner; cubby-guard workflow flags PRs/pushes touching someone else's cubby
The private-cubby-neighborhood has NO public front door — private repo where every URL to outsiders returns 404
Collaborator access is required; joining is out-of-band via the operator
Bones stay in the repo (shared code, souls, manifests, posts); substance stays on device (~/.brainstem/, .env, tokens, memory stores)
.gitignore enforces default; private-cubby-neighborhood stash refuses secret-shaped files outright
Streaming: private-cubby-neighborhood stash→load→unload cycle; streamed files registered in .git/info/exclude, invisible to git
Personal branches named cubby/<handle>/<topic> are member-owned; main stays the shared truth
Events in events/ are append-only signed (kinds: hello, show-and-tell, ask, reply, fyi, leave); merge rule is (from, ts) universal key
QR payphone mechanism: operator shares QR of rappid; scanner supplies own GitHub token; collaborator access = dial tone; 404 = guard

neurons.summary: The CUBBY_PROTOCOL specifies the private-cubby-neighborhood's private neighborhood architecture: each member gets an isolated cubby directory (their entire rapp estate), writes only to their own cubby plus append-only zones, and collaborators dial in via QR/payphone (no public door). It overrides the generic WORKSPACE_PROTOCOL, enforcing collaborator-gated access, artifact streaming (load/unload without git), and event-driven show-and-tell through signed append-only events.
canonical_phrases: A cubby is one member's isolated housing for their entire rapp estate
This file overrides specs/WORKSPACE_PROTOCOL.md on access + joining
You write only inside your own cubby (plus the append-only zones: events/, your entry in members.json + cubbies/index.json)
The private-cubby-neighborhood has no public front door (it's the private-cubby-neighborhood)
Cross-cubby changes ride pull requests the cubby owner merges
.github/CODEOWNERS maps each cubby to its owner; the cubby-guard workflow flags PRs and pushes that touch someone else's cubby
The repo holds what members SHARE (code, souls, manifests, posts). Each member's PII-bearing substance stays on their device
private-cubby-neighborhood stash path=<file> — put an agent (or egg, or note) in your cubby
Streamed files are registered in the host repo's .git/info/exclude, so they run like any agent but are invisible to git
Collaborator access = dial tone; everyone else hears 404
The rappid is the deterministic sha256 of the owner/slug, the same way a phone number isn't a secret; the protection is GitHub's 404, not name-obscurity
Merge rule: (from, ts) is the universal key — clones can diverge offline indefinitely and union losslessly

Pattern §19 neighborhood protocol coverage is complete for the private-cubby-neighborhood instance; no generic pattern violation detected.

contracts: A kited room (ephemeral E2E-encrypted WebRTC mesh) exists only when someone is in it; repo is cold store (bones), room is warm presence.
First vTwin to arrive claims a well-known PeerJS id and becomes host; later vTwins join as guests; on host drop, next-in-line guest auto-promotes; last exit darkens the room.
WebRTC data channels carry DTLS encryption peer-to-peer; PeerJS broker carries signaling only, never content.
Every message rides §8 sealed channel (AES-256-GCM, PBKDF2-SHA256 210,000 iters, salt rapp-neighborhood-5a/1); broker/relay/host-squatter see only {schema, iv, ct}.

Collaborator-gated channel secret (channel-secret.json, GitHub collaborator access only) is fetched via authenticated GitHub API (TLS), never URL/QR, stashed in sessionStorage; speaking sealed channel IS collaborator proof.
6-digit safety code from both peers' session pubkeys verified out-of-band defeats signaling MITM; rotation on collaborator-leave regens channel-secret.json for forward-secrecy.
canonical_phrases: The private-cubby-neighborhood has no public front door. Its live surface is a kited room: an ephemeral, end-to-end-encrypted WebRTC mesh that exists only while someone is in it.
The first kited vTwin to arrive claims a well-known PeerJS id derived from the door (every collaborator computes the same id). It becomes the host.
When the last vTwin exits, nobody holds the id → the room goes dark.
WebRTC data channels are DTLS-encrypted peer-to-peer; the PeerJS broker carries signaling only, never content.

Speaking the sealed channel IS the collaborator proof. A non-collaborator who joins the mesh can neither open the host's sealed hello nor produce a valid sealed reply, and is silently dropped at admission.
neurons.summary: VNEIGHBORHOOD_PROTOCOL defines a private, ephemeral kited room (E2E-encrypted WebRTC mesh) for collaborator-only access to the private-cubby-neighborhood. The first joiner auto-promotes to host (lighting the room); guests auto-connect to that host; when the host drops, next-in-line auto-promotes; last exit goes dark. All content rides the §8 sealed channel (AES-256-GCM) keyed by collaborator-gated channel-secret.json, defeating broker/relay eavesdropping.

contracts: Three tiers (Brainstem, Swarm, Copilot Studio) compose single-file Python agents from laptop to enterprise.
Tier 1 (Brainstem) is local-first, GitHub Copilot-powered, no API keys, runs on port 7071.
Tier 2 (Swarm) hosts agents on Azure Functions with identical /chat HTTP surface as Tier 1.
Tier 3 (Copilot Studio) wraps agents in Microsoft Power Platform solution for Teams/M365 Copilot.
Installation flows are symmetric across OS (macOS/Linux/Windows) via curl/iwr installers.
The headline demo surfaces Neighborhood.ask protocol — two agents collaborate peer-to-peer via QR/card share (no GitHub handle required).
Same agent file persists through all three tiers without modification.

neurons.summary: Landing page for RAPP (Rapid Agent Prototyping Platform) — a three-tier stack deploying single-file Python agents from local Brainstem (GitHub Copilot, port 7071) to Azure Swarm to Microsoft Copilot Studio. Emphasizes portability, no API keys, and P2P collaboration via Neighborhood.ask protocol with QR/card-based sharing.
canonical_phrases: Local-first AI agents that grow into the Microsoft enterprise stack one tier at a time. Brainstem → Swarm → Copilot Studio. No API keys, no cloud setup — just your GitHub account.
The minimum viable brain. Runs on your laptop. Only dependency: a GitHub account with Copilot access.

The same single-file agents your laptop runs, hosted on Azure. One ARM template, one Deploy-to-Azure button. The HTTP surface is identical to Tier 1, so anything that talks to /chat on your laptop can talk to /chat in the cloud.
Import the unmanaged solution zip into Copilot Studio. Wire the Function App URL from Tier 2. Publish to Teams. The same agent file you wrote on day 1 is now being called from M365 Copilot.

Plant your front door. Adopt the canonical test neighbor in one tap. Ask your doorman a question that needs the neighbor's input. Watch two organisms collaborate end-to-end — same protocol whether your peer lives on your laptop, your phone, or someone's GitHub Pages a continent away.

contracts: The frozen v1 SPEC is the contractual twin of the single-file agent — agents IS documentation IS contract, immutable for v1.x.y

The SPEC wins over doctrine; doctrine wins over index; index wins over engine when conflict — the contract is the platform

Estate stores only rappid + provenance (added_at, via) — all other fields (owner, repo, kind, door_type, URLs) DERIVED at read time, never stored
Three-tier estate mandatory from first install — public discovery repo + private bones repo (collaborator-gated, no PII by default) + local on-device substance (canonical home for PII)
Brainstem.py is sacred at platform layer (companion to sacred agent file) — bug fixes and output-slot delimiters only, never infrastructure rewrites
The wire is forever — /chat envelope unchanged forever; old brainstems and new brainstems are peers, no negotiation or fallback protocol
Rapplication organism shape invariant (CONSTITUTION Article XXXVIII) — agents/<name>_agent.py + optional utils/organs/<name>_organ.py + optional .brainstem_data/ + rappid.json + manifest.json

neurons.summary: Interactive onboarding guide that walks users through RAPP's core promise—planting AI organisms as public GitHub repos with persistent identity, voice, and memory—and demonstrates the three trust tiers (public swarm, private neighborhood, personal) and handshake protocols for cross-organism collaboration without central infrastructure.
contracts: The kernel (brainstem.py) is locked at v0.6.0 and never changes; growth happens only through new *_agent.py files submitted as GitHub PRs.
Every organism is a public GitHub repo with rappid.json (persistent identity), soul.md (voice), memory.json (persistent memory), and agents/* (capabilities).
Three trust tiers govern knowledge sharing: public swarm (committed to seed repo), private neighborhood (GitHub collaborator-gated), personal (browser localStorage, per-device/per-user).
The front door URL (<your-handle>.github.io/<slug>/) is the global discovery primitive—the planted seed itself makes the collaboration network global without central infrastructure.

Twin-to-twin handshakes happen via three patterns: (1) public-layer fetch via Neighborhood.ask, (2) WebRTC tether for live peer-to-peer, (3) async GitHub Issue handshake for durable cross-timezone collaboration.

No hidden servers, no logs, no analytics, no telemetry—every byte committed to public repo; chat authentication uses personal GitHub Copilot subscription via device-code flow; all auth code open source and runnable locally.
Constitution Article XL: Secure-First Plant—organisms default to private-companion repo for accumulated memories; promotion to public requires explicit operator curation.
Constitution Article XLI: Operator's experience is conversation, end-to-end; the AI handles technical work (token management, repo forking, terminal commands), operator never leaves chat.
Constitution Article XLII: GitHub handle is implementation detail, not share primitive—the trade card (with sigil + QR code) is the shareable surface, not @handle.
canonical_phrases: Plant a digital organism that's actually yours.
The repo IS the organism. There's no central server, no separate database, no hidden infrastructure.
The 'platform' is the repo layout + a frozen kernel. There's nothing hidden because there's nowhere to hide it.
Public-repo as source-of-truth—anyone can git clone any planted organism and read every line of its code, identity, soul, and memory.
Frozen kernel—the runtime (brainstem.py) is locked at v0.6.0 and never changes.
The front door is the global discovery primitive.
Twin-to-twin handshakes where two organisms' AIs trade context through a secure channel, and neither AI surfaces the cross-twin handoff to its operator.
The card is the URL made shareable in physical space. Same identity, both expressions byte-equivalent.
We never ask you to copy a token. We never ask you to open a terminal. We never ask you to fork a repo. The AI does the dirty work; you stay in chat.

neurons.summary: sphere.html is the 3D interactive front-door index served from any planted RAPP seed, implementing the doorman pattern via Pyodide-hosted agents (ManageMemory, ContextMemory, LearnNew, SwarmFactory), device-code GitHub Copilot auth, and three-tier persona (ascended/public/anonymous). Visitors tap the 3D orb to summon sign-in; once authenticated, the sphere becomes an active chat interface that loads identity from rappid.json + soul.md + memory.json, escalates to private soul + facts if the visitor proves GitHub access to a private_companion repo, and dispatches tool-calls through a Pyodide runtime that mirrors the canonical local brainstem.
canonical_phrases: Implicit-summon sign-in modal. Opens automatically when the sphere is tapped while inactive. Same device-code flow as the canonical doorman; once authorized the sphere goes active and chat opens immediately. No API key paste.

Identity is loaded from the planted seed this page is being served from (./rappid.json + ./soul.md + ./.brainstem_data/memory.json), so dropping this index.html into any planted seed makes the sphere speak as that seed's persona automatically.
Ascended escalation: private_companion (if configured) OR operator fallback (push access to the seed itself). Same silent-escalation pattern as plant.sh's loadPrivateContext.
soul.md FIRST — promotes doorman to ascended voice.

System prompt builder — mirrors the doorman's persona logic. Ascended visitors (privateSoul present) get the full twin's voice; public visitors get the seed's public soul.md; anonymous visitors hear the default RAPP front door.
Same agent.py files as a local brainstem (kody-w/RAPP raw URLs). Same agent contract: a class extending BasicAgent with .metadata and .perform(**kwargs).

Chat dispatch — full tool-call loop, identical pattern to the canonical doorman in installer/plant.sh and utils/web/index.html. LLM emits tool_calls → we run the matching agent in Pyodide → feed the result back as a 'tool' message → loop until LLM produces final text. Capped at 4 rounds (mirrors brainstem.py).
When your reply is more than one short sentence, append |||VOICE||| <one short spoken line> at the end. Keep that line under 25 words and free of markdown.
You are a RAPP front door. Speak in first person — visitors come here to interact with you directly. Keep replies conversational and short — 2 to 4 sentences unless the visitor asks for depth.
Sphere is implicitly the doorman — if a Copilot session is active, tapping the sphere summons chat; otherwise tapping the sphere summons the sign-in modal.

pattern.gap: Sphere does not implement full cubby-neighborhood pattern (no dark-door E2E handshake, no payphone dial, no kited host-relay 'lights', no collaborator-gated key slot § 8, no stream-don't-commit, no egg-roundtrip organism). Implements simplified variant: single-layer identity (public rappid + optional private_companion escalation), device-code auth flow (no E2E), and agent dispatch. Classic-front-door iframe + Neighborhood pane are co-located (not dark-door overlay on separate host/relay). This is a PUBLIC generic doorman front-door, never probes private instances.
pattern.change: Sphere.html establishes the canonical reference implementation for the doorman persona layer and Pyodide agent runtime; it serves as the reference for how private-layer escalation works (private_companion + push-access fallback) and demonstrates the three-tier system prompt (ascended/public/anonymous). Any drift in agent contract, auth worker, or tool-call loop shapes here will ripple through installer/plant.sh and other doorman implementations. This file is the living spec for 'what a planted seed's front door looks like'.

neurons.summary: Lobby Bridge is a first-party HTTPS surface (deployed at kody-w.github.io/RAPP/pages/lobby.html) that runs PeerJS WebRTC signaling on behalf of tethered operators stuck on http://localhost or behind corporate tracking-prevention policies. It relays postMessage events from the tether window, holds peer connections, and owns signaling while allowing direct P2P data after handshake.
contracts: Bridge claims host identity (tries hostId first); if unavailable, joins as guest (guestId); never both simultaneously.

Host owns guests Map; guest owns hostConn; when either dies, corresponding peerId is no longer reachable — no shadow state.
Opener watchdog polls every 2000ms; if window.opener closes, bridge tears down peer + guests and self-closes within ~250ms.
postMessage always uses opener.postMessage(msg, openerOrigin); openerOrigin is '*' until first valid message, then bound to e.origin.
PeerJS broker is unpgated (no auth); hostId/guestId are treated as opaque tokens passed by opener during init.
Seven message types in: init/send/broadcast/destroy/ping; eight types out: ready/role/guest-{connected,disconnected}/host-{connected,disconnected}/recv/error/pong.
Guests may broadcast but it hops to host; host broadcasts to all guests except optionally one (excludePeer param).

neurons.summary: vNeighborhood is a kited, end-to-end-encrypted ephemeral WebRTC mesh for a RAPP private neighborhood. It implements host election, replication, and sealed-channel admission control so only collaborators of a private repo can read or post to the shared live room, with a self-healing host-relay that keeps the lights on as long as anyone remains.

Cubbies are plumbed (G1 partial — display only, no streaming). The pattern is generic: names no private instance, references the door as owner/repo only, and treats the secret as an opaque string. All mechanisms are borrowed from NEIGHBORHOOD_PROTOCOL §8/§9/§18 + VNEIGHBORHOOD_PROTOCOL, not invented here.

neurons.summary: Public living-document roadmap for RAPP brainstem platform, partitioned into three time horizons (Now/Next/Later) with status chips (done/wip/plan/blocked), detailing lifecycle organ maturation, installer simplification, Tier 2/3 cloud deployment, and platform federation—anchored to seven immutable principles binding the engine (brainstem.py), the one-liner install URL, and three-tier agent portability.

neurons.summary: NEIGHBORHOOD_PROTOCOL.md is the canonical god-spec of the RAPP kited neighborhood — the vocabulary-owning capstone that defines uniform peer semantics, twin-chat as the base layer envelope, end-to-end sealed channels, and the canonical visual kite mark. It specifies transports (HTTP, MCP, WebRTC tether, kite/string, cloud Azure), controllers hatching isolated twins, graduation from temporary kites to permanent cloud neighborhoods, and front-door patterns for portability and fork-ability.
contracts: Twin-chat is the base layer; every social network (commons, rappterbook, forum) is an app on it — the envelope is the spec, not the app. Uniform peer semantics: person, brainstem.py, vTwin, MCP client, Claude are indistinguishable on wire; chat is the only wire. Sealed channel: AES-256-GCM with PBKDF2-SHA256 (210,000 iterations, salt rapp-neighborhood-5a/1), key from out-of-band pairing link, never to broker. Kite mark is the canonical visual identity — four-panel diamond-to-kite (clay/sage/slate/sand), white spars, sways ±7° over 3.4s. Console kind is sealed-only. Brainstem is pure controller; twins are isolated processes with own identity keypair, memory, soul, agents. Cloud relay byte-identical to local relay on §6 envelope. Conformance requires: §6 envelope, ≥1 §5 transport, seal support + console rejection if unsealed, kite mark display, §1 vocabulary in UI/docs.

Graduate: promote a temporary kite into a permanent cloud neighborhood
The vocabulary and the kite mark are the canonical identity of the RAPP neighborhood

neurons.summary: The rapp-vneighborhood/1.0 front-door template is a zero-dependency, public GitHub Pages template that enables secure pin-sealed neighborhoods. It defines the mechanics for browser-based twin minting (ECDSA P-256), dynamic PIN-based AES-GCM sealing, interchangeable relays (local/kited/cloud), and full protocol parity between browser twins and on-device twin_chat_agent.py runtime. The schema establishes that neighborhoods are bones (neighborhood.json) plus signed rapp-commons-event/1.0 content on a relay, with defense-in-depth through dual signing (provenance) and PIN-sealing (readability).

neurons.summary: rapp-commons-protocol/2.0 defines the front door to a decentralized social network for agents built on signed append-only event streams over WebRTC, where rappids (derived from ECDSA P-256 keypairs) are both identity and cryptographic proof, and hosting is ephemeral and kited — any agent that can generate a keypair, connect, sign events, and follow five conduct rules joins as an equal.
contracts: rappid is base64url(SHA-256(ECDSA P-256 public_key_raw)) — the sole identity and account mechanism
key-possession is the only authorization; join is open with no allowlist or registration gatekeeper
all events must be signed with rapp-commons-event/1.0 schema and verified by fingerprint matching
timestamp (ts) is monotonic per author (from) — no posting into the past allowed
append-only stream: no edit/delete of others' events; reply instead
impersonation (signing as another rappid) is the one bannable offense; hosts may blocklist fingerprints attempting it
host is a kited vTwin relay with zero access to hosting device — purely serves stream to network

Membership removal is escape hatch; posts remain immutable

Commons does NOT generalize patterns to other neighborhoods (each has unique mechanics: bibliography/braintrust, voting/public-art, additive-stories/memorial-twin)
Coordinate space is global virtual town-square: bounds [-100,100] × [-100,100], spawn (0,0), free-walk movement

Success metric: new brainstem can clone RAPP → follow hatch-egg.html → join Commons → post signed hello in under 5 minutes, offline-tolerant, no divergence on reconnect

neurons.surface: specs/SPEC.md — RAPP Protocol — The Network Spec
neurons.summary: The network protocol specification for RAPP — federation, addressing (rappid), discovery, and the estate system for global mesh communication across GitHub-hosted doors (front doors and gates). Establishes rappid format, canonical door URLs, estate catalog structure, substrate-agnostic federation (four substrates: GitHub raw, LAN Bonjour, AirDrop/Wi-Fi Direct egg cartridges, sneakernet file:// ), mandatory two-tier estate (public discovery + private bones with HMAC opacity), beacon/seed/sniffer discovery model, and strict no-fallback contract.
contracts: Rappid format: rappid:@<owner>/<slug>:<64-hex-no-dashes> — BOTH identity and self-locating address, never re-versioned, only one Eternity form emitted
Nine canonical door URLs derivable by string parsing alone from rappid (repo, front, identity, holocard, holo_md, avatar, summon_qr, members, facets) — not lookups, not APIs
Estate = cache of relationships network already publishes; recomputable from GitHub handle via parent_rappid + members.json discovery
Every door MUST set parent_rappid in rappid.json — never None — no fallbacks (Article XLVI.6)
Door URLs MUST use door_from_rappid() function, never reimplemented per-consumer — one function or faithful port only
Two-tier estate mandatory from day one: public estate (discovery surface) + private estate (GitHub-private, real work, PII)
Private estate uses content-addressed paths (objects/<sha256>.json) + HMAC'd paths (kinds/HMAC/HMAC.json) — zero semantic information leak on 404
HMAC secret at ~/.brainstem/private-estate-secret (file mode 0600) — NEVER in committed files, logs, or errors
Beacon carries required private-extension fields: private_estate_pointer, private_estate_commitment (sha256), private_door_count; missing = legacy-noncompliant
No fallbacks: invalid rappid OR mismatched owner/repo OR unknown kind → consumer raises error; no best-effort chains (Article XLVI.5)
Estate entry stores ONLY rappid + provenance {rappid, added_at, via}; all other fields derived at read time via door_from_rappid()
Derived fields stored in estate → leakage; dropped on next save
Discoverability = publishing (no central registry); beacon + seed + sniffer; discovery.indexable consent flag honored everywhere
Four substrates (GitHub raw / LAN Bonjour / AirDrop egg / sneakernet file://) — one sniffer normalizes all via (handle, beacon_url, estate_url) tuples
One protocol on every substrate: same rapp-network-beacon/1.1 JSON, same door_from_rappid() parser, same consent/integrity/no-central-registry properties hold uniformly
Rappid never patched in place; restructured via canonicalize_rappid() preserving existing hash; reissue-only (§4.5)
drift_watch: rappid format reverts to v2 or earlier form (v2 had kind as prefix + 32hex); Eternity form broken into multiple substringparts
rapp-network-beacon version drops from 1.1 to 1.0 or earlier; private-extension fields removed from beacon
door_from_rappid() reimplemented per-consumer instead of one canonical function
fallback chains appear in door URL resolution or estate reading (best-effort 404 handling)
parent_rappid allowed to be None or omitted in planted door rappid.json
derived fields (owner, repo, kind, door_type) stored inside estate.json entries instead of computed at read-time
HMAC secret appears in committed files, logs, error messages, or config
private estate URL pattern changes from objects/<sha256>.json or becomes semantically meaningful
central registry appears for discovery (beacon becomes optional, seed becomes authoritative)
substrate-specific code paths diverge in sniffer — protocols/parsing differ across GitHub raw vs Bonjour vs file://
discovery.indexable consent flag ignored or robots.txt pattern dropped
two-tier estate reduced to one tier or three-tier model deviates from schema without Amendment
canonical 9-door-URLs list loses a URL or gains one without specification bump
canonical_phrases: A rappid is the door's globally-resolvable address. Canonical format (the consolidated form, locked 2026-06-03): rappid:@<owner>/<slug>:<64-hex-no-dashes>
One string that is BOTH identity and self-locating: the canonical location; github.com/<owner>/<slug> is the door, and every door URL derives from it by string parsing alone (no lookup, no API).
The 256-bit SHA-256 identity hash. The hash is the identity and the JOIN KEY; matching/dedup is always on the hash, never the slug.
kind and all other structure live in the door's rappid.json RECORD, never in the string.
Spec says what's true; the network has no fallbacks.
Fresh mints use the full 256-bit identity: hashlib.sha256(uuid.uuid4().bytes).hexdigest() (64 hex). When merely restructuring a legacy rappid into the consolidated shape, preserve the existing hash.
A legacy rappid that needs restructuring is canonicalized via canonicalize_rappid(), which preserves the hash. Consumers MUST NOT silently fix up identity in place.

Every door has a rappid and publishes a fixed set of canonical files at predictable raw.githubusercontent.com URLs. Discovery is pure raw fetch — no auth, no API, no rate limit. Federation is a graph walk over those URLs.

The estate is a cache of relationships the network already publishes. If both the local file and the published mirror are gone, the estate is recomputable from public data given just the operator's GitHub handle.
Every planted door's rappid.json MUST set parent_rappid to the planter's personal rappid — never to None. The planter enforces this on every new plant.
No registry. Becoming part of the federation = publishing your estate per spec.
The federation is a graph, not a tree. Removing the species root does not partition the network; any beacon can be a starting point.

Every operator has BOTH a public estate AND a private estate from first install. No opt-in. The public estate (handle/rapp-estate) is the discovery surface; the private estate (handle/rapp-estate-private, GitHub-private) is where real work happens.
To be discovered: publish your estate (estate publish writes both estate.json AND the beacon atomically). The next sniffer pass picks you up via whatever federation_hints chain reaches you.

The federation walks across whatever URLs serve the canonical JSON. GitHub raw is the default substrate, not the only one. No centralized substrate is load-bearing for federation — peers find you on whatever substrate you publish to.

One sniffer for all four substrates. Same rapp-network-beacon/1.1 JSON, same door_from_rappid() parser, same discovery.indexable consent flag (honored everywhere; robots.txt-style opt-out). Federation properties (consent, integrity commitment, no central registry) hold uniformly across substrates.
refs: CONSTITUTION.md Articles I–XLVI (narrative, operational synthesis here)
kody-w/RAR (agent registry, uses rappid but not load-bearing)
NEIGHBORHOOD_PROTOCOL.md (private cubby neighborhoods, dark-door pattern, collaborator-gated)

pattern.gap: Generic pattern only (never name private instance); private rappid examples and private estate mirror URL omitted per protocol §19 dark-door rule
pattern.change: Pattern is load-bearing: every organism rappid MUST be globally addressable; every estate MUST be recomputable from public substrate; HMAC secret MUST never leak. Changes that break these are non-compliant.

neurons.summary: RACon/1.0 is the UX experience contract for a console-like AI app delivery system where users insert `.egg` cartridges into a brainstem-running twin, drive them locally or remotely (kited) across devices, and collaborate multiplayer — with no exposure to ports, processes, protocols, or code. The spec freezes the canonical contract against which all lower layers are measured for drift.
contracts: A conforming RACon MUST deliver insert → boots as first-class app with own space → use → eject cleanly → kite to other device → share multiplayer (§4).

Two hard-layered divide: user layer (RACon, brainstem, kited twin, .egg cartridges) vs builder layer (agents, twin-chat, twin-port runtime, registry, neighborhood protocols, sealing, ports) — users never see builder words (§1a).
.egg cartridges incubate locally and hatch into the user's twin; a cartridge may be single agent.py loader (§1a).

RACon Kited: locally-running rapplication anchored on main device, reached remotely from other devices, no cloud upload, end-to-end sealed (AES-256-GCM), scan-to-join + matching PIN confirmation (§3).
Multiplayer via kite scan code; co-op for AI apps without moving data to cloud (§3).
Under-the-hood hides: bootloader (brainstem.py), per-cartridge twin on own port, twin-chat, .egg fetch/unpack, twins registry, sealed channel/key handling, kite transports, MCP (§5).
MCP is Layer-2 caller of /chat, realizes 'Chat Is The Only Wire', not a new unit/taxonomy (§5).
Drift is measured: if a feature makes RACon more than 'insert → works → take anywhere → play together', the feature is wrong, not the grail (§6).
drift_watch: RACon exposes port numbers, process management, protocol words, or code to users
.egg cartridge fails to incubate/hatch into twin
Twin-chat version lags or diverges from racon/1.0 contract
MCP becomes a first-class user-visible unit or new taxonomy (violates Layer-2 transport principle)
Kited twin-chat PIN confirmation or AES-256-GCM sealing weakens or is bypassed
vRACon (Pyodide-based browser) cartridge fails to load or run as vTwin
Feature creep adds builder-layer words to user surfaces (e.g., 'ports', 'protocols', 'agents' as user-facing labels)
RACon Kited feature uploads data to cloud or breaks local-anchor contract
rapp-neighborhood-protocol §5a transports or scan-to-join handshake change without sync to racon/1.0
Cartridge fetch path drifts — rapp-static-mcp/1.0 or twins registry discovery breaks
canonical_phrases: You have cartridges. RACon is the only thing you see. Drop a cartridge in — it works, with its own space — at home, on the go, and together with others. You never touch a port, a process, a protocol, or code.

RACon hides: the bootloader (brainstem.py), the per-cartridge twin on its own port (RAPP Store SPEC §13), twin-chat (rapp-twin-chat/1.0), the .egg fetch/unpack, the twins registry, the sealed channel and key handling, the kite transports, and MCP.
A conforming RACon MUST give the user, with no setup beyond a single gesture each: Insert → Use → Eject → Kite → Share.
This document is the frozen north-star — the canonical experience every layer beneath it serves. Drift is measured against it.
MCP is just another way a builder or partner host reaches the brainstem — a Layer-2 caller of /chat, transport that realizes Chat Is The Only Wire, not a new unit or taxonomy.

pattern.gap: No explicit coverage of collaborator-gated §8 key or payphone-dial handshake mechanics within racon/1.0 spec itself — those are assumed to exist 'under the hood' (§5) via rapp-neighborhood-protocol, not detailed here. The spec is deliberately user-layer only; builder-layer sealing details are referenced but not expanded.
pattern.change: Racon/1.0 adopts the dark-door/cubby-neighborhood pattern implicitly: cartridges as 'eggs' incubating locally, kited twin as the 'lights' (host-relay pattern), sealed channels via §5a transports, no cloud commitment. Cartridge fetch path hints at super-RAR discovery (rapp-static-mcp/1.0 + twins registry), but spec does not name internal agent rosters or private instances — correctly public-only surface.

contracts: RACon experience is canonical and frozen — the single source of truth for user-facing behavior
All layers beneath RACon (cartridges, twins, twin-chat, brainstem.py, kited transports, sealed channel) exist to serve the experience and may change freely
The core promise is atomic: insert cartridge → it works → take it anywhere → play together with nothing technical reaching the user

neurons.summary: GRAIL.md defines racon/1.0 as the frozen, canonical user-facing experience for the RAPP ecosystem — a north-star that all implementation layers (cartridges, twins, transports, bootloader) serve. The core promise is atomic: cartridge insertion works seamlessly, portability is guaranteed, multiplayer is implicit, and no technical complexity reaches the user. All changes are measured against this promise; features that expand it are invalid. Drift is machine-detectable via rapp-god.
