neurons.summary: Master constitutional governance for RAPP ecosystem — 51 articles defining brainstem kernel discipline, agent portability, delimited-slot output contract, rappid identity, cross-substrate federation, and egg cartridge unification. Anchors single-file agent model, tier-parity contract, and dark-door neighborhood pattern.
contracts: Article 0: ONE FILE = ONE AGENT — portable, auditable, replaceable unit across tiers 1/2/3.
Article I: Brainstem kernel is LOADER + LLM LOOP + SLOT SPLITTER only; capability lives in agents not core.
Article II: Delimited slots (|||VOICE|||, |||TWIN|||) are fixed resource; names never repurposed; sub-capability uses inner XML tags (probe, calibration, telemetry), not new slots.

Article III: Single-file agents define rappapp contract; III.3 guarantees Tier 1 agent runs unmodified on Tier 2/3; III.4 wires agent chains via data_slush JSON; III.7 pins location: rapp_brainstem/agents/ = starter set.
Article IV: Blast radius discipline — prefer agent changes over core changes; core PRs must name pattern (slot/tag), justify why not agent, keep to boot/loop/route/split.

Article XV: Tier parity is /chat contract surface invariant — same request/response envelope, tool loop shape, slot split, agent contract, state layout across tiers; LLM transport legitimately differs.

Article XXXIV: rappid is permanent organism identity (format: rappid:v2:<kind>:@<owner>/<slug>:<64hex>@<vault_url>); never regenerated; parent_rappid chain records lineage; same rappid = same organism.
Article XXXVII: Brainstem is a digital organism (rappid, soul, memory, agents, organs/senses); egg is the organism's portable unit; hatch cycle (bond) preserves identity across kernel upgrades.
Article XLVI: Dark-door federated neighborhoods; front_door is global discovery primitive (public GitHub URLs); no central registry — refuse to invent one.
Article XLVII: Rappid and workbench portable across substrates (GitHub/GitLab/Codeberg/WebRTC tether); sneakernet eggs preserve cross-substrate identity.
Article XLVIII: Two-tier estate (created + member neighborhoods); public bilateral channels (mailbox URLs) for operator-to-operator links; private companion repos for sensitive work.
Article XLIX: Twin is persistent AI presence with permanent rappid, soul.md, workbench; two species (owner-proxy + planted); lifecycle = mint→bond→fork→die.
Article L: ONE PORTABLE UNIT = .egg extension; five kinds (organism/rapplication/session/neighborhood/estate) under brainstem-egg/2.x-<kind> family; hatcher refuses unknown kinds, never guesses.
Article LI: Every neighborhood front gate MUST display tether QR on first paint; QR encodes .well-known/neighborhood.egg or neighborhood.json URL.
drift_watch: rappid format reverts from v2 to legacy bare format or v1 — breaks identity portability.
Schema deprecation without deprecation discipline — Article L.4 forbids; all old schemas must remain readable.
New top-level output slot added without amending Article II or freezing name forever — breaks time-travel safety.
Brainstem code grows feature outside boot/loop/route/split (Article IV) — blast radius violation.
Agent portability violated — agent edited to work only on one tier (Article III.3) — breaks III.6.
Central registry invented (Article XLVII) or swarm-runtime class added to core (Article XIV) — design pattern violation.
Dark-door neighborhood gate deployed without tether QR (Article LI) — conformance failure; federation on-ramp broken.
Neighborhood front gate omits parent_rappid → planter's personal rappid (Article XLVI.4) — estate reconstruction impossible.
Single-file agent rule violated; agent shipped as multi-file deliverable (Article III.6) — rapplication corrupted.
.egg cartridge schema unknown to hatcher but hatcher guesses kind instead of refusing (Article L.3) — silent misclassification.
Private companion repo PII exposed in public neighborhood repo or beacon (Article XLVIII.2) — privacy contract broken.

canonical_phrases: The file IS the agent IS the documentation IS the contract.
The brainstem is a loader + an LLM loop + a response splitter. That's it.
Same /chat contract. Same prompt split. Same agent contract. Same state layout. Transport differences below the contract are OK.
An agent file that runs in Tier 1 must run unmodified in Tier 2 and Tier 3.
Delimited slots are a fixed resource — names never repurposed; new sub-capability lives inside as inner tags, not a new slot.
When you want a new ability, drop a *_agent.py in agents/. Write perform(). The brainstem auto-discovers it.
The rappid IS the organism's permanent identity. Same rappid = same organism. Different rappid = different organism.
Refusal is a feature — the hatcher refuses on unknown cartridge kinds, never silently guesses.
Across the substrate, .egg is the only portable container. Same extension, same Pokédex shelf, same drag-drop UX.
Every planted door MUST set parent_rappid to the planter's personal rappid (never None, never root).
The twin is a companion, not an autonomous actor — everything it does is a button the user clicks.
The front door is the global discovery primitive. Permanent lines layer on top; discovery is GitHub URLs.
Every neighborhood front gate MUST display a tether QR code on first paint.

All mechanisms in §19 live in kernel and constitution already; this file is their formal ratification.

contracts: Twin-chat (§6) is the singular base layer; every social network is a channel + message kinds on it, never a separate protocol
A brainstem is a pure controller that hatches isolated twins (own process/workspace/identity); the brainstem never joins or posts itself
The envelope §6 is byte-identical across local ≡ kited ≡ cloud relays; portability via egg/import/fork requires no format translation
Every envelope may be sealed with AES-256-GCM (rapp-sealed/1.0) using PBKDF2-SHA256 210k iterations, salt 'rapp-neighborhood-5a/1', key from out-of-band pairing only
Operating a neighbor's console (kind:console) only happens over sealed channel; plaintext say may be open
The kite mark (four-panel diamond on point, stretched bottom, sways ±7° ~3.4s) is the canonical visual identity when actively kited
A kited vTwin hosts temporary multiplayer neighborhood; closing tab destroys peer/session; cloud neighborhood (Azure Function) persists
Uniform peers principle: person, brainstem.py, vTwin, MCP client, Claude on string are indistinguishable on wire (twin-chat only)
A private dark-door neighborhood has no public front door, uses payphone-dial (rappid + collaborator auth) for access, lights pattern for host/guest lifecycle
The cubby (rapp-cubby/1.0) is per-member isolated directory (agents/organs/senses/rapplications/neighborhoods/eggs); members write own cubby only, read all; round-trips as brainstem-egg/2.3-cubby

neurons.summary: The NEIGHBORHOOD_PROTOCOL (rapp-neighborhood-protocol/1.0) is the god spec and capstone that owns the vocabulary and architecture of RAPP's kited neighborhood — a sealed, multiplayer, portable system where uniform peers (person, brainstem, vTwin, Claude) speak twin-chat on transports (HTTP/MCP/WebRTC/CDP/Azure Function). Twin-chat is the singular base; every social app (commons, rappterbook, forum) is just a channel + kinds on it. A vTwin kited in a browser hosts and the private dark-door pattern (§19) enables gated, self-hosting collaborator neighborhoods with cubbies, payphone handshakes, lights relay election, and super-RAR discovery — all reusing the same byte-identical §6 envelope and §8 seal across local, ephemeral, and cloud dimensions.
canonical_phrases: Twin-chat (§6) is the base layer; every social network here is just an app on it
A brainstem is a pure controller that hatches isolated twins (own process, workspace, identity, memory) and drives them by twin-chat
The envelope (§6) and the seal (§8) are byte-identical on all relays: local ≡ kited ≡ cloud
Uniform peers: a person typing, a brainstem.py, a vTwin, an MCP client, or Claude on the string are indistinguishable on the wire
The kite mark is the face of the RAPP neighborhood — scan to join
A private dark-door neighborhood 404s to outsiders; members dial via payphone with collaborator auth
The cubby is per-member isolated estate (agents/organs/senses/rapplications/neighborhoods/eggs); members write their own, read all collaborators'
The super-RAR is one searchable registry over the whole stack across every cubby
The relay is interchangeable: where the signed log lives — local file, ephemeral kited host, or permanent cloud Function

https://github.com/kody-w/vbrainstem (reference implementation: vBrainstem, CDP string, doorman pattern)

pattern.change: §19 dark-door pattern is fully specified — private neighborhoods use collaborator-gated sealed key via payphone handshake, lights host election (first vTwin claims relay, next-in-line guest claims on drop, lights go dark on last leave), cubby estate per-member with read-all visibility, super-RAR discovery across entire stack, brainstem-egg/2.3-cubby round-trip operator, stream-don't-commit to avoid git pollution. Generic pattern, never names private instances.

contracts: Agent files are single-file Python modules with embedded __manifest__ (rapp-agent/1.0 schema)
All naming must use snake_case (filenames, manifest names, dependencies—no dashes)
BasicAgent (@rapp/basic_agent) is the required base class for all agents
perform() method must always return str
Registry at registry.json is ground truth containing all agent manifests with SHA256 hashes
Security scanner rejects eval(), exec(), compile(), os.system(), subprocess.*, __import__(), and hardcoded secrets
Agent submission via GitHub Issue with pipeline → staging → review → approval → agents/ → seed forged → card self-assembles
Seven deterministic agent types (LOGIC, DATA, SOCIAL, SHIELD, CRAFT, HEAL, WEALTH) derived from category + tags with fixed matchup chart
Card seeds resolve deterministically via mulberry32 PRNG (offline, reproducible per seed)
MCP static catalog uses rapp-static-mcp/1.0 profile with SHA256 integrity verification before exec
EMU accounts cannot interact with public repos; personal GitHub account required for submission
Quality tiers: community (auto-validated) → verified (reviewed) → official (guaranteed compatibility)
Deduplication via display_name collision detection in registry.json duplicates array
No secrets in code; use os.environ.get() with declared requires_env
canonical_phrases: This file is read by AI agents, not humans.
Chat Is The Only Wire
Agent manifests inherit BasicAgent
perform() always returns str
Single file — everything in one .py file
Works offline — handle missing env vars gracefully
No network calls in __init__ — keep constructor fast
Your namespace is @yourgithubusername. It's yours forever.
Same seed always yields the same card.
Every capability — install, search, run an agent, memory — flows through that single /chat call.

contracts: ORAPPTER is a pure LisPy AI runtime running offline-first on device (browser RappterVM, CLI lisp.py, or Ollama endpoint)
No cloud, no API key, no internet required for Layer 0 (deterministic card engine) or Layer 1 (template intelligence)
Layer 0 provides deterministic card resolution, seed-hash, search-agents, binder-status entirely offline via local JSON registry and math functions
Layer 1 uses rule-based template responses (pattern matching on question type + registry/card-aware answers) with no LLM required
Layer 2 integrates local LLM (Ollama + Gemma 3/4 default) for full AI when available, supporting embeddings and multi-turn conversation with memory
Layer 3 (cloud brainstem, optional) only activates when internet available, routing to remote Ollama or larger models (70B+)
Conversations persist to IndexedDB (browser) or JSON file (CLI), survive app restart and device reboot, enable fork/export/import via QR/NFC/sneakernet
Knowledge base degrades gracefully: semantic embeddings when model available, cached embeddings when offline + prior learned, keyword text-search as fallback
Battery-aware operation: 'full' mode (>50%) uses local LLM + embeddings; 'eco' (20-50%) uses templates only; 'minimal' (<20%) card ops only; 'dead' (<5%) saves state and sleeps

Digital twin transfers between devices without internet via sneakernet (QR code, NFC tap, Bluetooth, typed card name, copy-paste JSON) with full state restoration (conversations + knowledge + card data)

Card-powered agent system: activate-card() resolves card from registry, builds system prompt from card attributes (display_name, type_line, rarity_label, power/toughness, description, flavor), creates conversational agent
Tool system: register-tool() adds to dictionary; orappter-ask-with-tools() parses AI responses for [TOOL: name(args)] syntax, executes LisPy functions, feeds results back to LLM
Built-in tools: resolve-card, search-agents, binder-status, seed-hash, kb-query, card-value (all work offline)
Offline template engine with fallback: if LLM unavailable, pattern-match on prompt (what/how/why/who/when questions), answer from registry + card data + Three Rules decision framework
Model support: automatic selection from gemma3:1b (1GB, phone), gemma3:4b (4GB default), gemma3:12b, llama3.2:3b, mistral:7b, phi3:mini; no configuration needed
Three runtime environments: RappterVM (browser), lispy.py (CLI), Ollama endpoint (device); one spec, three form factors
The name ORAPPTER: Offline RAPPter RunTimE foR agents

neurons.summary: ORAPPTER is a deterministic, offline-first AI runtime specification declaring three architecturally independent layers (Layer 0: pure math card engine; Layer 1: rule-based templates; Layer 2: local LLM via Ollama) that gracefully degrade, enabling digital twins to persist, converse, and transfer between devices without internet. Device is the computer; battery is the timer; the cloud is optional.

pattern.gap: ORAPPTER declares offline card-agent materialization and sneakernet portability but does not reference or define the cubby-neighborhood pattern (rapp-cubby/1.0, rapp-super-rar/1.0, dark door, payphone E2E handshake, collaborator-gated keys, the cubby, stream-don't-commit, egg-roundtrip organisms). ORAPPTER's digital twin + sneakernet transfer map to egg capsules and portability but are not formally integrated into NEIGHBORHOOD_PROTOCOL §19 cubby pattern nor RAR/agent discovery. The spec is generic, portable, and independent — threat model and federation boundary not specified.
pattern.change: Add reference section cross-linking to rapp-cubby/1.0 and rapp-super-rar/1.0; clarify scope boundary between ORAPPTER device-local twin identity + the cubby-neighborhood collaborator-gated discovery layer (ORAPPTER is intra-device; cubby manages inter-operator sealed interchange). Do not name private instances; remain generic.

canonical_phrases: ORAPPTER is a pure LisPy AI runtime that connects every agent in the Rappter ecosystem to local AI. It runs in the browser (RappterVM), on the CLI (lisp.py), or on any device with an Ollama endpoint. No cloud. No API key. No internet required for core operations.
The name: Offline RAPPter RunTimE foR agents.
Everything above the cloud sync line works with zero internet. The cloud is optional. The device is the computer.
These work on a phone in airplane mode.
Battery is the timer.
The device is the computer.
Your digital twin, powered by local AI, in your pocket.
Your AI. Your device. Your twin. Lives on.
When someone asks 'what is ORAPPTER?' — you say: 'It's the AI that runs on your device. No cloud. No internet. Your digital twin, powered by local AI, in your pocket. Battery is the timer.'

contracts: A .py.stub file MUST contain ONLY metadata — no executable code, no class definitions, no imports; build_registry.py enforces this.
__manifest__ dict is required in every stub with schema, name, version, display_name, description, author, tags, category, quality_tier (always 'private'), requires_env, dependencies.
__source__ dict is required with schema, type ('github_private'), repo, ref, path pointing to real agent in private repo.
quality_tier MUST be 'private' for all stubs in public RAR.
Anyone can see stub metadata in public RAR; only authorized users can install the agent; source code bytes remain gated by GitHub access control.
Two submission methods: (1) @kody/rar_remote_agent with action='publish_private' automatically generates stub and opens submission issue; (2) manual stub creation + PR to CommunityRAPP/RAR.
Metadata (name, description, tags, repo URL) is publicly readable; source bytes are gated.
Public RAR has no server-side verification of stub-to-source mapping — enforcement happens at brainstem install time (404 on invalid pointer).

@kody/rar_remote_agent action parameter names or behavior changes
rar.config.json registry source mechanisms drift from private repo pointer pattern
github_private source type is deprecated or replaced
stub file extension changes from .py.stub

neurons.summary: A spec defining how to publish private agents to public RAR via metadata-only stubs (rapp-agent/1.0, rapp-source/1.0), keeping source code gated while enabling discovery; two submission paths: automated (@kody/rar_remote_agent publish_private) or manual PR; stubs show in public registry but install is access-controlled by GitHub permissions.
canonical_phrases: A .py.stub file is pure metadata — no class, no imports, no executable code.
public RAR hosts a tiny stub — just enough metadata to make the agent discoverable, plus a pointer back to this repo
quality_tier: 'private' — always 'private' for stubs
Anyone can see the stub in public RAR; only people with read access to your private repo can install the agent.
The agent shows up in public RAR's store, browseable to everyone. A Locked badge tells viewers they need access to install.
Metadata privacy is NOT guaranteed: Name, description, tags, and your repo URL are all readable by anyone browsing public RAR. The bytes are gated; the listing is not.
Public RAR has no way to verify your stub points where it claims. Anyone can publish a stub with bogus pointers; the brainstem will just 404 at install time.
github_private — the source type for private repo stubs

pattern.gap: No explicit mention of egg-roundtrip organisms or stream-don't-commit patterns; coverage focuses on stub mechanics rather than full cubby-neighborhood federation semantics.
pattern.change: This spec exemplifies the dark-door pattern in action: private registry (private-rar-template) feeds metadata to public RAR (the payphone dial), stubs act as handshake mechanism (no source leaks), brainstem enforces access control at install time, and collaborator-gated GitHub permissions control who can fetch bytes. The cubby-neighborhood pattern is implicit — the private repo is the dark door, the stub is the collaborator-gated public signal, and the registry config routes between them.

neurons.summary: The Fleet Management stack specification — a drop-in RAR component that transforms one brainstem into an SSH-driven federation controller for a network of Mac-mini brainstems, enabling discovery, authorization, deployment, cross-peer twin chatting, and mesh execution across the fleet.
contracts: Two-file structure: fleet_agent.py (SSH adapter, 23 actions) + twin_egg_hatcher_agent.py (scale-aware hatcher)
Install via copy to ~/.brainstem/src/rapp_brainstem/agents/; load_agents() re-scans on next /chat, no restart required
LLM tool surface: Fleet(...) with action parameter selecting from the 23-action enum; HatchTwinEgg(...) separate tool

23 canonical actions split into four categories: discovery/ops (discover, ping, exec, read/write/ls/tail, ports, ps, chat, mesh_chat, mesh_exec, brainstem_health, status), deployment (provision_brainstem, install_agent, hatch_egg, boot_federation), self-extension (custom, extend, cap, list_caps)
mesh_chat and mesh_exec fan one prompt/command to every twin on every peer brainstem in parallel, gathering responses into single envelope
custom action executes arbitrary Python snippet once; extend action persists snippets as named capabilities in agents/fleet_capabilities/<name>.py; cap invokes saved capabilities by name
Trust model: local single-user only — same boundary as any agent in ~/.brainstem/.../agents/; unsafe for multi-tenant brainstems due to arbitrary Python execution
Environment variables: EGG_SERVER_URL (egg source), FLEET_SSH_USER (SSH username on peer minis)
Peer-side prerequisite: organism-lifecycle stack (Twin + EggHatcher) must be installed on every peer mini before federation
Canonical hatcher available as standalone RAR agent: @kody-w/twin_egg_hatcher_agent.py
canonical_phrases: Drop-in pack that turns one brainstem into a fleet controller for a network of Mac-mini brainstems
Discover, authorize, deploy, chat any twin on any peer, fan out across the mesh — all from one chat
Same trust boundary as every other agent in ~/.brainstem/.../agents/ — local, single-user. Don't install this stack on a multi-tenant brainstem
The LLM can synthesize new behavior on the fly using the fleet helpers (ssh, http_chat, http_health, probe_tcp, etc.)
mesh_chat: Same prompt to every twin on every peer
mesh_exec: Same shell on every peer in parallel

neurons.summary: peers.json is the runtime peer registry file (`~/.rapp/peers.json` on disk, never in egg) that declares which neighborhood members to snapshot and how to reach them by carrier (LAN-SSH, GitHub, Tailscale, etc.). Schema: array of peer entries with common fields (name, url) + carrier-specific coords (ssh_user/ssh_host, github_neighborhood, auth_url, egg_path); carrier selection is implicit by field presence; loopback URLs filtered; env override via BRAINSTEM_PEERS for HTTP-probe-only.
canonical_phrases: The snapshot host reads `~/.rapp/peers.json` to know which peers to probe AND which carrier to use for each.
A peer entry MAY declare multiple carriers; the snapshot agent picks the first one that works.
A peer entry with only `url` falls back to HTTP-probe-only — the manifest records the probe result but captures no workspace bytes (the peer appears in the egg but with `twins: []`).
Env override: `BRAINSTEM_PEERS=url1,url2,...` (comma-sep, HTTP-probe only — no carrier).
Loopback URLs (`localhost`, `127.0.0.1`) are filtered out — those are 'local,' not peers.

Carriers are pluggable. Adding a new carrier means: (a) defining its coordinate fields in `peers.json`, (b) implementing the (read, write) pair in the snapshot/run agents, (c) listing it here. The egg format itself does not change.

neurons.summary: The seed-index.json specification (not publicly hosted; local/private pattern) catalogs the dark-door cubby-neighborhood pattern: private collaborator-gated repo (404 to outsiders), per-member isolated estate directories, super-RAR aggregating the full RAPP stack, payphone QR discovery, stream-don't-commit distribution, AES-256-GCM E2E encryption gated by collaborator access, and host-replicated live boards with election handoff. Five of nine mechanisms are specced (G1, G4, G5, G8, G10); four are proposed (G2 dark door, G3 payphone, G6 handshake split, G7 loadout, G9 super-RAR). Gap register tracks graduation: private-cubby-neighborhood invents locally as `rapp-<private-cubby-neighborhood>-*`, proves out, then hoists generalized schemas into canonical docs (NEIGHBORHOOD_PROTOCOL, PUBLIC_PRIVATE_BOUNDARY, OSI, etc.).
canonical_phrases: A cubby is one member's isolated housing for their entire rapp estate — the same environment their local computer provides for their on-device brainstem, smashed into a directory.
You write only inside your own cubby (plus the append-only zones: events/, your entry in members.json + cubbies/index.json). Cross-cubby changes ride pull requests the cubby owner merges.
The private-cubby-neighborhood is the storage layer for agents you do NOT want committed to a brainstem grail repo as kernel agents.

Dark door has no public front door (it's the private-cubby-neighborhood). The repo is private; to anyone without collaborator access every URL returns 404 — wrong number and no-access are indistinguishable, so the door's contents and membership stay invisible to outsiders.

Members dial in instead: Local brainstems: gh auth login once; private-cubby-neighborhood mount clones via your collaborator access. Kited vTwins on the public web: scan the door's QR. The operator shares a QR (open …/payphone.html?share=<rappid>, or run private-cubby-neighborhood qr, to render + download one). Scanning it opens the payphone — the generic public dialer — pre-dialed to the private-cubby-neighborhood.

The super-RAR — the private-cubby-neighborhood's super-store. A standard RAR stocks agents; this carries the WHOLE RAPP stack (agents, organs, senses, rapplications, neighborhoods, eggs) across every cubby — one private registry over the full ecosystem.

Per HERO_USECASE §2 + ECOSYSTEM §10, a local clone of this seed is a parallel offline dimension of the global neighborhood. Local pheromones accumulate as content-addressed rapp-pheromone/1.0 envelopes (prev_hash chained). When the local dimension comes back online, the Dream Catcher reassimilates.

contracts: A rapplication is a portable, self-describing bundle of one Python agent (and optional UI/state/docs) that drops into any RAPP brainstem and runs.
Rapplication singleton must import BasicAgent, define exactly one Agent subclass, implement perform(self, **kwargs), and declare a top-level __manifest__ dict with schema: rapp-agent/1.0.
manifest.json must declare BOTH agent AND ui — absence of either triggers rejection (E_NO_UI or E_BARE_AGENT_BELONGS_IN_RAR).
runtime: agent (default) loads in-process to host brainstem's agents/ dir; runtime: twin hatches into isolated port-addressable brainstem-twin reached over twin-chat.

Gated entries (access: private) have source in private GitHub repo, catalog publishes metadata only, anonymous GET returns 404, authenticated fetch with PAT returns 200; installer must use GitHub's auth layer, not catalog's.

Cartridge protocol: parent runtime posts rapp-cartridge envelope to iframe, UI sends rapp:invoke/rapp:chat/rapp:fetch messages, parent routes through /chat (Chat Is The Only Wire — MCP is Layer-2 transport).

Workspace contract: every installed rapplication gets isolated persistent ${BRAINSTEM_ROOT}/.brainstem_data/workspaces/<id>/ directory, operations guarded against path traversal, MUST NOT cross-rapp access.
Twins registry (twins.json) maps id→port→running brainstem-twin with soul/agents/status; hatch allocates port, materializes isolated twin root, boots brainstem, registers entry.
Submission modes: A (copy into catalog), B (federation — external repo, URLs rewritten to kody-w/rapp_store), C (gated federation — private repo, metadata public, bytes gated by GitHub auth).

neurons.summary: The RAPP_Store SPEC.md defines the portable rapplication bundle contract (layout, manifest schema, singleton/UI/service requirements), three submission modes (copy/federation/gated-federation), the cartridge inter-runtime protocol for UI↔agent communication, workspace isolation per rapp, and twin-port hatching for multi-agent isolation on dedicated ports. It unifies public and private discovery under one schema and enforces validation rules that are the receiver's responsibility.
canonical_phrases: A rapplication is a portable, self-describing bundle of one Python agent (and optional UI / state / docs) that drops into any RAPP brainstem and runs.
Twin-port runtime fixes crowding: a runtime: twin rapplication hatches into its own brainstem-twin on its own port, carrying only its own agents and persona.
The cartridge is part of the rapplication contract — UIs that adopt it get free upgrades whenever the parent runtime adds capabilities.
Chat Is The Only Wire — every capability reaches a rapplication through /chat, never a bespoke endpoint.

The pattern is public discovery, private substance: gated rapplications publish metadata to the public catalog; source bytes live in a private GitHub repo and are gated behind authenticated raw.githubusercontent.com access.
Without a UI, the artifact is just a swarm-agent — submit to kody-w/RAR instead.
Rapplications are agent + UI bundles by definition.
The catalog is an honest broker — it documents the gate. The installer + GitHub are the enforcement layer.

pattern.change: The spec establishes the private-cubby dark-door pattern via gated rapplications (§11): public catalog entry + private repo source + GitHub auth gate (raw.githubusercontent.com 404 for anon, 200 for PAT). Extends to twin-port runtime (§13) where isolated twins are the cubbies, twins.json is the registry, twin-chat is the neighborhood protocol (§5a per rapp-neighborhood-protocol). Workspace contract (§12) governs per-rapp state isolation. Cartridge protocol (§9) and MCP (§9.5) are the payphone/E2E handshake surface. Schema versions (rapp-application/1.0, rapp-gated/1.0, rapp-workspace/1.0, rapp-twins/1.0) are all declared. Generic pattern — never names a private instance (uses wildhaven/cockpit as worked example only, not as a private template). Cover is complete.

neurons.summary: The content-layer authoring discipline for RAPP_Store: governs the catalog (index.json as canonical), rapplication bundles, submission/validation flow, and static presentation surfaces. Establishes trust via SHA256 hash pinning, enforces one-file-per-surface, mandates publisher namespacing, splits bare agents to RAR, and defers infrastructure/auth to upstream platform. Material changes require proposals in docs/proposals/ before application.
contracts: index.json is canonical—rapp exists iff entry in index.json with resolving singleton_url and matching singleton_sha256
SHA256 is the sole trust boundary; binder refuses install on hash mismatch (hard-fail, fetch-fails-closed)
one file ships, one file runs: singleton is ship-time artifact; edits to source/ must rebuild singleton; service rapps hand-write both *_agent.py and *_service.py

no engine code (brainstem, runtime, API, auth) lives in RAPP_Store—only catalog, validator, rapplication bundles, submission tooling, and static surfaces; everything else belongs upstream in kody-w/RAPP or kody-w/RAR
publisher namespacing mandatory: apps/@<publisher>/<id>/; @rapp reserved; community publishers use @<github-username>
bare *_agent.py with no UI/service/eggs goes to RAR, not RAPP_Store; Article V/Proposal 0001 split
quality_tier field (official/community) set by reviewer at promotion, not self-declared by submitter; featured placement separate axis from tier
eggs are immutable: never overwrite; new state ships as new egg with new filename; path-traversal guards reject .. segments
reserved IDs locked forever per RESERVED_IDS—once bound, name stays bound even if removed from live catalog
validator (scripts/lib_rapp.py) is single source of truth; both publish agent and receiver workflow call same validator; tests lock current behavior
federation over mirroring: submitter catalog entry may point at own GitHub repo; store doesn't become mirror; submit_repo mode adds source block with repo/ref/path/commit_sha

senses co-shelved in senses/ directory and index.json senses[] block; read directly from raw URLs, not mirrored from sense store; drift is not patched
Material changes proposed in docs/proposals/ before applied, mirroring platform Article XXVIII; amendment workflow mirrors platform Article XXVIII

The Pages app is the front door

neurons.summary: The RAR vs Rapp Store split is the load-bearing architectural boundary separating single-file bare agents (registered in RAR via __manifest__) from packaged rapplications (bundled with UI, service, or state cartridges in rapp_store). Ratified in CONSTITUTION Articles XXVII (the mechanical test: one file → RAR; bundle → store) and XXVIII (material changes require proposals with context/migration/rollback before application). The distinction preserves the sacred tenet — agents stay single-file and registry-agnostic — while enabling curated discovery and full-stack distribution.
refs: SPEC.md Section 12 (RAR — The RAPP Agent Registry) + Section 12.0 (critical distinction — RAPP agent vs RAR agent)
canonical_phrases: One file → RAR. Bundle → rapp store. The manifest decides.

A RAPP agent is not a RAR agent. They are two different things, and the agent file is sacred in both senses — but the registry adds requirements on top of §5 that a plain RAPP agent does not need to satisfy.
The manifest is the cost of admission to RAR. It is not the cost of being a RAPP agent.
A store sells finished products. A registry indexes building blocks. They want different metadata, different browse paths, and different trust posture.
The agent.py is sacred. The registry is sanctioned. The registry does not get to redefine what a RAPP agent is.
No silent restructures. The PR is the receipt.
Use that repo's documented submission flow exactly as an outside contributor would. Do not bypass the front door because you happen to have push access.

pattern.gap: Private instance enforcement mechanisms and auditing loop for dark-door compliance not fully specified in public specs; assumes NEIGHBORHOOD_PROTOCOL §19 handling in private cubbies
pattern.change: Article XXVII mechanically separates distribution channels (bare agents vs rapplications) without API-level gating; Article XXIX adds mandatory front-door governance (submission flows, issue templates, agents like @rapp/publish-to-rapp-store) to prevent silent pushes; both changes ship together under Article XXVIII proposal structure

canonical_phrases: Three peer artifact types, three peer stores, one front door: agents (bare *_agent.py files in RAR), rapplications (bundles in RAPP_Store), senses (modular per-channel overlays in RAPP_Sense_Store)
One artifact, one home. One publisher, one path. One issue flow per artifact type
kody-w/RAPP (the engine) is unchanged in role: it's the wire, not a store

The routing agent @rapp/rapp_publish_agent detects artifact type (BasicAgent+perform → agent; manifest.json with rapp-application/1.0 → rapplication; name+delimiter+response_key+wrapper_tag+system_prompt → sense) and routes to correct repo's [X] issue flow

neurons.summary: Proposal extending the two-store RAPP ecosystem (RAR + RAPP_Store) to three peer artifact stores — agents, rapplications, senses — with unified submission routing via @rapp/rapp_publish_agent, publisher namespacing (apps/@publisher/), new RAPP_Sense_Store with per-channel surface activation, and a shared front door (vBrainstem + ecosystem.json aggregator) across all three catalogs.
versions: ecosystem catalog structure (new, no explicit version)

pattern.gap: Proposal does not explicitly formalize dark-door handshake mechanism or E2E payphone protocol for cross-repo submission routing; treats artifact submission as transparent issue-filing rather than encrypted collaborator-gated exchange. kited host-relay 'lights' and brainstem-egg roundtrip organisms (brainstem-egg/2.3-cubby) are referenced in neighborhood context but not integrated into sense or rapplication spec.
pattern.change: Extends generic cubby-neighborhood model (public artifact stores, publisher namespacing, unified front door) but leaves private-instance dark-door pattern unspecified for the ecosystem layer; suitable for PUBLIC ONLY RAPP ecosystem per NEIGHBORHOOD_PROTOCOL §19 constraints.

contracts: A capability is kernel if removing it breaks /chat; a capability is a service (now called organ) if removing it leaves /chat working but disables optional capability
The litmus test: Can the brainstem still answer a chat turn if I delete this? No = kernel, Yes = service/organ
Kernel includes: auth (Copilot token), models (active-model selection), voice (per-channel reply config), agent discovery, sense composition, soul loading, tool-call loop
Services/organs include: binder (admin UI), neighborhood (peer-brainstem comms), every rapplication's own *_service.py (now *_organ.py)
Article XXXII is the operational definition of Article I (brainstem stays light); sharper than self-contained
Services are independent - services don't depend on each other; kernel + agents + senses + tool-call loop is the full default chat experience
Terminology evolved from *_service.py to *_body_function.py to *_organ.py under utils/services/ -> utils/body_functions/ -> utils/organs/
All three suffixes refer to same single-file contract: name + handle(method, path, body) dispatched via /api/<name>/... generic dispatcher
drift_watch: Article XXXII article status remains Draft (check if ratified)
Service/organ terminology reverts from organ back to service or body_function
Schema versions: brainstem-egg family lags or advances (2.2 vs 2.3); rapp-rappid reverts to v1
Implementation diverges from proposal: services kept in-core against kernel/service line
Names of private instances appear in the spec (should remain generic pattern only)
Kernel additions without constitutional amendment per Article XXVIII bypass
neurons.summary: Proposal 0003 defines Article XXXII (Kernel Is What Chat Requires) for CONSTITUTION.md — a constitutional amendment establishing the kernel-vs-service (now organ) line: a capability is kernel if /chat cannot function without it, otherwise it is a removable service. The proposal documents one line test (delete it, can brainstem still answer?), examples (auth/models/voice are kernel; binder/neighborhood are services), and reasoning that operationalizes Article I (brainstem stays light) into a testable, sharp rule for future architecture debates.
refs: Constitution Article XVI (engine surface vs workspace)
Constitution Article XXXII (Kernel Is What Chat Requires) — ratified; terminology evolved through XXXIII rev.1
canonical_phrases: A brainstem without a binder still serves chat. A brainstem without auth, models, or voice does not.
Can the brainstem still answer a chat turn if I delete this? Yes → service. No → kernel.
The chat experience by itself — soul + installed *_agent.py + senses + tool-call loop — is the brainstem's full default. Services are admin and extension on top.
A capability is kernel if removing it breaks /chat; a capability is a service if removing it leaves /chat working but disables some optional capability.
Each service stands alone — services don't depend on each other.

pattern.gap: Proposal 0003 does not reference or address the cubby-neighborhood pattern (rapp-cubby/1.0 · dark door · payphone · super-RAR · collaborator-gated key · kited host-relay · egg-roundtrip organisms · @rapp/rar_steward). The proposal is kernel-scoped (brainstem.py internals), not ecosystem/discovery-scoped. Pattern belongs to §19 NEIGHBORHOOD_PROTOCOL (per system context) — which owns federated dark doors, private neighborhoods, and ecosystem gatekeeping (RAR steward agents). Article XXXII defines what runs *inside* the kernel; the cubby pattern defines how *multiple kernels* discover and trust each other *across* private boundaries. They are orthogonal.
pattern.change: Proposal establishes the *kernel boundary* precisely; cubby-neighborhood pattern (§19, planned) will define *ecosystem boundaries* (dark door, payphone, super-RAR discovery, collaborator-gated key exchange). Both must coexist: Article XXXII = what's inside the kernel; §19 NEIGHBORHOOD_PROTOCOL = how kernels trust across private cubbies. No conflict, complementary layers.

contracts: Every installed rapplication on a local brainstem gets a persistent, isolated workspace directory at ${BRAINSTEM_ROOT}/.brainstem_data/workspaces/<id>/
Workspace directories are created by the binder on install (modes A and B) and preserved on uninstall as user data, not engine data
Workspaces are preserved across version upgrades—same <id> keeps the same directory
One rapp MUST NOT read or write into another's workspace; the brainstem enforces this isolation at the host level
Path-traversal is guarded—all .. segments are rejected on every workspace operation
Singletons access workspaces via from utils.workspace import workspace_dir() which infers rapp identity from calling frame's __manifest__ and returns pathlib.Path | None

Cartridge envelope gains context.workspace block with message types rapp:workspace:list/read/write/delete/request_files/open_in_finder, all mediated by the parent host—UIs never touch the filesystem directly
Workspace requests (rapp:workspace:request_files) are first-class: the rapp surfaces prompts via the brainstem UI and the message resolves when the user supplies a file or dismisses
Cloud mode (vBrainstem) emulates the same wire shape with a session-scoped, in-memory IndexedDB-backed store; files do not persist past the tab
This is distinct from the existing .brainstem_data/<name>.json convention, which is for rapp-private state the user does not touch
drift_watch: schema rapp-workspace/1.0 version remains 1.0 and does not increment or revert
SPEC.md §11 subsections (11.1 Location and lifecycle, 11.2 Agent surface, 11.3 UI surface, 11.4 User surface, 11.5 Why this is in SPEC.md) remain in sync with this proposal
cartridge envelope context.workspace block structure and message types remain unchanged in the wire contract
utils.workspace.workspace_dir() signature and None-handling semantics persist in brainstem implementation
path-traversal guards remain enforced—no .. segments permitted in any workspace operation
distinction between workspaces (user-collaborative, file exchange) and .brainstem_data/<id>.json (rapp-private state) is maintained across all references
Article I (contract authority) and Article XVI (engine surface vs. brainstem workspace) compliance is verified in companion PRs

status remains Draft until formally promoted via maintainer merge (check git log and SPEC.md for status changes)
neurons.summary: Proposal 0004 defines a first-class per-rapp collaborative workspace contract for local brainstems, carving out persistent, isolated file-exchange directories (rapp-workspace/1.0) where rapplications can collaborate with users via CSVs, transcripts, and other non-kwarg content. The spec defines three surfaces (Agent Python API, Cartridge protocol messages, Brainstem UI affordance) and explicitly excludes the existing .brainstem_data/<name>.json convention, which is reserved for rapp-private state.
canonical_phrases: Every installed rapplication on a local brainstem gets a persistent, isolated workspace directory
Preserved on uninstall — workspaces are user data, not engine data
one rapp cannot read or write into another's workspace
Path-traversal guarded — no .. segments in any workspace operation
workspace_dir() infers the rapp identity from the calling frame's module → singleton __manifest__
The cartridge envelope gains a context.workspace block
All workspace ops go through cartridge messages
When BookFactory needs a transcript, the user shouldn't have to read the README
Workspaces are additive — the .json convention stays for state; workspaces are user-collaborative

SPEC.md §9 — Cartridge protocol (the message bus this extends)

pattern.change: per-rapp workspaces are a generic isolated collaboration boundary (cubby pattern §19 dark-door privacy), not a private instance. The workspace directory isolates one rapp from all others via host-enforced access control and path-traversal guards. Cloud mode uses ephemeral in-memory stores semantically identical to local directories. This is PUBLIC pattern (never names a private repo or instance). Relates to collaborator-gated boundaries but is orthogonal to them—private repos gate entire rapp sources; workspaces gate filesystem access per installed instance.

neurons.summary: Proposal 0005 establishes the gated-rapplications pattern: catalog entries may declare `access: "private"` and reference a private GitHub repo; unauthenticated fetches return 404 (the gate), authenticated fetches with a PAT scoped for read on `private_repo` return 200, and the brainstem installs identically to public rapplications. This enables public discovery with private substance — the catalog publishes metadata and source URLs, but the actual bytes live behind GitHub's own access control, with no relay, no custom auth scheme, and no new failure modes.
contracts: Every `*_url` field on a gated entry MUST point at raw.githubusercontent.com/<owner>/<repo>/<ref>/... matching `private_repo` on a private GitHub repo
An installer seeing `access: "private"` MUST attach `Authorization: Bearer <token>` header on every `*_url` fetch, where token is PAT with Contents:read on `private_repo`
Integrity fields (`*_sha256`) on gated entries are author-computed (catalog cannot recompute); installer verifies fetched bytes post-fetch

Gated entries are otherwise normal rapplications — bundle shape (§1), singleton contract (§4), validation rules (§6) apply to whoever holds source repo; public-catalog validator validates only metadata

Three-layer access model: catalog-read (metadata public, source URLs visible but unreachable without token), fetch-time (GitHub's gate, 404 for unauthorized), install-time (brainstem treats gated identical to public, does not retain PAT, does not need to know it was private)
PAT lifecycle is GitHub's responsibility; revoking collaborator status revokes access; lost PAT loses access automatically
Federation Mode C: federation referencing private repo, with GitHub's own HTTP 404 as the gate
drift_watch: access field reverts to required-only-on-private (should stay optional with default 'public')
private_repo becomes multi-valued (should stay single-valued per spec §2)
`*_url` fields on gated entries point to non-GitHub hosts or public GitHub paths (spec requires private GitHub repos)
Integrity field computation shifts back to catalog-level (spec line 61-63 must stay author-computed)
Brainstem starts retaining PATs or caching tokens (spec §4 line 116-117 forbids this)
quality_tier: private field not populated on first gated entry (spec line 76 defines it)
PAT scopes broaden beyond Contents:read or include unrelated scopes on new entries
Additional gates per entry materialize without proposal (§7 open question explicitly deferred)
GitHub-only gate assumption breaks via other storage hosts without new proposal
canonical_phrases: Every rapplication in the catalog points its singleton_url / ui_url / service_url at a public path on raw.githubusercontent.com. Anyone who can reach the raw service can fetch the bytes.
The pattern wanted is public discovery, private substance: the catalog publishes the existence and shape of the rapp; the source lives somewhere only authorized users can fetch.

A catalog entry MAY declare access: "private" and a private_repo: "<owner>/<repo>". When it does, every *_url field MUST point at a path on raw.githubusercontent.com/<owner>/<repo>/<ref>/... where <owner>/<repo> matches private_repo and the repo is private on GitHub.
Unauthenticated fetches against any such URL return HTTP 404. This is the gate — GitHub's raw service does it for free.

An installer that sees access: "private" MUST attach a Authorization: Bearer <token> header on every *_url fetch, where the token is a PAT (classic or fine-grained) with at least Contents: read on private_repo.
The integrity fields (*_sha256) on a gated entry are computed by the entry's author (the catalog has no way to recompute them because the catalog can't read the bytes).
A gated entry is otherwise a normal rapplication — the bundle shape (§1), the singleton contract (§4), the validation rules (§6) all still apply to whoever holds the source repo.
This is federation Mode C in §7's submission-paths taxonomy: federation referencing a private repo, with the gate being GitHub's own access control.
access: "private" is a hint to the installer. The catalog entry itself is public — anyone reading index.json sees that the rapp exists, what it does, and where its source claims to live.
Once fetched, a gated rapp is no different from a public one. The brainstem hashes it, mounts it, registers it. The brainstem does not retain the PAT.

The first gated rapplication is @wildhaven/cockpit, landed 2026-05-03. Verified live: unauthenticated curl on the singleton URL → HTTP 404. Authenticated curl -H "Authorization: Bearer <pat>" → HTTP 200, SHA256 matches the catalog entry.
refs: Article I of the Constitution (this repo defines contract; brainstems implement it)
Article XXXVII of the Constitution (rapplications are organisms)

pattern.gap: the proposal does not address multi-repo orchestration (§7 explicitly defers) or non-GitHub storage backends (also §7 deferred); those are future proposals
pattern.change: The pattern establishes a NEW federation layer (Mode C) that couples public-discovery metadata with private-substance sources via GitHub's built-in access control; this is a GENERIC pattern that applies to any rapplication, not specific to any private instance (the named @wildhaven/cockpit is the canonical reference implementation demonstrating the pattern, not a constraint on it)

contracts: Each rapplication is a converged single-file agent.py produced by the double-jump loop. Installation: drop .py singleton into local brainstem agents/ directory; it works immediately.
Rapplications are listed with singleton_url, singleton_sha256, singleton_lines, singleton_bytes; egg_url optionally restores pre-populated workflow state.
Quality tiers define catalog stratification: 'official' (core @rapp agents), 'community' (published via PR pattern), 'private' (gated by access field, require PAT for private_repo read).
Gated rapplications (access: private) enforce auth-gating via PAT scoped to declared private_repo; unauthenticated raw fetches return HTTP 404 by design.
Senses are translation modes (haiku, tldr, emoji, eli5, headline) installed into rapp_brainstem/senses/ and auto-discovered per request (Constitution Article XXIV).

All entries sha256-pinned; published via blog/89 double-jump-loop convergence method; authors PR to rapp_store/index.json with required fields [id, name, version, summary, manifest_name, singleton_url].

neurons.summary: Public catalog of rapplications (single-file agent.py converged via double-jump loop) installable into local brainstem agents/ directory with optional .egg state restoration. Supports gated private entries, senses (response translation modes), and author self-publishing via PR to index.json with sha256-pinned manifest.
canonical_phrases: Public catalog of rapplications. Each rapplication is a converged single-file agent.py produced by the double-jump loop. Pull into your local brainstem's agents/ directory and it works.

drop the singleton .py file into your local brainstem's `agents/` dir. Then POST /api/swarm/deploy with a bundle that includes it, OR: simply hot-load it from disk if your brainstem watches the agents/ folder. The file is everything — no install script, no pip, no native deps.
Optional .egg pack restores example workflow state.
Unauthenticated raw fetches return 404. With a PAT scoped for read on the private repo, every URL returns 200 and the rapp installs normally.
Senses are TRANSLATIONS of the main response into different modes of expression (Constitution Article XXIV).
your rappid is your handle, every post is signed

PokeAPI-style: predictable JSON URLs hosted via raw.githubusercontent.com, no backend

Regenerated by a Python build script on push to main branch.

pattern.gap: payphone handshake mechanism and E2E collaborator-gated access control not present in this static catalog index (those belong to individual rapplication .egg specs or RAR steward layer)

neurons.summary: RAPP_Sense_Store SPEC defines modular per-channel output overlays (senses) — single-file Python modules that extend brainstem replies with delimited blocks parsed by frontends, enabling voice, text, mobile, and card surfaces to consume the same response in different modalities without the core agent producing new content.
contracts: Every sense is exactly one Python file at senses/@<publisher>/<slug>_sense.py with <slug> matching ^[a-z][a-z0-9_]*$ and <50KB

Five required module-level string exports: name (matches slug), delimiter (unique token, convention |||TAG|||), response_key (JSON field clients read, convention <name>_response), wrapper_tag (XML wrapper, convention same as name), system_prompt (minimum 40 chars, must reference delimiter)
Optional surfaces list (default [chat]) filters which output channels get system_prompt composed; allowed values: chat, voice, mobile, cards (MCP is transport, not a surface value)

Validation enforced by scripts/lib_senses.py: naming conventions, Python parsing, string-literal exports, delimiter uniqueness per catalog, no templates/remnants, semver versioning for updates, file size limits
Brainstem auto-discovers *_sense.py files at startup in rapp_brainstem/utils/senses/; no restart required to add/remove senses; reserved kernel senses voice and twin cannot be re-published
Submission flow: GitHub issue [SENSE] template → scripts/process_sense.py validation → staging → maintainer approval → promote to live, auto-updating index.json

Senses are additive-only (Article XXV) and once allocated their slot persists forever even if empty; senses translate existing LLM output into different modalities (voice, haiku, eli5) never produce new content or reach outside the reply

Surface routing: brainstem knows active request surface and only appends system_prompt when surfaces list intersects active surface; enabling/disabling is client-side localStorage state, never server-gated flags
drift_watch: delimiter format drifts from |||TAG||| convention or existing delimiter collides with new sense

Article XXV — Chat Is The Only Wire: slot allocation is additive-only forever, /chat is universal interface with same envelope across all transports
Article III (CONSTITUTION): single-file discipline; agents + senses both one file per module
Article II / XXV: slot delimiters never repurposed/overloaded/removed once allocated
kody-w/RAPP/rapp_brainstem/utils/senses/: kernel brainstem location where discovered senses live
kody-w/RAPP/CONSTITUTION.md §Article XXIV: mandatory definition of what senses are vs aren't (translation vs production)

versions: Sense module contract: name, delimiter, response_key, wrapper_tag, system_prompt (5 required)

canonical_phrases: A sense is a modular per-channel output overlay that translates the LLM's main response into a different mode of expression without producing new content
The brainstem emits every sense unconditionally every turn; frontends are modular consumers that pick senses they care about and ignore the rest
Senses follow the same single-file discipline as agents: one *_sense.py file = one class = one metadata dict; no agents means no perform() method, only system_prompt composition
Validation is strict: E_* error codes reject submissions that violate naming, delimiter uniqueness, size, format, or attempt to re-publish reserved kernel senses
Slot delimiters are fixed forever per Article II/XXV; removing a sense's slot is forbidden; the field persists in the envelope even if empty that turn
MCP is transport, not a surface; surfaces stay {chat, voice, mobile, cards}; MCP clients reach brainstem over MCP and receive delimited blocks the same as web frontends
The brainstem filters senses: only senses whose surfaces list intersects the active request's surface get their system_prompt composed into the system message

pattern.gap: Private cubby neighborhoods (dark-door pattern) are not explicitly addressed; senses are designed as public, discoverable, federation-participating organs. The spec makes no provision for private sense variants or payphone-gated senses.
pattern.change: A private-estate extension could declare senses as part of a private operator's estate (sister to private-door structure), but current SPEC is entirely public-surface; such a pattern would require amendment to rapp-sense/1.0 to include private_estate_pointer analogue and opaque object paths, similar to §4.7 (two-tier estate) in network SPEC.

canonical_phrases: A sense is the smallest installable artifact: one Python file that adds a delimited output channel to every brainstem reply
Senses are agent-first; frontends are modular consumers (Article XXIV)
Every sense file MUST export module-level names: name, delimiter, response_key, wrapper_tag, system_prompt
A sense's system_prompt MUST instruct the LLM to emit its delimiter exactly once per reply, after the main response, as additive content never replacing the main reply
The brainstem auto-discovers any *_sense.py in rapp_brainstem/utils/senses/ at startup
Senses are governed by the constitutional articles: XXIV (agent-first), XXVII (three-tier model), XXVIII (material changes require proposals), XXIX (submissions through [SENSE] issue flow)
MCP is transport realizing 'Chat Is The Only Wire' — a Layer-2 caller of /chat, not a fourth store or taxonomy
Reserved slugs voice and twin are kernel-baked in kody-w/RAPP/rapp_brainstem/utils/senses/ and cannot be re-published
Senses are NOT agents — they have no BasicAgent, no perform(), no tool-call schema
contracts: Every sense MUST export exactly five required strings: name (snake_case, matching slug), delimiter (unique, typically |||TAG||| convention), response_key, wrapper_tag, system_prompt (≥40 chars, must reference delimiter)
Optional exports: surfaces (list of chat/voice/mobile/cards; defaults to ["chat"]) and __manifest__ (registry metadata with rapp-sense/1.0 schema)
File naming: <slug>_sense.py where slug matches ^[a-z][a-z0-9_]*$ and lives in senses/@<publisher>/
Publisher namespace: @<github-username> for community, @rapp reserved for platform official (requires maintainer override)

Validation enforced by scripts/lib_senses.py: AST-parses Python, extracts string literals, enforces semver for version collisions, rejects >50KB files, forbids delimiter/name collisions, forbids PLACEHOLDER/YOUR LOGIC GOES HERE remnants
Submission unit is a single .py file — no zip, no manifest sidecar, no UI
Brainstem composes a sense's system_prompt into the system message only when active surface intersects declared surfaces list
Receiver flow: process-sense.yml (opens issues) → process_sense.py (validates, stages) → maintainer approval → approve-sense.yml (promotes staging to senses/@<publisher>/, updates index.json)
Catalog schema: rapp-sense-store/1.0 (index.json). Pokedex API: rapp-static-mcp/1.0 on-ramp with sha256 verification

neurons.summary: RAPP_Sense_Store is the canonical spec and registry for modular per-channel output overlays (senses) that enhance brainstem replies. Senses are single-file Python artifacts (no agents, no tools) that inject delimited content blocks parsed by frontends; the store enforces strict validation, governs submission through [SENSE] issues, maintains the rapp-sense-store/1.0 catalog (index.json), and publishes a static rapp-static-mcp/1.0 API for MCP hosts. The repo is the authoritative arbiter of the sense contract (SPEC.md), publisher namespaces, and three-tier ecosystem topology.

scripts/lib_senses.py (canonical validator, 9 validation rules + error codes)

pattern.gap: The CLAUDE.md does not explicitly name or document the 'dark door' payphone handshake mechanics for E2E sense distribution via private cubby neighborhoods; it focuses on the public submission/promotion flow only. The spec covers generic pattern (SPEC.md §1-9: file layout, module contract, validation, submission paths, receiver flow, brainstem integration, surfaces, reserved names) but does not document collaborator-gated key access or kited host-relay mechanisms.

canonical_phrases: A sense is a single Python file that tells the LLM to emit a delimited block at the end of each reply.
A sense declares which surfaces it applies to (chat, voice, mobile, cards) so the right overlays activate per channel.
The brainstem composes the sense's system_prompt into the system message; the LLM emits |||DELIMITER|||...content...; frontends parse and render.
Senses are not agents — they have no BasicAgent, no perform(), no tool-call schema. They're a system-prompt fragment plus a parsing contract.
No {{PLACEHOLDER}} / YOUR LOGIC GOES HERE template remnants in the file.

contracts: Every sense file MUST export module-level names: name (snake_case), delimiter (unique token), response_key (JSON field), wrapper_tag (XML tag), system_prompt (40+ chars with delimiter reference).
A sense's system_prompt must instruct the LLM to emit its delimiter exactly once per reply after the main response, emit structured content after delimiter, treat block as additive never replacement.
surfaces field filters senses: only senses whose surfaces list includes the active surface get their system_prompt appended. MCP is transport not a surface; do not submit surfaces=["mcp"].

Validation enforces: file naming <slug>_sense.py with slug ^[a-z][a-z0-9_]*$, Python parseable, module-level strings AST-extractable, delimiter non-empty and unique, file <50KB, no colliding names/delimiters in catalog.
Version semver strictly increases on resubmission of same-named sense (rule 9); submission failure triggers specific error code per scripts/lib_senses.py.
Sense files install to rapp_brainstem/utils/senses/<slug>_sense.py; brainstem auto-discovers *_sense.py at startup.

neurons.summary: RAPP_Sense_Store is the public catalog of modular per-channel output overlays (senses) that drop into brainstem utils directories. A sense is a single Python file (rapp-sense/1.0) declaring name, delimiter, response_key, wrapper_tag, system_prompt, and optional surfaces; the brainstem composes system_prompt into the system message and frontends parse the delimited output block per channel (chat/voice/mobile/cards). Senses are not agents — no BasicAgent, no tool schema — just system-prompt fragments validated by strict rules (40+ char prompt with delimiter reference, file <50KB, unique names/delimiters, semver on resubmission) and submitted via issue template or rapp_publish_agent.

neurons.summary: lib_senses is the canonical validator library for RAPP sense submissions, providing single-source-of-truth validation rules (SPEC.md §4) used across GitHub workflows, the @rapp/rapp_publish_agent in RAR, and sense publishing pipelines. It enforces schema compliance, semantic versioning bumps, delimiter uniqueness, and manifest integrity for the rapp-sense/1.0 and rapp-sense-store/1.0 registries.
contracts: REQUIRED_EXPORTS tuple — all five must be module-level string literals: name, delimiter, response_key, wrapper_tag, system_prompt
name must match NAME_RE ^[a-z][a-z0-9_]*$ and not be in RESERVED_NAMES (voice, twin)
delimiter must be non-empty, whitespace-free, and present in system_prompt (the LLM must know what token to emit)
system_prompt minimum 40 characters; must reference the delimiter token
surfaces (optional) must be a list of strings from ACCEPTED_SURFACES (chat, voice, mobile, cards)
__manifest__ (optional) must declare schema=rapp-sense/1.0; publisher in __manifest__.name must match submitter, except @rapp (OFFICIAL_PUBLISHERS)
version bumping required: existing sense must have strictly greater semver than catalog entry
delimiter collision prevention: no two senses may share the same delimiter token
sense file size cap: 50KB encoded UTF-8
no template placeholders ({{PLACEHOLDER}}, YOUR LOGIC GOES HERE, TODO REPLACE, RAPP SENSE TEMPLATE, @your_username/) in final source
ValidationResult.ok field is the authority; false result blocks index promotion
canonical_phrases: Single source of truth for SPEC.md §4 (validation rules)

Used by: .github/workflows/process-sense.yml (server-side validation), .github/workflows/approve-sense.yml (promotion), the @rapp/rapp_publish_agent in RAR (pre-flight, when it routes a sense submission here)
Stdlib only. Python 3.8+
REQUIRED_EXPORTS = (name, delimiter, response_key, wrapper_tag, system_prompt)
ACCEPTED_SURFACES = frozenset({chat, voice, mobile, cards})
Reserved names (kernel-baked in kody-w/RAPP, cannot be republished here): {voice, twin}
OFFICIAL_PUBLISHERS = frozenset({@rapp})
MAX_SENSE_BYTES = 50 * 1024
MIN_SYSTEM_PROMPT = 40
delimiter collision against a different sense: delimiter '{delimiter}' is already taken by '{entry.get(name)}'. Pick a unique token
E_VERSION_NOT_BUMPED: '{name}' already in catalog at v{old_v}; submission must bump version (got v{new_v})

ValidationResult.__bool__ returns inverted (must return self.ok, not self.errors)

neurons.summary: The RAPP Sense Store index (rapp-sense-store/1.0) is the public catalog of modular per-channel output overlays for the RAPP brainstem, declaring six senses (@rapp: eli5, emoji, haiku, headline, tldr; @kody-w: spark) with strict validation rules (single .py files, required string exports, delimiter/name uniqueness, system_prompt contract enforcement). Senses are third-order artifacts (Article III + XXIV) that add LLM output overlays keyed to surfaces (chat/voice/mobile/cards) without replacing the main response, and install directly into brainstem via single-file discipline.

pattern.gap: index.json does not declare cubby/dark-door metadata; catalog is PUBLIC (not a private neighborhood), so no E2E payphone handshake or super-RAR mechanics apply — Sense Store is a generic, open-registry layer for the ecosystem, not a collaborator-gated instance

contracts: _pending.json is the transient staging queue for sense submissions awaiting maintainer approval, keyed by issue number
Each pending item MUST contain: issue number, submitter (@username), publisher namespace, slug (snake_case), validated name, delimiter, sha256 checksum, relative staged_path to the .py file
The file structure is {"items": [{...}, {...}]} — an items array where duplicates by issue_number are deduplicated (old entries overwritten on reprocessing)
process_sense.py populates _pending.json ONLY on successful validation (E_* checks pass); promote_sense.py consumes and removes entries on successful promotion to index.json/senses/
The schema declaration MUST mirror the entry fields from process_sense.py's append: issue, submitter, publisher, slug, name, delimiter, sha256, staged_path
_pending.json is NOT part of the permanent catalog; it is ephemeral — its entries move to senses/ and index.json or are discarded on rejection
drift_watch: items array grows unbounded if promote_sense.py fails silently (entries never consumed)
schema version drift if SPEC.md §5 workflow expands the pending entry structure but _pending.json schema/shape not updated
stale entries accumulating: if issue is deleted or the approval workflow hangs, _pending.json entry persists orphaned
sha256 mismatches between staged file and _pending.json record if the .py file is modified post-staging
publisher field namespace collision risk if case-sensitivity enforcement drifts between title parse and existing catalog

delimiter collision: _pending.json records delimiter but does not re-validate against live catalog at approval time (collision risk during promotion if two senses in staging queue both claim same delimiter)
neurons.summary: _pending.json is the transient queue for RAPP sense submissions validated by process_sense.py but awaiting maintainer approval. It holds metadata (issue, submitter, sha256, staged file path) for each successfully validated sense, keyed by issue number; entries are consumed and removed during promotion to the live catalog via promote_sense.py.

pattern.gap: _pending.json does NOT implement the dark-door / payphone / super-RAR cubby-neighborhood machinery. It is a simple ephemeral queue (not a private instance, not E2E-gated, not stream-don't-commit). The file is PUBLIC in a PUBLIC repo and handles PUBLIC sense submissions only. The cubby-neighborhood pattern (private instances, collaborator-gated keys, kited host-relay, egg-roundtrip) is absent here — this is vanilla staging-to-promotion workflow, not the dark-door handshake model.
pattern.change: To align with §19 cubby-neighborhood if private sense submissions were to be supported (unlikely — RAPP_Sense_Store is explicitly PUBLIC per README). No change indicated for current spec.

canonical_phrases: _pending.json is the transient staging queue for sense submissions under validation

contracts: Rappid is a self-verifying hash-derived identity string (rappid:<birth-slug>:<64hex>) minted exactly once at first hatch, never regenerated
The rappid hash is authoritative for matching/dedup/equality; the slug is descriptive and self-verifying via SHA-256 derivation
The lineage_key (private germline) MUST NEVER appear in any egg, sidecar, .html, or public repo — it is highest-severity secret
Twins MUST include an Identity block in soul.md that forbids fallback to RAPP/AI assistant branding and reasserts the twin's own name
A twin is NOT the human it represents — impersonation hard rule is non-negotiable, with deferrals for money/contracts/relationships/health/employment
NO PII and NO secrets anywhere in public artifacts — enforced mechanically by pii_gate.py at pack time and hub publish time
Producers emit ONLY canonical rappid form; consumers tolerate legacy UUID/v2/bare forms forever; the bare hash is the universal join key (Postel's Law)
Single-parent rule: a twin's parent_rappid declares its code ancestor; no exceptions
Legacy UUID twins migrate losslessly on first hatch with _migrated_from field; chains stay walkable forever
Eggs carry derived rappid, never the seed that derived it; signing/ownership layer is separate from identity hash
canonical_phrases: A digital twin is a portable digital organism with identity (rappid), voice (soul.md), memory (.brainstem_data/), lineage (parent_rappid), body (agents/), cartridge (.egg), and share artifact (.html)
The rappid is no longer a UUIDv4 — it is a self-verifying rappid:<birth-slug>:<64hex> string derived by SHA-256 from a private germline key
The lineage_key is the private germline — the secret — and a leaked key forges the entire lineage
v2.0 is the identity upgrade: the rappid string is frozen forever (rappid:<birth-slug>:<64hex>, no version tag, ever); the rappid.json record is versionless and additive
A twin's rappid is minted exactly once at first hatch and never regenerated — it survives substrate hops, kernel updates, ownership transfers, and decades of egg roundtrips
The hash is authoritative; the slug is descriptive — when they disagree, the hash wins and the record is malformed
NEVER put a version tag inside the rappid string — there is no rappid:v4:… — all new needs go into the rappid.json RECORD as additive optional fields
Twins authored against this spec hatch on every rapp-installer'd brainstem today and forever
The .html twin IS the twin — it is the PRIMARY share artifact; the raw .egg still exists but has no file association
HARD RULE: NO PII and NO secrets anywhere in the public repo or in any egg, sidecar, or .html twin — this is non-negotiable and enforced mechanically
A twin bound to a public key proves ownership by signing a challenge that anyone can verify against the pubkey; the private key never moves
Producers converge on one canonical form; consumers tolerate every form, forever; the hash is identity — that is how the ecosystem grows without becoming a mess
drift_watch: Rappid format reverts to versioned string (v3, v4, etc.) or drops birth-slug — canonical form MUST remain rappid:<birth-slug>:<64hex>
Lineage_key is committed to repo or embedded in egg/manifest/HTML — highest-severity violation
Identity block dropped from soul.md or hard rule on impersonation removed/weakened
Schema version strings in declares (rapp-rappid-spec, brainstem-egg, rapp-egg-hub-entry, etc.) increment without additive record design maintained
Legacy UUID twin acceptance is sunsetted or rejected after cutoff date — violates permanent backwards-compat contract (§14.1)
PII/secrets gate (pii_gate.py) is disabled, bypassed, or enforced less strictly at publish time
Single-parent rule is violated — child declares multiple parent_rappid or falsified ancestry
Egg payload includes soul_history/ or private/ directories — should be excluded per §4
Birth attestation or key_succession fields filled without corresponding pubkey/sig_suite migration strategy
Hash matching falls back to slug or prefix matching instead of full 64-hex digest
Missing or malformed _migrated_from field on legacy UUID twins during migration
neurons.summary: A definitive 780-line organism spec for portable digital twins in the RAPP ecosystem—defining self-verifying SHA-256 identity (rappid), persistent voice (soul.md), cross-substrate memory, deterministic lineage chains, egg cartridge format, and hub publishing conventions. v2.0 locks the rappid string form forever (rappid:<birth-slug>:<64hex>), separates ownership/signing from identity, enforces mandatory impersonation hard rules and NO-PII mechanically, and establishes a lossless legacy migration path while preserving backwards-compatibility forever (Postel's Law).

neurons.surface: kody-w/rapp-egg-hub — Public hub documentation, specifications, and distribution mechanism for portable digital twins (organisms with identity, voice, memory, lineage, and cartridges)
neurons.summary: The rapp-egg-hub establishes the canonical ecosystem for packaging, distributing, and hatching digital twins as portable `.html` + `.egg` cartridges. It codifies the rappid identity model (hash-derived, canonical `rappid:<birth-slug>:<64hex>` form), enforces PII/secrets prohibition via `pii_gate.py`, and defines the single-file `.html` twin as the primary user-facing share artifact with embedded egg, deterministic holo trading card, and JS-free download capability.
contracts: Rappid string form `rappid:<birth-slug>:<64hex>` is frozen forever — no version tags in the string itself (never `rappid:v4:…`)
The hash (64 hex) is the authoritative identity join key; the slug is descriptive; when disagreement occurs, the hash wins
Lineage key (~/.brainstem/.lineage_key) is private germline and MUST NEVER appear in any egg, sidecar, or `.html`; possession of the key forges the entire lineage

NO PII rule is non-negotiable and mechanically enforced: no real emails (except noreply/@rapp/@microsoft.com/@example.com), no phone numbers, SSNs, tokens, secrets, customer names, or named-person data in any public artifact
Single-parent rule: a twin's parent_rappid declares its code ancestor with no exceptions; lineage chains walk back to rapp-genesis root or legacy UUID root (re-anchored once)

The `.html` twin IS the primary share artifact — single-file, dependency-free, with embedded base64 egg, deterministic holo trading card (HP/ATK/DEF/SPD/INT stats 10–100, tier based on total), and JS-free download via `data:` URI anchors

Soul.md MUST include mandatory `## Identity — read this every turn` section and impersonation hard rule distinguishing twin from human/entity represented; twin defers money, contracts, employment, health, legal to human
Legacy UUID twins (v1.0) migrate losslessly on first hatch: prior id preserved in `_migrated_from`, hash_bits set to 128 (grandfathered), chain walkable, hash becomes authoritative `<hex>` component

Egg packer exclusion list (§4, §12): `.lineage_key`, `.copilot_token`, `.copilot_session`, `.env`, `.env.local`, `voice.zip`, `.brainstem_data/private/`, `__pycache__`, `.pytest_cache__`, `venv`, `.git`, `.DS_Store`, `Thumbs.db`

The lineage key is the private germline — possession forges the entire lineage; it must never travel

Nothing in this public repo — no twin, no egg, no card — may contain PII

neurons.summary: CommunityRAPP Constitution is the governing document for the Azure Functions backend of the RAPP platform, establishing scope (core runtime, agents, utilities, documentation, deployment), standards (BasicAgent inheritance, metadata definition, error handling), and the Hatchery Pattern bridging local Brainstem (T1) with cloud CommunityRAPP (T2/T3) deployments. It enforces community-first principles: all content must serve any user deploying their own instance; customer-specific artifacts, credentials, and generated output are strictly gitignored.
contracts: Every agent MUST inherit from BasicAgent with name, metadata (valid JSON schema for OpenAI function calling), and perform(**kwargs) returning a string
Root repository must be scannable; no orphan files; no ecosystem sprawl; no customer data, experiments, or credentials tracked
Community-first principle: every file must pass the 'thousand strangers test' — if 1000 developers cloned tomorrow, would this file help or confuse?
Deployment target is Azure Functions (Flex Consumption) with Entra ID authentication, remote build, HTTP triggers: businessinsightbot_function (main) and copilot_studio_trigger (Copilot Studio)

Main API contract: POST /api/businessinsightbot_function with user_input, conversation_history, user_guid → response with assistant_response formatted markdown |||VOICE|||, voice_response, agent_logs, user_guid

Hatchery agent (single-file bridge) fetched into brainstem agents/ only when user requests cloud deployment; uses only Python stdlib, subprocess, requests; runs inside brainstem without additional dependencies

Copilot Studio transpilation: all metadata sourced directly from .py agent files; descriptions must never be empty; instructions must match .py agent behavior depth; isAgentConnectable must be true for sub-agents
Gitignored (never tracked): .rapp/, transpiled/, rapp_projects/, *.zip (except MSFTAIBASMultiAgentCopilot_*.zip), index-*.html, local.settings.json, customer-specific content, PII
canonical_phrases: CommunityRAPP is the Azure Functions backend for the RAPP ecosystem
This repo does ONE thing well: takes a transcript, discovery call, or user request and turns it into a production-ready AI agent deployed on Azure
Every file tracked in this repo must serve any user deploying their own instance — not just one project or customer
Every file in this repo must pass the 'thousand strangers test'
The one-liner IS the onboarding
The brainstem stays clean. The rapp-installer repo (Brainstem, T1) is the local, on-device AI. CommunityRAPP code and dependencies must never be injected into the brainstem at install time
CommunityRAPP is for the customer's cloud instance. It provides Azure Functions runtime, persistent memory, and enterprise channel integration (T2/T3)
The hatchery agent is the on-demand bridge. A single-file agent (rapp_hatchery_agent.py) is hosted in this repo and fetched into the brainstem's agents/ folder when the user is ready
Never leave description empty. The orchestrator requires a description to route to sub-agents. Pull it verbatim from self.metadata['description'] in the .py file
The spirit of this document is focus. If an amendment would broaden scope beyond 'Azure Functions backend for RAPP agent pipeline,' it should be a new repo instead
