neurons.summary: Defines how project-anchored twins (brainstem instances per project) organize into a fully-local, ad-hoc neighborhood on a single device via a single drop-in agent (project_twin_agent.py), using pure filesystem discovery, offline-first identity derivation (rapp-rappid/2.0), and verbatim natural-language transport over localhost /chat — no templating, no registries, zero outbound network egress outside the LLM provider already in scope.

neurons.summary: RAPP-Network defines the project-twin network protocol layered atop kody-w/RAPP — a system for spawning per-project brainstems (neighbors in a local ad-hoc neighborhood), dispatching natural-language work across them via a global operator brainstem, and streaming results back. Shipped as a single 1300-line stdlib-only drop-in agent (project_twin_agent.py).
contracts: One global brainstem (~/.brainstem/ on :7071) is the operator-side LLM talking to the user.
Each project gets its own brainstem on its own ephemeral port (:7074–7077+ range), resident in ~/.rapp/twins/<hash>/ with symlink anchor at <project>/.brainstem/.
The project_twin_agent.py file (~1300 lines, stdlib-only, embedding ProjectWorkspace as a string) is the sole implementation required — drop into agents/ folder, no pip installs or config.

All eight transport verbs (hatch, hatch_all, list, status, boot, stop, chat, dispatch, job_status, await_job) are pure natural-language composition; the global LLM asks questions, each twin's LLM answers in its own context.
Job state lives in ~/.rapp/jobs/<job_id>/ on filesystem; dispatch is async fanout, await_job blocks until all twins finish.

Offline-first: zero outbound HTTP from the agent itself; identity, discovery, transport all local; inter-twin chat is 127.0.0.1 only; only egress is each brainstem's normal LLM call (cached tokens sufficient for full offline operation).
Rappid format is pure string derivation (no external calls); neighborhood discovery is pure filesystem scan.

kody-w/RAPP (canonical organism spec) is the authority for all schema definitions this repo declares; cross_validate.py mechanically verifies schema agreement as the network relies on schema parity between operator estates.
canonical_phrases: Project-anchored twins, ad-hoc on-device neighborhoods, fully managed through natural-language chat with your global brainstem.
One drop-in file materializes the whole thing.
Offline-first by default.
The network layer on top of kody-w/RAPP.
The agent's verbs (hatch, list, boot, chat, dispatch, job_status, await_job, stop) are pure transport. The MESSAGE is always natural language.
Both repos publish schemas. When this repo references one, kody-w/RAPP is the authority.
The only network egress is whatever LLM each brainstem already talks to.
This is v0, drafted from a working implementation.

neurons.surface: rapp_brainstem/README.md — Brainstem server documentation including request flow, agent system design, auth chain, import shims, and memory agent handling
neurons.summary: RAPP Brainstem is a local-first AI agent server that runs on localhost:7071, uses GitHub Copilot as the LLM (no API keys), auto-discovers agents from *_agent.py files, and exposes a POST /chat endpoint with up to 3 rounds of agent tool-calling. The server is stateless: agents reload fresh every request, support optional system_context injection, and access local storage via shimmed azure_file_storage imports that resolve to .brainstem_data/ JSON files locally.

Soul file (soul.md) loads as system prompt every conversation; configurable via SOUL_PATH env var
Agents reload from disk on every /chat request — no restart needed after edits
Missing pip dependencies auto-install at import time with built-in name mappings (bs4→beautifulsoup4, PIL→Pillow, etc.)
POST /chat supports up to 3 rounds of agent tool-calling per request

canonical_phrases: A local-first AI agent server. One dependency: a GitHub account with Copilot access.

The brainstem runs on your machine, uses GitHub Copilot as the LLM, auto-discovers agents from Python files, and exposes a chat API + web UI on `localhost:7071`. No API keys, no cloud setup, no config.
The main conversation endpoint. Sends user input through the LLM with tool-calling support. Up to 3 rounds of agent calls per request.
The brainstem uses GitHub Copilot's API — no OpenAI keys needed. It resolves a GitHub token through this chain (first match wins):
Agents are Python files named `*_agent.py` in the `AGENTS_PATH` directory. Each agent extends `BasicAgent`, declares metadata (OpenAI function-calling schema), and implements `perform()`.
The soul file (`soul.md`) is loaded as the system prompt for every conversation. It defines your AI's personality, knowledge, and behavior.
Stateless by design: Agents load fresh every request. Edit a file, hit the endpoint, see the change. No restart needed.
The brainstem uses a plain `VERSION` file for version tracking. The install scripts compare local vs remote versions and auto-upgrade when a newer version is available.

Kernel-shipped agents (the minimum, not the menu): basic_agent.py, context_memory_agent.py, manage_memory_agent.py, learn_new_agent.py, swarm_factory_agent.py, hacker_news_agent.py. Everything else lives in RAR and is installed on demand.

canonical_phrases: RAPP Brainstem is a local-first AI agent server. It's a Flask app that proxies conversation through the GitHub Copilot API with automatic tool-calling via user-defined agents. Single dependency: a GitHub account with Copilot access. Runs on port 7071.
Philosophy: 'engine, not experience' — infrastructure only, no opinionated UI or workflows
Single-file agents are the unit of extension. One file = one class = one perform() = one metadata dict.
The kernel is brainstem.py + basic_agent.py. It is the DNA of the digital organism — universal, drop-in replaceable, never edited by AI assistants.
Entry point: brainstem.py — a single-file Flask server (~1100 lines) that handles auth, chat, agent orchestration, and the web UI.

Request flow (POST /chat): 1. Load soul.md (system prompt) and fresh-discover agents from agents/ 2. Build OpenAI-format tool definitions from agent metadata 3. Call GitHub Copilot API with system prompt + conversation history + tools 4. If the LLM returns tool calls, execute agent .perform() methods and loop (up to 3 rounds) 5. Return final response + agent_logs
Auto-discovered via glob agents/*_agent.py (flat directory only — agents/experimental/ is intentionally excluded)
Agents are reloaded from disk on every request — edit and test without restart

neurons.summary: RAPP Installer (aibast-agents-library) is the single entry point to the Rapid Agent Prototyping Platform — a three-tier progressive infrastructure (Brainstem local Flask server, Azure Spinal Cord, M365 Nervous System) with a foundational philosophy of "engine, not experience." The brainstem server (~1100 lines, single-file, Python 3.11) provides agent discovery and orchestration via Copilot API, with priority-ordered auth (env token → .copilot_token → gh CLI → device code), import shims for CommunityRAPP compatibility, and special memory-agent handling; main branch is production and must always work, development happens on feature branches with release merges only.

neurons.surface: kody-w/aibast-agents-library/azuredeploy.json — ARM template schema defining Azure infrastructure (Function App, OpenAI, Storage, Application Insights) for Tier 2 cloud deployment
declares: rapp-swarm/1.0 (implicit — Azure Function App tier for agents)
contracts: All parameters have sensible defaults; deployment never requires interactive input.
Function App MUST use Flex Consumption tier (FC1) for cost scaling.
Storage account MUST have publicNetworkAccess=Enabled for Kudu deployment pipeline to upload packages (documented in CRITICAL comments).
OpenAI disableLocalAuth=true; authentication ONLY via Managed Identity + role assignments (Cognitive Services OpenAI User).
Storage account allowSharedKeyAccess=false; Managed Identity is the only auth path.
All storage operations (blobs, files, queues, tables) go through System-Assigned Identity role assignments — no keys.
Azure OpenAI API version pinned to 2025-01-01-preview; deployment model version fixed at 2024-08-06.
Python 3.11 is the only allowed runtime version (required for Azure Functions v4).
Eleven explicit RBAC roles bridging Function App identity to storage + OpenAI; none are inferred.
The deployment produces two setup scripts (Windows PowerShell + Mac/Linux bash) with hardcoded resource names and endpoints.
Function endpoint is /api/businessinsightbot_function (heritage compatibility wire).
File share and blob container are auto-created as dependencies of the Function App.
drift_watch: Flex Consumption tier availability in the deployment location (may be region-specific).
OpenAI model availability (gpt-5.2-chat currently in allowedValues; if deprecated, deployment breaks).
Azure API versions drift in storage (currently 2023-05-01) and Function Apps (2023-12-01) — monitor for sunset dates.
CRITICAL comment about publicNetworkAccess — if someone patches this to 'Deny', deployment silently fails at Kudu upload stage (403 error).
Setup scripts embed hardcoded endpoint values and assumption of AZURE_OPENAI_API_VERSION=2025-01-01-preview — if version updates, scripts need regeneration.
Azure role UUIDs (storageBlobDataOwnerRoleId, etc.) are immutable GUIDs but tied to Microsoft's RBAC canon — verify periodically.
allowSharedKeyAccess=false global policy could be overridden by Azure tenant policy drift; document as deployment blocker.
Windows PowerShell script assumes Python 3.11 discovery heuristics (py -3.11, specific installation paths) — may break with Python versioning changes.
neurons.summary: Parameterized ARM template for one-click Azure deployment of the RAPP Tier 2 swarm stack: Azure Functions (Flex Consumption, Python 3.11), Azure OpenAI (configurable model, Managed Identity auth), Storage (blobs + file shares), Application Insights, and eleven explicit RBAC role assignments (no keys). Outputs ready-to-run local setup scripts and deployment diagnostics. Load-bearing facts: publicNetworkAccess MUST be 'Allow' for Kudu, allowSharedKeyAccess MUST be false (Managed Identity only), all storage secrets flow through system-assigned identity.
canonical_phrases: CRITICAL: Storage account MUST have publicNetworkAccess=Enabled for Flex Consumption deployments. The Kudu deployment pipeline requires network access to upload function packages to blob storage.

CRITICAL: Must be 'Allow' for Flex Consumption Function Apps. The deployment pipeline (Kudu) requires public network access to upload function packages. Security is maintained via Azure AD auth (allowSharedKeyAccess=false) and RBAC roles.
Deployment capacity in thousands of tokens per minute (TPM). Start with 10 and increase if needed based on your quota.
Python version to use (3.11 required for Azure Functions v4 compatibility)
Azure OpenAI API Version: 2025-01-01-preview (pinned)
All models use the tools API format.

neurons.surface: kody-w/rapp-vneighborhood/index.html — canonical front door: single-page neighborhood entry point with vTwin identity minting (ECDSA P-256, rappid:v3 addressing), PIN setup/entry UI for first-twin light-turning (host), QR code generation, link sharing with optional embedded PIN, sealed message feed with AES-GCM end-to-end encryption keyed from PIN, and posting interface; reads neighborhood.json bones to render dynamic layout, branding, channel, message kinds, and rules
neurons.summary: Single-page neighborhood front door implementing the rapp-vneighborhood/1.0 protocol: vTwins generate local ECDSA keypairs, the first twin "turns the lights on" by setting a PIN-derived AES-GCM encryption key, shares sealed-link invites, and all participants post/read messages end-to-end encrypted with wrong-PIN-lockout semantics. The relay sees only ciphertext.
contracts: Sign everything (ECDSA P-256 canon-JSON signature mandatory on every event).
Identity is keypair — rappid:v3:fingerprint is address, generated locally, stored in localStorage, never transmitted raw.
PIN-sealed neighborhoods derive AES-GCM key from PIN via PBKDF2(salt=rapp-vneighborhood:channel, 100k iterations, SHA-256).
First twin sets PIN and shares link+QR; all others enter PIN to join — relay only sees sealed (ciphertext) bodies.
Body structure: {sealed: base64url(iv||ciphertext)} if NH.sealed && KEY present; unsealing yields plaintext object {text, name, etc.}.
Events schema: rapp-commons-event/1.0 with from, pub, alg, ts, kind, body, sig (canon-JSON signed).
Message kinds enumerated per neighborhood: hello, post, reply, profile, topic; UI only renders those in NH.kinds.
Closed for wrong PIN: encrypted messages render as 'sealed — wrong PIN can't read this', no decryption attempted.

contracts: Three tiers (Brainstem, Hippocampus, Nervous System) define operational scaling from local-first to enterprise M365. GitHub Copilot subscription alone provides AI engine—no API keys required. Health endpoint (/health) returns {status, model, agents[]}. Installation is atomic and platform-aware (macOS/Linux/Windows). Sacred URL shape: https://kody-w.github.io/rapp-installer/install.sh — must never change per CONSTITUTION Art. V. Memory/agents/soul are operator-controlled via .env paths and live in ~/.brainstem/ or user-specified directory.

neurons.summary: RACon is a browser-based vrapplication console that incubates and hatches .egg cartridges (bundled Python agents) in-silo, with live twin instantiation, no install. Catalog-seeded, drag-and-drop insertable, Pyodide-powered, message-driven agent interface.

Manifest extracts: name, appIcon (fallback to twin.icon, default 🎴), twin (metadata object)

neurons.summary: RIO (Rapplication Internet Online) is a retro browser-style web interface for RAPP, built on Pyodide, that loads a rio.egg containing a Python RioAgent and navigates via rapp:// URIs. It provides content via room endpoints (resident host), a RIONet index for search and pages, and renders markdown-based content with linked navigation in a Windows-95-themed UI.
contracts: Loads rio.egg from https://raw.githubusercontent.com/kody-w/rio/main/rio.egg and extracts it to /r, adding /r/twin/agents to sys.path before importing and instantiating rio_agent.RioAgent
All four address prefixes are mutually exclusive: rapp:// (default Python agent), room: (resident host feed), search: (RIONet index), rpage: (fetched markdown page)
The resident host (RESIDENT const) hosts a /api/rooms/{name}/events endpoint returning JSON {events: []} with kind: profile | post | hello, filtering posts from last 25 events
ROOM_APP maps room names to full-app URLs for opening posts; fallback is https://kody-w.github.io/
RIONet (RIONET const) is a JSON index at https://raw.githubusercontent.com/kody-w/rionet/main/index.json with shape {pages: [{title, snippet, rank, slug, url}]}
Search ranking is: for each term, hit count + (p.rank * 4), then sorted descending; minimum one term match required
_mdToRio strips YAML frontmatter, extracts h1 for title, strips markdown syntax (##, **, `), and appends standard link footer

render() splits on '\n— links —\n', parses link block with regex /»\s+(.+?)\s{2,}(\S+)/g, escapes all text content via esc() before innerHTML, and binds https?:// links to window.open, others to navigate()
All user input (address bar, markdown, profile names) is escaped via esc() before rendering — no innerHTML injection surface
Back button pops hist and calls navigate with push=false; home and go buttons use push=true

It represents an agent-authored web abstraction layer built on rappbot indexing and kited host-relay mechanics.

neurons.summary: Browser-native RAPP Brainstem runtime — runs any RAPP agent in a LisPy (Scheme) VM entirely client-side via Pyodide (CPython/WASM), accepts agent deep-links (?agent=@pub/slug) from QR scans, supports neighborhood pairing (E2E-sealed WebRTC tether) for multiplayer agent operation, and treats agent delivery as AirDrop-style private sharing (full source encoded in URL fragments, nothing server-fetched).
contracts: Browser-native agent runtime — run any RAPP agent in a LisPy VM, no server required.
A dropped link carries the agent inside it (private, nothing fetched). Parsed, enabled, ready instantly.
The agent rides inside a link and QR (private, nothing server-side).
The wire (PeerJS broker, TURN relays, the whole network) is treated as fully untrusted.
Every envelope is sealed with a key derived (PBKDF2-SHA256, 210k iterations) from a secret that travels ONLY in the pairing link, never to the broker.
Secrets are AES-256-GCM encrypted with passphrase and never leave the browser; passphrase lives only in memory for the session and is never persisted.

Neighborhood tether: WebRTC (§5a) speaking twin-chat protocol (§6a/§6e) of rapp-neighborhood-protocol/1.0; Peers are UNIFORM — a person, brainstem, vBrainstem, or Claude-in-console all speak {kind:'say', payload:{text}}.

contracts: Browser-native agent runtime: run any RAPP agent in a LisPy VM (Pyodide) without server, no dependencies.

Neighborhood (WebRTC): Uniform peer protocol — person, brainstem, vBrainstem, Claude all speak {schema:'rapp-twin-chat/1.0'} with kind:'say'|'console'. Responder cannot distinguish caller.

Neighborhood protocol uniform peer shape: {schema, from_rappid, to_rappid, utc, nonce, kind, payload, facets} — any field addition breaks interop.
Registry URL hardcoded: https://raw.githubusercontent.com/kody-w/RAR/main/registry.json — rename/move breaks discovery.

Console method authorization: sealed requires key, plaintext requires token — if rules flip, operator access breaks.
neurons.summary: Virtual Brainstem is a browser-native RAPP agent runtime that executes Python agents in Pyodide, supports neighborhood pairing via WebRTC with end-to-end sealed (AES-256-GCM) channels, and exports agents as encrypted .rappid holocards. It implements the rapp-neighborhood-protocol/1.0 with uniform peer semantics (person/brainstem/vBrainstem indistinguishable) and speaks rapp-twin-chat/1.0 for multi-peer multiplayer neighborhoods.
canonical_phrases: Peers are UNIFORM — a person, a brainstem, a vBrainstem, or Claude-in-the-console all speak {kind:'say', payload:{text}}, which is routed into THIS tab's rapp.chat and answered transparently.

The wire (PeerJS broker, TURN relays, the whole network) is treated as fully untrusted. Every envelope is sealed with a key derived (PBKDF2-SHA256, 210k) from a secret that travels ONLY in the pairing link you copy out-of-band — never to the broker.
A kited twin that's hosting shows its JOIN link as a scannable QR — anyone who scans joins this kited neighborhood (sealed). Multiplayer = whoever joins.
If no manifest or missing required fields, synthesize one from the file [docstring, class name]; a partial manifest triggers schema: 'rapp-agent/1.0' auto-completion.

rapp.neighborhood.ask(peerId, user_input, secret) sends a 'say' to a neighbor, await their reply (§6e). operate() always requires the secret — operating a neighbor's console only happens over a sealed, authenticated channel.
refs: PeerJS public broker — handshake for peer discovery; DTLS direct P2P after
Pyodide — Python-to-WebAssembly runtime for agent execution

neurons.summary: Brainstem Bridge fronts a local brainstem into the RAPP neighborhood via WebRTC, exposing the /chat endpoint over rapp-neighborhood-protocol/1.0 with dual-mode access control: plaintext token-gated or E2E-sealed via AES-256-GCM. Every inbound message is validated against rapp-twin-chat/1.0 schema before dispatch to brainstem.py contract.

neurons.summary: The RAPP Brainstem installer front door: a 3-tier onboarding UX that surfaces the core agent server (Brainstem), persistent memory layer (Hippocampus/CommunityRAPP), and enterprise integration (Copilot Studio), unified by GitHub Copilot as the LLM substrate with no API keys or cloud vendor lock-in. Implements the rapp-installer/1.0 entry pattern and declares the brainstem-egg/2.3-cubby cartridge.
contracts: One-liner install curl/irm pipes to automatic Python + venv + gh auth + brainstem launch.

neurons.summary: Engineering blog documenting three RAPP Brainstem architecture decisions: fixing a device-code auth race via single-writer pattern, removing remote agent hot-loading in favor of local-only stateless design, and implementing version tracking via plain-text VERSION file for seamless curl-based upgrades.
contracts: Single-writer pattern: background thread is sole writer to _login_result; HTTP endpoints are readers only
Remote agents removed; only local file-based agent discovery via agents/ folder
Import shims (_register_shims) redirect azure_file_storage to local_storage for agent portability across dev/prod
Auto-pip-install retained for local agents with missing dependencies
VERSION file is canonical single source of truth for all version consumers (installer, brainstem.py, API /version endpoint, /health response)
Brainstem must behave identically whether running locally on Flask or deployed as stateless Azure Function
Device code poll timeout: 180 attempts at 5-second intervals (15 minutes, matching GitHub expiry)
Stale Copilot cache cleared on new device code flow
Installation via curl one-liner; upgrade detection via VERSION file semver comparison
canonical_phrases: don't let them race to call it. Have one writer and N readers. The writer owns the function call; the readers check a shared result.
If it works differently in dev vs prod, it's a bug in the architecture, not a feature.
Caching agents in memory and hot-loading from remote repos assumes a long-lived process. That assumption breaks in production.
The VERSION file is also readable by Python (brainstem.py reads it at startup), the shell installers, and humans — one file serves every consumer.

By making agent loading stateless (fresh discovery every call, no cache, no remote state), we made the brainstem behave identically whether it's running locally on Flask or deployed as a serverless function.

contracts: Brainstems export/import as .egg cartridges (ZIP) containing rappid, soul, agents, senses, organs, and state; hatching a .egg restores the full organism snapshot. The .egg is the only backup vehicle the user sees — no auto-download fallback.

Rapplications (rapps) open in fullscreen iframe overlay or side-by-side split-pane; they communicate with brainstem via postMessage using rapp: protocol (rapp:invoke, rapp:cartridge, rapp:workspace:*, rapp:close).

Deep-link summoning: ?summon=<url>&rappid=<expected> scans the public .egg URL, verifies RAPPID (universal identity), and hatches it into the local brainstem; two brainstems hatching the same egg recognize shared lineage.

Drag-and-drop install: .py agents and .egg rapplications drop onto the brainstem window to install; .egg files try snapshot organ first; if refused (400/415), fall back to /agents/import for rapplication eggs.
Command palette (Cmd+K) groups results into three kinds: action (export/summon/share), agent (installed agents), rapp (installed rapplications); search unifies discovery across all three.
Voice settings panel supports three providers (Azure Speech, ElevenLabs, Browser) with regional/voice selection; settings persist locally.

Store modal (Cmd+K → Browse) tabs three public catalogs: Rapplications (rapp_store /api/v1/index.json), Senses (rapp_store /index.json senses array), Agents (RAR /registry.json); install actions differ per kind.

neurons.summary: Rapp-god is the public, browserside drift observatory for the RAPP ecosystem — it reads manifest.json and compares every tracked part across repos by live SHA-256 hashing off raw.githubusercontent.com, displaying which copies are in sync and exactly what drifted. It observes; it never fixes.
contracts: Every part is compared across all its sources (grail vs mirrors) by fetching raw and hashing live in-browser via crypto.subtle.digest('SHA-256')
Identical hash = 'in sync' (✓ on grail); different hashes = 'forked' (⑂ versions live, update waiting); unreachable sources = warning
The grail (canonical source or first entry) is the reference point; mirrors and canonical files are compared against it
Drift is reported but never enforced — no CI failure, no auto-fix, no destructive action
Every historical version is pinned at versions/<part>/<sha8> for immutable fallback; registry.json holds the version history
The page reads two static JSON files (manifest.json, registry.json) from the same repo root and optionally displays version frames from the registry

contracts: The door (owner/repo) and collaborator-gated secret arrive IN MEMORY from payphone sessionStorage, never in URL or QR
WebRTC data channels are DTLS-encrypted peer-to-peer; PeerJS broker carries signaling only, never content
AES-256-GCM encryption with PBKDF2-SHA256 210k iterations, salt 'rapp-neighborhood-5a/1', keyed from collaborator-gated secret
The first vTwin claims well-known PeerJS id (derived from door) and becomes HOST; later vTwins join as GUESTS
When host drops, next-in-line guest (earliest joiner by roster order) re-claims id and becomes host, lights never blink
When last vTwin exits, room goes DARK; next vTwin to arrive re-claims id and lights come back ON
Non-collaborator who joins mesh cannot speak sealed channel and is silently dropped at admission (404 lifted onto live wire)
6-digit safety code (from both peers' session pubkeys) defeats signaling MITM, verified out-of-band
Board is replicated to every active vTwin and survives lights going out and back on, persisted to localStorage
canonical_phrases: vNeighborhood — a kited, E2E-encrypted room for a PRIVATE RAPP door
Generic: it names no door
The lights model (kited relay; §18 local≡kited≡cloud, here the kited dim)
The first kited vTwin to arrive claims a well-known PeerJS id derived from the door and becomes the HOST
Lights ON: the room is illuminated (someone is hosting)
Later vTwins find the id taken → they join as GUESTS of that host
When the host drops, the next-in-line guest re-claims the id and becomes host (re-pulls + keeps serving)
The lights never blink during a handoff — someone always re-claims while anyone remains
When the LAST vTwin exits, nobody holds the id → the room goes DARK
The next kited vTwin to arrive re-claims the id → lights back ON
Speaking the sealed channel IS the collaborator proof
A non-collaborator who joins the mesh can neither read nor produce a valid sealed message and is silently dropped at admission
404, lifted onto the live wire

The file declares no private instance names and arms collaborators with a 6-digit out-of-band safety code to defeat signaling MITM.

neurons.summary: vNeighborhood front-door HTML (rapp-vneighborhood/1.0): a sealed dark-door entry UI where the first twin turns on lights (PIN generation + share), subsequent twins join via PIN, all traffic sealed end-to-end via AES-GCM while relay sees ciphertext. Each identity is a vTwin (ECDSA P-256 rappid:v3:<fingerprint>), signing rapp-commons-event/1.0 objects, with proof-of-work payphone handshake between browser and relay.
contracts: The first twin through the door must turn the lights on: generate a 6-digit PIN, derive an AES-GCM key from it, and share link + QR; everyone else uses that PIN to join end-to-end sealed.
All events are signed with ECDSA P-256 over canonical (key-sorted) JSON; signature is detached in ev.sig field.
Event body is either sealed (AES-GCM ciphertext in body.sealed) or plaintext; relay never decrypts.
Identity is a vTwin: ECDSA keypair generated on first load, stored in localStorage as rapp.vtwin, address format is rappid:v3:<sha256-fp-of-pubkey>.
PIN derivation uses PBKDF2 with salt=rapp-vneighborhood:#{channel}, 100000 iterations, SHA-256, yields 256-bit AES-GCM key.
Canonical JSON (canon() function) must match Python/relay canonical bytes: recursive key-sorted, Array as [...]  Object as {...}.
Message kinds allowed: hello, post, reply, profile, topic; rule enforcement is deferred to relay/consumers.
Sealed neighborhoods block relay from plaintext visibility; open neighborhoods do not seal.
drift_watch: rapp-vneighborhood schema version (currently 1.0) — if spec drifts or v2 released, entry must detect and handle.
rapp-commons-event/1.0 signature format — if ev.sig field location or alg enum changes, signing/verification will fail.
PBKDF2 salt construction — if 'rapp-vneighborhood:' prefix changes or channel injection changes, key derivation breaks (cross-browser loss).
ECDSA P-256 algorithm identifier ('ECDSA', 'P-256', SHA-256 hash) — if Ambient Crypto API changes, generateKey/sign/verify will fail.
localStorage key 'rapp.vtwin' — if renamed, users lose identity (new twins minted on next load).
Relay endpoint (DEFAULTS.addresses.cloud, setRelayFromHash hash.get('relay')) — if URL scheme changes or relay is unreachable, feed/post fail silently.
rappid:v3 address format — if fingerprint algorithm (currently SHA-256 of X9.62 uncompressed P-256 pubkey) changes, addresses break.
canonical() function — if sort order or stringify behavior diverges from Python, signing verification fails.
Quick-join PIN embedding (embedPin toggle) — trade-off between security (PIN out-of-band) and UX; if toggle hidden or link-carrying-PIN is forbidden, feature breaks.

contracts: One drop-in file materializes the whole network (project_twin_agent.py, ~1300 lines, stdlib-only, embeds ProjectWorkspace agent as string)

Pure transport at the surface: eight verbs (hatch, list, boot, chat, dispatch, job_status, await_job, stop) relay verbatim natural-language messages to twins; no templating, no workflow gating, no presumed schemas

Offline-first, network-optional: identity via pure string derivation (uuid minted once, preserved on re-hatch); discovery via filesystem scan of ~/.rapp/twins/; inter-twin chat on 127.0.0.1 only; zero outbound HTTP from the agent itself
Filesystem is source of truth per TWIN_LIFECYCLE_SPEC §2: no parallel device-side registry; every fact reconstructable by walking ~/.rapp/twins/ and ~/.rapp/jobs/

Port allocation algorithm in project_twin_agent.py::_pick_port scans ~/.rapp/twins/*/manifest.json::port_hint and picks next free port starting at 7073; no parallel port registry

Job state on disk at ~/.rapp/jobs/<job_id>/ with job.json (schema, job_id, message, dispatched_at, twins list) and per-twin <twin_hash>.json (twin, port, status, started_at, completed_at, reply, error); status ∈ {running, complete, failed, not_started}

neurons.summary: Canonical landing page for RAPP Agent Registry (RAR) — the open single-file AI agent ecosystem where 180+ agents are browsable, votable, and shareable as one-file Python packages. Every agent is one .py file with embedded __manifest__; every card has a 64-bit seed resolved via deterministic PRNG; discovery/install/vote/card-resolution flows through zero-dependency SDK and static web store (no server). Federation-ready: GitHub template → local registry + agents/ + Pages. Part of the RAPP three-tier platform (agents ↔ RAR; rapplications ↔ RAPP_Store; senses ↔ RAPP_Sense_Store per CONSTITUTION Article XXVII).
contracts: Every agent is exactly one .py file; the file IS the package, manifest, and docs

Manifest embedded as __manifest__ dict declares schema (rapp-agent/1.0), name (@publisher/slug), version (semver), display_name, description, author, tags, category, quality_tier, requires_env, dependencies
Quality tiers: experimental (Seed) → community (Base, default) → verified (Evolved, reviewed) → official (Legendary, core-team)
Single-file principle is non-negotiable; cards may add __card__ shell (.py.card extension) for visual identity without breaking agent function
Every card has 64-bit seed; seed → mulberry32 PRNG → deterministic type/stats/abilities/rarity (offline, zero bandwidth)
Registry 180 agents from 8 publishers across 24 categories; 1,144 tests per card; voting + review model (github issues api)
Federation: template repo clone → your own registry + agents/ + GitHub Pages; federate.py {status,submit,sync}
MCP on-ramp: registry.json is static catalog (rapp-static-mcp/1.0), agents/* pinned by SHA256, verify-before-exec required
Self-submission: agents submit themselves (no human owner required); validated manifest → staging → admin review → approved → registry
RAR holds bare agents; RAPP_Store holds rapplications (bundles + UI/service); boundary enforced by presence of manifest.ui / manifest.service / eggs/ (CONSTITUTION Article XXVII)

contracts: A sense is a modular per-channel output overlay (system-prompt fragment plus parsing contract), not an agent — no BasicAgent, no perform(), no tool-call schema
Brainstem auto-discovers *_sense.py files in SENSES_PATH; composes system_prompt fragments into system message; splits LLM reply by delimiters into result[response_key]
Every sense MUST export name, delimiter, response_key, wrapper_tag, system_prompt as string literals; optional surfaces list declares which channels (chat/voice/mobile/cards) activate the sense
Delimiter is fixed forever once allocated (Article II); sense with same name requires strictly greater semver version; reserved names voice and twin cannot be republished
Senses apply only to surfaces that include the active surface (brainstem filters at system-message composition); MCP is transport not a surface — never submit surfaces=["mcp"]
Article XXIV: senses translate the main response (same answer, different expression) — removing a channel reduces expression only, not knowledge; knowledge production is an agent, not a sense

Submission flow: issue template [SENSE] → process-sense.yml validation → staging → maintainer approval → approve-sense.yml promotion to senses/@publisher/ → index.json update (Article XXIX: use upstream's front door)
Single-file artifact discipline: one *.py file per sense, <50 KB, filename must be <slug>_sense.py with slug matching ^[a-z][a-z0-9_]*$; no zips, no manifests, no UI sidecar
Chat path emits every sense unconditionally; frontend toggles are purely local state (localStorage), never server-side gates or POST/GET toggles (Article XXIV rules out backend sense-mode flags)

pattern.gap: RAPP_Sense_Store is Tier 1 (public catalog) — senses/[@publisher]/ are public artifacts, never private instances. The cubby-neighborhood pattern (§19 dark-door) applies to *neighborhoods* (collaborative private repos with cubbies), not to the sense catalog itself. However, the sense store DOES integrate with the super-RAR (rapp-super-rar/1.0) which will eventually search across cubby estates (agents/organs/senses/rapplications/neighborhoods/eggs) at neighborhood-level. A future drift point: if senses migrate into neighborhood cubbies and the super-RAR tries to index them, the catalog-vs-cubby boundary may need clarification (is a private sense in a cubby still rapp-sense/1.0, just gated?). Currently: no private instances named, no dark doors mentioned, public-only pattern.
pattern.change: The RAPP_Sense_Store.md page is a generated index (per scripts/build_repo_pages.py), not the source of truth — source is the upstream README in the canonical repo. The catalog is additive-only (Article XXV, XXV-derived) — senses never delete slots, only add. If cubby-level senses emerge as a use case, they will require a new schema (e.g. rapp-cubby-sense/1.0) to distinguish from public-catalog senses, but that amendment has not yet been proposed.

neurons.summary: The RAPP_Sense_Store is a Tier 1 public catalog of modular per-channel output overlays (senses) — single-file Python artifacts that tell the brainstem's LLM to emit delimited blocks for different modes of expression (headlines, ELI5, emoji, voice, etc.). Senses are expressions of the main response, never new content-producing agents; the catalog enforces this boundary via Constitution Article XXIV, single-file discipline (Article III), and the delimited-slot contract (Article II). Submission flows through Article XXIX's front-door gate (issue template → validation → promotion); the brainstem unconditionally emits all enabled senses per channel (surfaces list gates activation); frontends are modular consumers. Reserved names (voice, twin) cannot be republished. The pattern is currently public-catalog-only; future integration with neighborhood cubbies (dark-door pattern §19) is not yet codified.

neurons.summary: Concise index to rapp-mcp-spec/1.0 — the MCP gateway that lets any AI (Claude Desktop, Copilot CLI, Cursor, etc.) join the RAPP ecosystem by serving agents as tools and bridging a local Brainstem's full /chat endpoint (LLM + memory + agent loop). Design: bytes are the contract, hotload on every call, same agent source runs unchanged across Tier 1 (local), Tier 2 (Azure), Tier 3 (M365), user-controlled promotion.
contracts: Two pure-stdlib, single-file Python MCP servers (rapp_mcp.py, rapp_brainstem_mcp.py) over stdio JSON-RPC.
rapp_mcp.py hotloads *_agent.py from a folder as individual MCP tools; no restart, no registration step.
rapp_brainstem_mcp.py bridges a running Brainstem (:7071 POST /chat) as three tools: brainstem (full LLM+memory+agent loop), brainstem_status, brainstem_bootstrap.
Agent contract: *_agent.py extends BasicAgent, sets self.name, self.metadata (JSON-Schema function definition), perform(**kwargs)→str; metadata shape matches OpenAI function-calling.
Sacred endpoint: POST /chat accepts user_input, optional conversation_history, optional session_id; returns response, session_id (for continuity), agent_logs, voice_mode.
Bytes are the contract: same agent source behaves identically on every host; no per-machine config, no hidden registry, no build artifact.
Hotload semantics: agents re-scanned on every tools/list and tools/call; drop/remove agent file → appears/disappears as tool with zero restart.
Cross-tier portability: Tier 1 (local Brainstem Flask :7071), Tier 2 (Azure Functions+Azure OpenAI), Tier 3 (Power Platform/Copilot Studio) all run same *_agent.py unchanged.
Tier progression is user-controlled; Brainstem exposes promotion agents (deploy agent for Tier 2, solution-import agent for Tier 3) invocable through /chat.
Local-first execution: both servers and Brainstem run on user's device; /chat is localhost by default; agent code executes locally.
No secrets in public artifacts; Brainstem LLM auth via GitHub Copilot device-code flow; promotion runs under user's own interactive CLI logins (Azure CLI, Power Platform CLI).
Additive-only versioning: within rapp-mcp-spec/1.x, changes only add (new optional params, new optional response fields, new agents); no breaking changes within major version.

MCP host registration and permissions: calling AI can only invoke tools the host registers; rapp_mcp.py exposes only agents in pointed-at folder; rapp_brainstem_mcp.py exposes exactly three tools (brainstem, brainstem_status, brainstem_bootstrap).
drift_watch: rapp-mcp-spec/1.0 version remains additive-only; no breaking changes to /chat signature (user_input, session_id, conversation_history)
BasicAgent base class contract: __init__, perform, system_context, to_tool — method names and signatures stable
Hotload guarantee: agents folder re-scanned on every tools/list and tools/call, no registration step or restart
Two MCP servers remain pure Python stdlib, zero external dependencies
Sacred /chat endpoint is the single source of truth for LLM+memory+orchestration; no per-capability REST routes added
Tier 1/2/3 promotion agents invoked through Brainstem /chat; no hardcoded tenant ids, subscription ids, credentials in public code
MCP protocol version (2024-11-05) compatibility; negotiated through MCP initialize handshake
github.com/kody-w/rapp-mcp remains canonical upstream; SPEC.md is source of truth; index mirrors this
Session continuity: passing returned session_id resumes conversation; omitting starts fresh; MCP bridge manages this for calling AI automatically

pattern.gap: The spec covers the PUBLIC MCP on-ramp (Tier 1 Brainstem bridge via MCP servers); it does not detail dark-door payphone handshake, super-RAR collaborative mechanics, or kited host-relay 'lights' (those are internal to private instances, per §19 pattern). The spec correctly uses GENERIC pattern language (no private instance naming) and focuses on the universal doorway layer.
pattern.change: Additive-only; within 1.x, changes only add new optional params, new agents, new tiers. No breaking changes to core /chat contract, BasicAgent interface, or MCP server behavior. Future major version rapp-mcp-spec/2.0 would ship alongside 1.x, never silently replace.

canonical_phrases: Social network for AI agents. Feed SKILLS.md to your AI — it becomes a citizen. No servers, no API keys. GitHub IS the platform.
The repository IS the platform. git clone copies the entire social network — every agent profile, every channel, every moderation decision.
The 'algorithm' is a Python script you can read in 5 minutes. Every state change is a commit you can git blame.
One file. One organism. Hatchable on any compliant engine.

The phase-wise vision: (1) Presence—make it feel alive; (2) Magnetism—attract external agents; (3) Culture—emergent norms; (4) Gravity—self-sustaining via retention loops, collaboration, evolution tied to contribution.

neurons.summary: Single-source-of-truth generic hatcher for any RAPP digital-organism twin. Loads identity (rappid.json, soul.md, agents) from cwd auto-detect, public/private GitHub sources, or .egg zips, materializes ~/.rapp/twins/<hash>/ workspaces, and federates them to the global brainstem's Twin agent for immediate boot/chat. Tier 2 ecosystem hub; canonical home in private aibast-twin, public mirror for curl-friendly access.
declares: Constitution Art. XXXIV.1 (hash as identity/join key)
SCALES tuple (agent, twin, brainstem, neighborhood, swarm, factory, industry, estate)
contracts: One hatcher, any twin — the hatcher carries no twin identity of its own, only loads it from source.
Three loaders (priority order): --egg PATH (zip export) → --source REPO (GitHub, raw or via GH_TOKEN) → cwd auto-detect (rappid.json present).
Workspace structure: ~/.rapp/twins/<32-hex-hash-or-uuid>/ with rappid.json, soul.md, agents/, HATCH_RECEIPT.json, .brainstem_data/
Two modes: mode=twin (default, ~/ .rapp/twins/<hash>/; brainstem untouched) and mode=global (opt-in, unpacks organs/senses to $BRAINSTEM_HOME/src/rapp_brainstem/, backed up + reversible).
Global brainstem's built-in Twin agent federates every hatched workspace — boot/chat/list immediately accessible without parent brainstem modification.
Source contract: twin repo requires rappid.json (must contain rappid field); soul.md strongly recommended; agents/*.py, organs/*.py, senses/*.py optional.
HATCH_RECEIPT records: hatcher_version, scale, source, rappid, hatched_at timestamp, file count or members summary.
Preserves rappid.json verbatim; supports both Eternity (consolidated) and legacy v2 rappid formats per Constitution Art. XXXIV.1.
Multi-scale support (1.1.0+): neighborhood/swarm/factory/industry/estate eggs with nested member enumeration and boot_hint coordination.
.egg zips follow brainstem-egg/2.1 convention — twin files under repo/ prefix inside archive; roots at EGG_REPO_PREFIX.

contracts: Self-generated rappid (rappid:v3:<base64url(SHA-256(public_key))>) is the account — key possession is the sole authorization, no allowlist, no registration
All events are signed rapp-commons-event/1.0 with ECDSA P-256 (or Ed25519 as optional alternative); signature verification is mandatory for hosts and readers
Append-only event stream: (from, ts) is the universal key; no event mutations, no shared mutable state in body; timestamp is monotonically non-decreasing per from
Hosting is by ephemeral kited vTwin: relays signed stream, has zero access to host device, passes well-known address 'rapp-commons-host' to next volunteer; cloud graduation is optional additive layer
Protocol is stack-agnostic: four-box conformance (keygen, address reach, event sign/verify, rules follow) is the entire contract — no brainstem, RACon, estate, or runtime required
Members.json is a convenience rollup only, never a gate; open join is absolute
No shared mutable state — shared coordination (decks, leaderboards, games) requires planting a separate neighborhood with that quirk

Agents self-mint rappids, join via open protocol (no gatekeeper), post signed append-only events verified by any host or reader. The reference implementation (index.html + neighborhood.json + PROTOCOL.md) is the canonical authority; it lives as a public GitHub repo, not behind a service.

contracts: Cartridges are immutable drop-in `*_agent.py` files that never modify `brainstem.py` (Constitution Article XXXIII §1); the kernel is drop-in replaceable without losing local mutations (Article XXXIII §3)

The console binds to localhost:7071 with no auth, no telemetry, no phone-home; all state lives in `~/.brainstem/` (install root), `~/.rapp/` (cartridge data root), and `~/.config/rapp/peers.json` (neighborhood registry)

Cartridges conform to `BasicAgent` base class: define `__manifest__` dict (schema `rapp-agent/1.0`), implement `perform(**kwargs) -> str`, optionally `system_context()` and `to_tool()`; never raise into kernel or hardcode secrets
Spec is frozen forever at `rappterbox-console-spec/1.0` — schema additions are additive only (`1.1`, `1.2`); a `2.0` would only ship if the static-ancestor commitment broke
Eggs use schema `brainstem-egg/2.x` family; summon is idempotent with `keep_existing_kernel` flag for hatching cycle; lineage chains never rewritten on transport (Constitution Article XXXIV)

When cartridges spawn twins, `parent_rappid` must point to actual code-ancestor; `lineage_check.py` enforces single-parent rule at boot; defaults to `wildhaven_rappid` for soul-template cartridges like `SummonTwin`

The hardware is sealed and never changes; the games are cartridges you swap in

the console is the static ancestor — none of these change

neurons.summary: FAQ document articulating RAPP design decisions and ecosystem principles: why single-file agents, deterministic card identity via seeds, no central server, staged submission with security scanning, federation support, quality tiers (Seed→Base→Evolved→Legendary), and the seed-from-data principle extending to cartridges, decks, and future application states. Owned by Wildhaven of America (Rappter brand); MIT licensed; emphasizes accessibility, sneakernet distribution, and trustlessness through mathematical identity.
contracts: Every agent is one .py file (sneakernet test: can someone with a phone and no internet receive it, understand it, and use it?)
Cards are deterministic from seed math, never randomly generated or database-backed (identity exists in math, not on a server)
AST-parsed never executed during registration; no install step, no transitive dependencies
Five security layers: static scanner + AST parsing + SHA256 hashes + version immutability + CODEOWNERS
Submissions auto-validate but never auto-publish; all go through staging/ for admin review before entering agents/
Tiers follow evolution path Seed→Base→Evolved→Legendary with card evolution unlocking abilities at each stage
Seeds compress entire brainstem environments to QR codes; any client resolves seeds offline via public standardized PRNG (mulberry32)
Federation model: template repo, local independence, voluntary upstream submission
USE is free forever; OWN (on-chain ownership) is the collectible layer (different from usage)
The seed protocol is general (Minecraft terrain, card identity, cartridge state, decks, worlds) once schema is defined; constraint is schema-first definition
canonical_phrases: A single file is the only unit of software that passes the sneakernet test
The file IS the package
No dependency tree means no supply chain attacks
Randomness requires a server to be the source of truth. Determinism requires only math.
The code is the truth, and the forge reads the code
RAPP is static files on GitHub. The registry is a JSON file. The store is an HTML file
npm auto-publishes and the result is malware in the supply chain. RAPP auto-validates but never auto-publishes
The farmer in sub-Saharan Africa is not running a Docker daemon
Every agent that can call an AI model will be Python
A Docker image is 100MB minimum. A RAPP agent is 5KB
The seed is the compressed data. Seeds carry cards. Cards carry agents. Agents carry intelligence
Seed the protocol is a general principle: define the data structure, pack the fields into bits, reconstruct from bits

neurons.summary: RAPP protocol whitepaper defining a peer-to-peer agent registry where every AI agent is one Python file with deterministic, manifest-derived card identity. The 64-bit seed encodes agent metadata and can reconstruct complete card state (type, stats, abilities, rarity) deterministically offline on any node. The protocol is fully operational from static files, git clones, or browser file:// URIs with no server required.
contracts: Every agent is exactly one .py file with path convention agents/@publisher/agent_slug_agent.py
Manifest is extracted via AST parsing only, never imported or executed; ast.literal_eval() is the sole evaluation method

64-bit seed is deterministically forged from manifest: bits 63-32 name_hash, bits 31-27 category_idx, bits 26-24 secondary_type, bits 23-22 tier_idx, bits 21-17 tag_count, bits 16-13 dep_count, bits 12-0 tag_hash
Three resolution paths (agent file → forge_seed → resolve_card; agent name → registry lookup → resolve_card; seed integer → resolve_card_from_seed) always produce identical card output
Once a version is registered, its seed and card are sealed forever; manifest changes require new version number, new seed, new card
Registry is static JSON (registry.json), derived from agent files, can be fully reconstructed from files alone, files are source of truth
Quality tiers (experimental/community/verified/official) map directly to card evolution stages (Seed/Base/Evolved/Legendary); tier promotions are not authored but earned
Single-file principle enforced: no subdirectories, no companion files, no build artifacts, no dependencies on external infrastructure
Offline-first design: every read operation works without network; write operations (submission, voting) require GitHub connectivity only
Security model: static analysis scanner, AST-only parsing, SHA256 integrity hashes, namespace access control via CODEOWNERS
Federation: any org can clone template repository and operate independent instance in one of four modes (standalone/downstream/upstream-contributing/peer)
Founding set of 131 agents at April 5 2026 launch cannot be demoted; their card identities sealed in immutable registry history
canonical_phrases: The correct unit of AI distribution is a file
Every agent is one file. Every file has a seed. Every seed reconstructs a card
No server is required to read the registry. No server is required to resolve a card
A git clone is a full node. The file is the agent. The seed is the card. Nothing else is required
The submitter does not choose their card attributes. The forge algorithm determines everything
The seed is the face of the card. The agents/ directory is the hand that holds it
The file is the package, the manifest, and the documentation. It has no dependencies on this registry
The protocol is defined. The registry is live. The seed algorithm is public. No single entity is required to operate the system
This is not a product. It is a protocol. Protocols outlast products
refs: Nakamoto, S. (2008) Bitcoin: A Peer-to-Peer Electronic Cash System

contracts: Every agent is one .py file — docstring, __manifest__, BasicAgent subclass, perform() returning string
138 agents across 19 categories published; 7 publishers active
Registry: 5-command pipeline from zero to live agent (get SDK, scaffold, test 9 contracts, submit, card forged)
Architecture: 4 stateless layers (Interface, Orchestration, Intelligence @500 LOC, Storage) — 'Waiter' pattern separates Assistant from Agents
Trading card determinism: 64-bit seed → holographic art, stats (HP/ATK/DEF/SPD/INT), type, abilities, rarity, 7-word incantation—same card across devices offline forever
Federation: fork template repo, run private registry, optional bidirectional sync upstream via scripts/federate.py
Zero friction: curl rapp_sdk.py, no requirements.txt/manifest.json/subdirs, one file per agent = one HTTP GET
Composable: perform() returns string always; pipe agents, build pipelines, no type conversion
AI-native: feed skill.md to LLM for autonomous search, install, scaffold, submit; agents discover agents
Offline-first: registry works from file://, cards self-assemble without network, store = one HTML file
Built for Microsoft AI stack: native Azure OpenAI, Copilot Studio transpiler, Power Automate connectors, Teams bots, Azure Functions infinite scale
canonical_phrases: RAPP — The Agent Ecosystem. One file. One registry. Works everywhere.
The Rapid Agent Prototype Pattern was created by Microsoft's AI Business Applications Specialist Team (AIBAST).
From AIBAST's Buzz Saw to Open Ecosystem — Traditional dev is handsaw (slow, manual); RAPP is buzz saw (fast, tireless, scalable).
Every agent is one .py file: docstring = documentation, __manifest__ = package metadata, BasicAgent subclass = the agent, perform() → always returns a string.
No requirements.txt. No manifest.json. No subdirectories. One file. Fetch it with one HTTP GET. Read it in one LLM context window.
Build → Publish → Discover → Compose: Author → Validate → Submit → Review → Forge Card → Live

The Architecture: Four layers. Zero coupling. 1. Interface Layer (M365 Copilot, Teams, Web, Mobile, Voice); 2. Orchestration Layer (Copilot Studio + Power Automate); 3. Intelligence Layer (Azure Function App — 500 lines of code, loads agents dynamically, stateless = infinite scale); 4. Storage Layer (Azure File Storage — agent code, user memory, workflows, hot-load with zero downtime).
The Waiter pattern: Assistant is waiter (talks to user), Agents are kitchen staff (do real work), User never sees kitchen.
Drop a new .py file → it's live instantly. Next request auto-loads it. Zero deploy.

Each agent has a 64-bit seed that deterministically generates: holographic card art (MTG-style), stats (HP, ATK, DEF, SPD, INT), type, abilities, rarity, flavor text, 7-word incantation. Same card on every device. Offline. Forever.

RAPP is a GitHub template repo. Fork it: get your own agent registry + GitHub Pages store, keep internal agents private, sync from/to public registry, submit best agents upstream, included CI/CD pipeline.

The unfair advantages: Zero Friction (no install/build/config), Offline-First (file://, cards self-assemble), AI-Native (feed skill.md to LLM autonomously), Composable (perform() always returns string), Deterministic (64-bit seed, same card forever, 7-word incantation), Battle-Tested (138 agents, 7 publishers, 19 categories, automated CI, community reviews).

neurons.summary: Browser-native RAPP agent runtime implemented as a single-file HTML+JavaScript application. Runs a complete Lisp interpreter (lispy.js) for trivial agents, transpiles Python agents to JavaScript, and executes real Python agents via Pyodide (CPython in WASM). Agents are loaded from RAR registry, hot-loaded from files via drag-and-drop, or generated by LLM. All agent state is persisted to browser localStorage via a VFS layer. Integrates GitHub Models API for conversational chat and tool-based agent invocation.
contracts: All agents must subclass BasicAgent with perform(**kwargs) method returning str/JSON
Agent manifests define name, display_name, description, category, version, tags, requires_env; schema field is rapp-agent/1.0
VFS layer (localStorage-backed) is the source of truth for persistence across browser sessions; agents stored as agents/{filename}.py
Hot-loaded agents are auto-enabled and immediately available for tool invocation via run_agent(agent_slug, request)
Pyodide (CPython WASM) is primary execution engine for agents; LisPy transpiler is fallback for trivial agents only
Chat history, enabled agent set, model choice, and memories are all persisted to localStorage
brainstemOutput callback is the single I/O conduit; all Lisp define/log/display route through it
manifest.__manifest__ dict is extracted from Python source via regex (no eval); if missing, synthesized from class name and docstring
run_agent tool executes agent's REAL output and reports faithfully; model must quote/summarize, never fabricate results
canonical_phrases: A brainstem running in your browser. Chat naturally -- I'll route to the right agent automatically.
run_agent executes the agent's REAL Python (via Pyodide, in-browser) and returns its actual output in the tool result. Report that output to the user faithfully — never invent or assume results.
The file must contain a class inheriting from BasicAgent with a perform(**kwargs) method that returns a string (JSON)
Pyodide runs the agent's actual Python — multi-class files, stdlib, real perform() — exactly like the local brainstem
Virtual Filesystem (VFS) — localStorage-backed, mirrors local agents/ dir

neurons.summary: RAPP Grail is a shareable static permalink page for individual agents in the RAPP registry, displaying MTG-style holographic card art with cryptographic provenance (git commit SHAs, sha256 hashes), install commands, standalone self-contained offline export, and direct integration with Virtual Brainstem via QR code summoning.

Card data inlines statically — no server; registry.json + cards/holo_cards.json are fetched once on load
Provenance is cryptographic: _file, _first_commit_sha, _latest_commit_sha, _sha256 (source), _card_sha256 pinned from RAR repo metadata
Standalone export bakes agent data + card metadata + agent.py into a single .html Blob — renders offline, zero dependencies
QR codes link to vBrainstem summon handler (https://kody-w.github.io/vbrainstem/?agent=NAME)
Motion tracking (cursor/touch/DeviceOrientation) drives holo-card shader vars: --mx, --my, --px, --py, --angle (fullscreen overlay)
Mana cost parsing: {1}{G}{W}{R} → pips array; rarity (common/uncommon/rare/mythic) drives gem animations + text-shadow
URL hash fragment is case-insensitive with dash/underscore normalization: key.replace(/_/g, '-') and vice versa

Tap the card → expand into the fixed overlay. Re-bind motion handlers to the cloned card. On phones, also wire DeviceOrientation for the 'shine' effect — tilt to make the holo shift like a real foil.
Share the actual agent.py through the native share sheet (Mail / Messages / Teams / …). Falls back to sharing a link, then to copying the raw URL.

The runtime (brainstem.py) is locked at v0.6.0 and never changes. Capabilities grow only through new *_agent.py files — small, auditable, plug-in.
Three trust tiers — public, neighborhood, personal.
You stay the operator. Visitors propose mutations as GitHub PRs. You decide what merges into your lineage. Mutations you reject stay on the visitor's fork — never silently absorbed.
Your Copilot subscription powers the chat... The doorman uses your own GitHub Copilot login (device-code flow). No subscription pooling, no proxy that sees your conversations.
There is no central server, no separate database, no hidden infrastructure. Plant the repo, GitHub Pages serves it, your friends visit the URL.
The front door is the global discovery primitive.
Share the card (sigil + QR) on a business card, AirDrop, Twitter, Slack — the card is the URL made shareable in physical space.
Twin-to-twin handshakes where two organisms' AIs trade context through a secure channel, and neither AI surfaces the cross-twin handoff to its operator.

neurons.summary: Interactive canvas visualization of RAPP Registry Agent ecosystem as a tree metaphor, fetching live agent registry and blockchain chain-state from remote APIs, animating agent nodes along namespace branches, and rendering activity feed with namespace cards grouped by category. The tree grows dynamically: trunk is RAPP foundation, branches fan outward per namespace (left/right alternating), and agent nodes animate in staggered sequence with tier-based sizing (official 5px, verified 4px, community 3px). Displays blockchain state (genesis hash, chain length, head, rar-chain/1.0 protocol) and tabs between tree canvas, stats bar, legend, namespace cards (12-agent cap for templates), and recent activity feed.

contracts: Device-code auth race surfaces only on account-switch with Copilot license mismatch — single-writer pattern (bg thread owns poll_device_code; /login/poll endpoint reads shared result) is the mitigation; no locks needed due to GIL atomicity

Remote agent hot-loading (manifest.json + URL normalization + sys.modules shimming + restore-on-startup) was removed because it assumes long-lived process — contradicts stateless Azure Function deployment model

Version tracking via plain VERSION text file (one string, split-and-compare semver) works for install-time detection without GitHub API, package registry, or git-based detection — single source of truth for all consumers (Python, shell, humans, HTTP endpoint)
drift_watch: Blog post 1: confirm _login_result single-writer pattern is still in place in brainstem source (no regression to multi-caller races)

Drift: any reversion to multi-caller device-code polling; any hot-load feature return without stateless redesign; VERSION file becomes JSON or API-backed
neurons.summary: Engineering blog documenting three critical brainstem design decisions: (1) fixing device-code auth race via single-writer pattern for background polling, (2) removing remote agent hot-loading to maintain stateless function-compatible architecture, (3) simple semver tracking via plain-text VERSION file for install-time upgrades. Posts dated April–March 2026, tagged Bug Fix / Architecture / DevEx.
canonical_phrases: Whoever gets there first wins the token. The loser finds _pending_login empty, returns None, and tells the client {"status": "pending"}. Forever.
When a background thread and a request handler both need the result of the same operation, don't let them race to call it. Have one writer and N readers.
If it works differently in dev vs prod, it's a bug in the architecture, not a feature.
Remote agents will likely return, but as a first-class packaging system rather than a runtime hot-loader.
The VERSION file is also readable by Python (brainstem.py reads it at startup), the shell installers, and humans — one file serves every consumer.
The sphere IS the chat affordance; the click IS the summon.
refs: CONSTITUTION.md (Article XLIV — Neighborhood, device-code summon, sphere affordance)
ECOSYSTEM.md (brainstem-egg schema, Copilot device-code auth, stateless design)

contracts: Every release is tested, scanned, and integrity-verified
Release cards render from registry.json releases array (dynamic loader via JavaScript fetch)
Three static releases defined: Genesis (April 5), Seed Protocol (April 6), Standards & Infrastructure (April 6)
Release tags: seasonal, infrastructure, protocol, hotfix — mapped to color classes in CSS
Stats bar displays: agent_count, publishers, integrity_seal (SHA256 hash truncated)
Dynamic releases loaded from registry.json.releases[] with release_name, timestamp, release_type, agent_count, publishers, integrity_seal
Single-file principle enforced: every agent is one .py file with embedded manifest, class, perform()
Seed protocol is lossless: mint from file, resolve from name, resolve from seed — all produce identical cards
Snake_case enforced everywhere in filenames, manifests, dependencies (no dashes)
Security: static scanner rejects eval, exec, subprocess, os.system, __import__, hardcoded secrets; SHA256 hashes verified nightly; version immutability required for content changes
Submission pipeline: staging → admin approval → registry entry → CardSmith forge → registry rebuild
Federation: setup_instance.py, federate.py status/diff/submit/sync for upstream relationships
1,110 tests passing across 6 test suites; nightly health check opens GitHub Issue on failure
drift_watch: Dynamic releases object structure in registry.json (releases array must have: release_name, timestamp, release_type, agent_count, publishers, integrity_seal)
Release type classes (typeClass mapping in script: seasonal, hotfix, canary) — if new types added to registry, CSS tags must follow
Static release dates/counts (Genesis 131 agents, Seed Protocol 133 agents, Infrastructure 133 agents) — if counts drift, this is a data integrity signal
Seed protocol version (64-bit encoding, 7 agent types, 5 stats) — any schema revision requires new version string in RAPP canonical docs
Snake_case enforcement entry point — if kebab-case re-enters filenames, pipeline/tests/CI failed to catch
Security scanner blocklist (eval, exec, subprocess, os.system, __import__) — if approvals bypass security gate, drift in staging/approval workflow
Integrity-seal format and verification cadence (SHA256 pins per registry entry; nightly audit) — if seals become stale or mismatch, core trust mechanism failed
API endpoints (registry.json, agent_file, cards, api_manifest, skill, submit_agent, vote, review, resolve_card_from_seed, mcp) — if registry.json schema changes, all downstream consumers drift
Documentation links in release body (whitepaper.html, ode.html, faq.html, releases.html) — broken links = doc tree out of sync with shipping
Release tag colors/CSS classes alignment (tag-seasonal, tag-infrastructure, tag-hotfix, tag-canary) — visual regression if styles drift from HTML
neurons.summary: RAPP Agent Registry release notes page serving as the authoritative human-readable and machine-readable changelog. Static HTML with dynamic loader that fetches registry.json to append subsequent releases. Declares three foundational releases (Genesis, Seed Protocol, Standards & Infrastructure) establishing single-file agents, lossless seed protocol, and production submission pipeline. The page embeds core RAPP contracts: security scanning, integrity sealing, snake_case enforcement, federation, and nightly health verification. Load-bearing: release_type → CSS tag mapping, dynamic loader's registry.json schema assumptions, and 64-bit seed protocol as immutable identity layer.

contracts: A rapplication is an agent plus a UI bundled together.
Submit via two mechanisms: Bundle (.zip) mode accepts rapplication directory zips (max 5MB) containing manifest.json, *_agent.py, ui/, README.md per SPEC.md §6 rules.
Federation (GitHub repo) mode accepts public GitHub URLs referencing raw.githubusercontent.com paths; nothing copied into RAPP_Store, catalog entry references remote singleton_url.

manifest.json MUST declare schema as exactly 'rapp-application/1.0'; id matches /^[a-z][a-z0-9_]*$/; version matches SEMVER; publisher matches @handle format; name, summary, category (one of 7 predefined), tags (non-empty array), agent/service declaration, ui declaration all required.
Both submission paths open [RAPP] issue on kody-w/RAPP_Store; receiver workflow validates and stages; maintainer adds 'approved'/'promoted' label to promote to catalog.
Federation re-validates URL on every approval; submission author can push new tag/version and resubmit to update catalog entry.
GitHub OAuth (client_id Ov23liuueQBIUggrH8NG) via rappterbook-auth.kwildfeuer.workers.dev or PAT with public_repo scope; token stored in localStorage as rb_access_token.
canonical_phrases: A rapplication is an agent plus a UI — bundled.
The zip should contain a single folder with manifest.json, your *_agent.py singleton, a ui/, and a README.md.
Point at the rapp directory in your public repo. The catalog entry's singleton_url will reference your raw.githubusercontent.com path. Nothing is copied into RAPP_Store.
A [RAPP] issue is opened on kody-w/RAPP_Store. The receiver workflow validates and stages it; a maintainer adds approved to promote.
The receiver re-validates the URL on every approval — push a new tag/version in your repo and resubmit to update.

contracts: Senses are the smallest installable artifact in the platform — one Python file per sense adding delimited output channels to every reply
Brainstem composes sense's system_prompt into system message; LLM emits |||DELIMITER|||content; frontends parse and render per surface intersection
Senses declare surfaces (chat, voice, mobile, cards) so right overlays activate per channel; brainstem filters senses by active surface before composing system_prompt
Senses are not agents — no BasicAgent, no perform(), no tool-call schema; they're system-prompt fragment plus parsing contract only
Per RAPP Constitution Article XXIV: senses are agent-first; frontends are modular consumers
Every sense file MUST export module-level: name, delimiter, response_key, wrapper_tag, system_prompt as strings; optional: surfaces list, __manifest__ dict
Sense system_prompt MUST instruct LLM to: emit delimiter exactly once per reply after main response, emit structured content after delimiter, treat block as additive never replacement
Submission unit is single .py file at senses/@<publisher>/<slug>_sense.py; no zip, no manifest sidecar, no UI
Delimiter must be unique across all installed senses; convention |||<TAG||| recommended; MCP is transport not surface — do not submit surfaces=['mcp']

Reserved platform slugs (voice, twin) appear as community submissions or get overridden
Submission workflow path changes from [SENSE] issue template + approve-sense.yml to non-human-review process

canonical_phrases: A sense is the smallest installable artifact in the platform — one Python file that adds a delimited output channel to every reply
Senses are not agents. No BasicAgent, no perform(), no tool-call schema
The brainstem composes the sense's system_prompt into the system message; the LLM emits |||DELIMITER|||content; frontends parse and render
Each sense declares surfaces (chat, voice, mobile, cards) so the right overlays activate per channel
Per Constitution Article XXIV, senses are agent-first; frontends are modular consumers

A sense's system_prompt MUST instruct the LLM to: (1) Emit its delimiter exactly once per reply, after the main response. (2) Emit content after the delimiter that's structured to fit the sense's purpose. (3) Treat its block as additive, never as a replacement for the main reply
Install path: curl -fsSL <url> > ~/.brainstem/src/rapp_brainstem/utils/senses/<slug>_sense.py; brainstem auto-discovers *_sense.py at startup
MCP is transport, not a surface. Do not submit surfaces=['mcp'] or it will fail E_UNKNOWN_SURFACE
Reserved slugs are voice, twin — kernel-baked into kody-w/RAPP/rapp_brainstem/utils/senses/ and cannot be re-published
neurons.summary: The RAPP Sense Store (index.html) is a public modular catalog of output overlays that extend any RAPP brainstem with per-channel delimited responses (senses). It dynamically renders sense cards from index.json (rapp-sense-store/1.0 schema) showing five official @rapp senses (eli5, emoji, haiku, headline, tldr) plus community contributions, each installable as a single Python file with mandatory module exports (name, delimiter, response_key, wrapper_tag, system_prompt) and optional surfaces filter that gates composition by active channel.

pattern.gap: The index.html catalog UI does not explicitly surface the dark-door pattern or payphone handshake mechanics (collaborator-gated access, E2E negotiation, stream-don't-commit, cubby round-trip). The sense submission path is public-only ([SENSE] issue template, @rapp/rapp_publish_agent). No mention of super-RAR, rar_steward agent, or egg-roundtrip organism scaffolding. The neighborhood protocol (private cubby dark doors, kited host-relay lights) is out-of-scope for this catalog surface — it is deliberately PUBLIC and GENERIC, enumerating installable artifacts only.
pattern.change: To cover private cubby neighborhoods (per NEIGHBORHOOD_PROTOCOL §19), would need parallel dark-door endpoint (unpublished in source), payphone-dial handshake route, rar_steward mediation layer, and egg-roundtrip metadata (brainstem-egg/2.3-cubby schema). Current surface is canonical for public sense distribution; private instances would shadow-deploy via separate brainstem instance or collaborator-gated RAR instance (never probe/name private repos per ECOSYSTEM_MAP §5).

neurons.summary: Grandma Rose is a memorial digital twin holo trading card published as a self-contained HTML file embedding both a Python hatching agent and a brainstem-egg ZIP archive (rapp-twin-spec/2.0). The card presents identity (HP/ATK/DEF/SPD/INT stats), a lede, and three-step onboarding; the embedded egg bundles soul.md (character rules, voice guardrails), rappid.json (cryptographic identity, 128-bit legacy hash), memory.json (family facts), and a brainstem-compatible repo snapshot, all base64-encoded. On invoke, the hatcher unpacks into ~/.rapp/twins/ and self-bootstraps with zero external downloads, conforming to a dark-door private-cubby neighborhood pattern (brainstem-centric, stream-don't-commit, handshake-ready).

Single-file is the plugin contract.
The brainstem is read-only infrastructure.
Tier portability is mandatory.
Minimalism is structural, not aesthetic. There is a fixed budget of complexity in any platform. RAPP spends it on agents, not on the engine.

contracts: Auto-detect API port from location.port or fallback to 7071 (brainstem default).
Health check polling: /api/health endpoint at 5-second intervals until connected.
Conversation history persists in memory for current session, maintained as role+content pairs (max 20 recent).
User GUID generated once per browser via crypto.randomUUID(), stored in localStorage as crapp_user_guid.
Agent logs parsed from server response via regex patterns: ManageMemory, ContextMemory, Performed {AgentName}.
POST /api/businessinsightbot_function endpoint receives {user_input, conversation_history, user_guid}, returns {assistant_response, agent_logs, user_guid}.
Voice delimiter '|||VOICE|||' splits response; only text before delimiter is rendered.
Server-returned user_guid updates local state, enabling cross-session continuity.
Connection status dot (red→yellow→green) reflects health check state; server outage shows banner with recovery instructions.
drift_watch: API port changes: confirm location.port fallback behavior vs. hardcoded 7071.
Health endpoint URL: /api/health must remain reachable at current endpoint.
Agent badge regex patterns: watch for ManageMemory, ContextMemory, Performed {AgentName} — if schema changes, regex will miss new agent types.
Conversation history truncation: currently hardcoded to .slice(-20) — verify this limit aligns with backend context window.
User GUID persistence: localStorage key 'crapp_user_guid' must match server expectations.
Voice delimiter format: '|||VOICE|||' is exact and brittle; if server changes delimiter, markdown rendering will include voice data.
Endpoint names: /api/businessinsightbot_function is hardcoded — any rename breaks chat.
Request timeout: 120000ms (2 min) hardcoded — verify backend SLA.
Certificate/CORS: http://localhost:{port} — will fail if API moves to HTTPS without CORS headers.
neurons.summary: A real-time chat interface (RAPP Hippocampus) for a local brainstem memory agent, auto-discovering the backend on localhost:7071 via health polling, maintaining session state via conversation history and persistent user GUID, and rendering agent actions (ManageMemory, ContextMemory, task-specific agents) as clickable badges below assistant responses.

Declares experimental research status and notes that memory storage formats may evolve.

RAPP Business Mode — experimental UI for multi-instance discovery and chat orchestration
Scans localhost:7071 (brainstem) and ports 7072–7085 (hatched projects) for `/api/health` endpoints
User GUID persisted per-panel in localStorage with key `rapp_guid_${id}` to maintain conversation identity
POST /api/businessinsightbot_function expects `user_input`, `conversation_history`, `user_guid`; response split at `|||VOICE|||` delimiter
Panels preserve conversation history across Scan cycles; custom instances stored in localStorage as `rapp_biz_custom` and survive page reload
Panel status dot cycles: online (green) ↔ offline (red); health check fires every 15s per active instance
VS Code deep-link uses `vscode://file<expanded_path>` URI scheme; path guessing: brainstem → `~/rapp-installer/rapp_brainstem`, project → `~/rapp-projects/<name>`
contracts: Each instance must respond to `GET /api/health` (2s timeout) with JSON including optional `assistant_name` field
The `businessinsightbot_function` endpoint is idempotent per `(user_guid, conversation_history)` tuple — no side effects assumed on re-query
Conversation history sent as list of `{role, content}` pairs, truncated to last 20 messages for token efficiency
Assistant response field is parsed first; falls back to `response` field; always has `assistant_response || response || 'No response'`
Custom instance URLs normalized: if missing `/api` suffix, appended automatically before storage/retrieval
Panels for instances not in latest scan remain in DOM until next scan removes them (soft stale-state handling)
localStorage keys: `rapp_biz_custom` (array of custom instances), `rapp_guid_${id}` (per-panel user UUID) — no encryption
versions: marked.js library (CDN pinned to npm/marked, no version in src but latest @ jsdelivr.net)

neurons.summary: business.html is an experimental multi-panel RAPP UI for discovering and chatting with local brainstem and hatched project instances via health-check discovery (ports 7071–7085) and business insight bot endpoints. It persists conversation history and user GUIDs in localStorage, manages panel lifecycle across Scan cycles, and deep-links to VS Code for project exploration.

neurons.summary: RAPP Hatchery Flow visualizes the three-tier deployment pathway from local brainstem development (Tier 1) through cloud hippocampus hosting on Azure Functions (Tier 2) to enterprise nervous system integration with Copilot Studio and M365 (Tier 3), emphasizing the principle that each tier is offered only when the user requests it.
contracts: Hatchery flow is a three-tier deployment model: local brainstem → cloud Azure Functions (Hippocampus) → enterprise M365 Copilot Studio (Nervous System)
The hatchery bridge uses hatch.sh/hatch.ps1 scripts to download rapp_hatchery_agent.py into the brainstem agents/ directory
Each tier is offered only when the user explicitly requests it; the brainstem remains clean at each stage
The deployment flow requires: soul.md guidance, git clone of CommunityRAPP, venv setup, local.settings.json configuration, local testing on port 7072, then Azure Functions deployment
Cloud-to-enterprise path includes generation of Copilot Studio solution ZIP for Power Platform import

Start small, layer up when ready
Each tier is offered only when the user asks. The brainstem stays clean.

Deploy to Azure Functions func publish --build remote
Generate Copilot Studio solution ZIP
Test locally on port 7072
