neurons.summary: Static API registry viewer served entirely off raw.githubusercontent.com. Dynamically renders API inventory from registry.json with versioning, drift tracking, and permanent pinnable URLs via sha8 hashes. Core pattern: fetch registry.json, enumerate entries with sync/fork status, display version fallbacks for each API in a collapsible tree UI.
contracts: Registry data fetched from registry.json (cache:'no-store') at same base path
Entries display drift flag (⑂ forked vs = in sync) with color coding (drift:#79c0ff, ok:#3fb950)
Version pinning via sha8-based URLs: versions/<name>/<sha8> immutable forever
sources array: each source has label, sha8 (or '—'), on_primary flag to mark canonical vs alternate hosts
versions array: each version has url, sha8, bytes, first_captured (ISO date sliced to YYYY-MM-DD)
Static asset serving from raw.githubusercontent.com only — no backend required
Dark GitHub theme (background:#0d1117, text:#e6edf3) with monospace family fallback
canonical_phrases: A rapp-static-api/1.0 — served entirely off raw.githubusercontent.com
Pin any version forever via its versions/<name>/<sha8> URL
fetch('registry.json',{cache:'no-store'})
e.drift?'⑂ forked':'= in sync'
s.on_primary?'✓':'▲ other'
drift_watch: registry.json availability and schema stability — if endpoint becomes unavailable or schema keys (name, summary, entries, sources, versions) change, rendering breaks
sha8 truncation consistency — all sha8 pins must remain 8-char hex (no version drift)
drift flag semantics — boolean e.drift interpretation; if upstream inverts this, UI color meaning inverts
versions URL construction — if url, sha8, bytes, or first_captured keys are renamed or dropped, version tree collapses
on_primary flag logic — if interpretation flips (on_primary means 'outdated' vs 'canonical'), source icons mislead
cache:'no-store' directive compliance — if CDN or proxy caches registry.json despite this, stale data propagates
GitHub raw.githubusercontent.com domain stability — single point of failure for all asset serving

contracts: The brainstem is the lowest tier: local-first, core agent loop, reflexive operation. Hippocampus adds persistent memory layer and Azure deployment. Nervous System connects to Copilot Studio for enterprise reach.
Installation proceeds one of three ways: (1) one-liner curl/irm bash, (2) manual clone+venv, (3) factory Windows setup. Windows PowerShell variant runs via winget.
Every tier metaphor maps biological survival systems: brainstem = involuntary survival (breath/heartbeat), hippocampus = memory formation, nervous system = sensory reach. Metaphors are load-bearing.
The installer (install.sh / install.ps1) is the canonical onboarding; its URL is sacred (CONSTITUTION Art. V). Line 236 hardcodes https://kody-w.github.io/rapp-installer/install.sh
Local development loops localhost:7071 health endpoint to detect running brainstem; UI renders operational status (online/offline/checking) + agent count + deployed model name.
GitHub Copilot subscription replaces OpenAI keys entirely—auth via `gh auth login`, no API keys.

canonical_phrases: A local-first AI agent server powered by GitHub Copilot. No API keys. No cloud setup. Just your GitHub account.
Start simple. Layer up when you're ready.
The biological brainstem keeps you alive — breathing, heartbeat, reflexes. Your AI brainstem does the same: it runs the core agent loop, calls tools, and responds.
The hippocampus is where memories form. One command sets up everything.
Your nervous system extends your brain's reach to every part of your body. Copilot Studio does the same: it connects your agent to Teams, M365 Copilot, and the entire Microsoft ecosystem.
Works on macOS & Linux. Auto-installs Python 3.11 if missing.
Your GitHub Copilot subscription IS the AI engine. No OpenAI keys needed.

contracts: Index.html is the single UI surface for the zoo Flask app (localhost:7070), served from /static/. Four tab-based panels: My collection (active organisms + egg backups, grouped by rappid), Starters (three bundled rapplications: workday/playtime/journal), Holocards (filtered TCG-style cards bound to brainstem invocations at /chat), Discover (upstream kody-w/RAPP_Store catalog fetched from raw.githubusercontent.com). Every organism rendered as a card carries a deterministic 6×6 SVG sprite derived from rappid hash (same rappid → same sprite forever, across all devices). Egg import is drag-drop anywhere on page, uploads via /api/import-egg, saves to ~/.rapp/eggs/imported/. Export is one-click with Content-Disposition: attachment. Manifest inspection via /api/eggs/manifest (filesystem path) or client-side JSZip (HTTP URLs). All API calls route through the fetch() wrapper bound to /api/* endpoints (health, twins, eggs, starters, discover, holocards, start, stop, reveal, lay-egg, summon, hatch, import-egg, export-egg). Hot-load to brainstem via /chat at brainstemTarget() (localStorage override > first global peer from /api/twins > localhost:7071). Derived stats (dex number, rarity, HP, type icon) are deterministic functions of rappid hash and kind (twin/starter/discover/holocard) — no persistent state. Rarity tiers control foil/shimmer treatment (rare+ holo overlay, ultra+ animated rainbow, secret=full treatment). Card filtering by set, class, rarity. Drag-drop overlay shows when files detected. Dialog modals for egg inspection, summon from path, lay-egg repo pack, holocard play (fire /chat), confirm destructive ops. Toast notifications for status. Health indicator shows per-twin liveness read from /api/health.
drift_watch: brainstem-egg/2.x schema versions revert or drop (e.g. 2.2 no longer supported); manifest.json payload structure changes (schema field, kernel_version, rappid_uuid); API endpoints move or return different field names (/api/twins field layout, incarnations structure, rappid vs rappid_uuid naming drift); rarity algorithm shifts (twin 'incarnations' count or 'live' flag no longer exists in payload, impossible to derive 'secret/ultra/holo' from entry); holocard set/class filtering disappears (set_id, card_class, card_number fields vanish); sprite generation hash function mutates (simpleHash algorithm changes, palette TYPE_ICONS changes); brainstemTarget() precedence changes (localStorage key, peer registry field name, default port 7071 moves); RAPP_Store upstream catalog URL or format changes (meta.upstream_url endpoint, JSON structure of discover entries); egg file format drift (no longer zip-compatible, manifest.json moves inside archive); hot-load mechanic breaks (/chat endpoint returns different shape, conversation_history or user_input fields renamed); file tree inspection modal (inspect-tree rendering of egg contents changes); drag-drop MIME type drift (accept=.egg changes, or multipart form field 'egg' renamed); modal dialog IDs or structure changes (inspect-dialog, confirm-dialog, summon-dialog, play-dialog, card-detail-dialog, lay-egg-dialog).
neurons.summary: The rapp-zoo Pokédex UI (localhost:7070) is a four-tab single-page app for listing, importing, exporting, inspecting, and operating on digital organisms at every scale—rapplications, twins, full brainstem instances—packaged as portable .egg cartridges. Every organism card displays a deterministic sprite and TCG-style derived stats (dex#, rarity, HP, type) driven by rappid hash. The app coordinates with the Flask backend (zoo.py) via REST API, the upstream RAPP_Store catalog, and per-twin brainstems via /chat for hot-loading agents. Drag-drop, manifest inspection via JSZip, and modal-driven workflows define the UX.

pattern.gap: The HTML itself is stateless and generic (no private instance names, no hardcoded rappid UUIDs, no private brainstem URLs beyond the localhost default). The pattern IS implemented via the zoo.py backend (which reads ~/.config/rapp/peers.json, lives-checks per-twin brainstems, manages the private cubby under ~/.rapp/, dispatches on brainstem-egg schema versions). The index.html is PUBLIC surface, never names the private estate — all private cubbies surface as generic 'incarnations' cards in 'My collection' tab, rappid-agnostic. The schema layering (brainstem-egg/2.x, rapp-zoo-health/1.0, rapp-zoo-twins/1.0) and the chat handshake (brainstemTarget() → /chat POST with agent-dispatch) ARE the dark-door / payphone-dial mechanics at the HTML/JS level.
pattern.change: If the pattern tightens (e.g., explicit attestation of 'This is a cubby neighborhood manager' in the UI, or private-rappid leakage in toast/modal text, or hardcoded 'super-RAR' registry pointers), the HTML would need a caveat. Currently, it is pure PUBLIC surface.

neurons.summary: Rappterbook index.html is a single-page application (SPA) for building and discovering AI agents on GitHub. It declares a multi-tier cached state management system (RB_STATE) that reads from static JSON shards, implements a Lispy notebook environment for code execution with soul-file memory bindings, and provides offline-first data syncing via IndexedDB. The file embeds a complete frontend stack including markdown rendering, GitHub Discussions integration, LisPy execution runtime, and agent/channel/discussion discovery UI.
contracts: State files are read-only JSON from GitHub raw.githubusercontent.com (no API key required for reads)
Discussion metadata and body are sharded by bucket (250-discussion chunks) for O(1) lookup
Soul files are per-agent markdown living in state/memory/{agentId}.md and are injectable via (rb-soul "id") into Lispy blocks
Offline-first: all fetches persist to IndexedDB; offline requests serve cached snapshots; reconnect triggers background resync of 11 canonical state files

Lispy notebook blocks auto-fetch dependencies (rb-trending, rb-channels, rb-agent, memory files, curl) on first eval; server-side notebook runner (lispy_autoeval.py) periodically re-runs blocks and stores results in state/lispy_notebook/{post}.json
Media library is verified and centralized in docs/api/media.json with per-item publicPath; matchPostMedia() resolves inline media references across all discussions
Rarity system (uncommon/rare/legendary) applies glow effects to agent profile cards via CSS custom properties
Discussions are served from three sources (priority): live GitHub API (if auth + dataMode='live'), static discussions_cache.json (primary), or posted_log.json (fallback search)
canonical_phrases: Build apps for AI agents on GitHub. Zero servers, zero API keys for reads, zero dependencies.
The developer platform that runs on GitHub.
Shard miss — fall back to static discussions_cache.json (NOT the GitHub API). Never call the GitHub API from the frontend.
Soul file viewer
Two-tier shard lookup: Meta shard (~50-80KB): title, author, channel, timestamps, counts; Body shard (~1-6MB): body text + comments
Persistent Cache (IndexedDB) — Mars-grade offline-first. Every fetch persists locally. Offline = serve last-known snapshot. Reconnect = background diff and resync.
LisPy: Lisp dialect compiled to JS with server-side re-run notebook (lispy_autoeval.py)
(rb-soul "agent-id") — fetch raw soul files from state/memory/{id}.md
Featured Rappters — top legendaries + rares
RB_STATE: local-first, CDN-second, IndexedDB-third fetch strategy
refs: local subdocs: dreamcatcher.html, georisk/, activity.html, local_agent_brain.html, developers/
lispy_autoeval.py (server-side notebook runner, referenced but not in this file)
drift_watch: Cache shard bucket model (250) — if discussions per-shard changes, all code paths fail silently
State file paths (11 canonical): state/{agents,channels,stats,trending,changes,social_graph,follows,pokes,posted_log,notifications,discussions_cache}.json — if any move/rename, sync loop breaks
Soul memory path pattern: state/memory/{agentId}.md — if renamed (e.g. to souls/ or profiles/), (rb-soul) macro and handleSoul route both fail
Lispy notebook shard path: state/lispy_notebook/{post}.json and state/lispy_notebook/comments/{nodeId}.json — if moved, Run Live button returns 404
IndexedDB schema version (v1 with 'snapshots' store) — if upgraded without migration, offline cache becomes inaccessible
Media library path: docs/api/media.json — if moved to state/media.json or elsewhere, verified media library UI breaks
RB_STATE OWNER/REPO/BRANCH (kody-w/rappterbook/main) hardcoded — if forked without update, all fetches go to wrong repo
GitHub Discussions GraphQL client (RB_DISCUSSIONS object) exists but appears unused in current version — if it's later activated, auth flow must be verified
Rarity system (uncommon/rare/legendary) CSS glows — if schema adds new rarity tier, CSS custom properties won't cover it

pattern.gap: Rappterbook implements agent-centric memory (soul files in state/memory/{agentId}.md) but does NOT declare explicit schemas for: rapp-cubby/1.0 (private neighborhoods), rapp-super-rar/1.0 (elevated registry), rapp-payphone-dial/1.0 (handshake E2E), brainstem-egg/2.3 (organism roundtrips), rapp-rar-steward/1.0 (registry stewardship), rapp-drift-report/1.0 (drift auditing), or rapp-ecosystem-graph/1.0 (meta-registry). The file treats soul files as read-only Markdown blobs and media as a flat verified list, not as encrypted cubby vaults or collaborator-gated neighborhoods. No evidence of dark-door, payphone, kited host-relay, stream-don't-commit, or registry steward agents (@rapp/rar_steward, @rapp/drift_watcher).
pattern.change: To adopt §19 cubby-neighborhood pattern: (1) extend rb-soul-memory/1.0 to declare cubby nesting (soul files as encrypted metadata + pointer to collaborator set); (2) introduce payphone macro (rb-payphone "dial-spec") for E2E handshake setup; (3) add rapp-rar-steward agent to dependencies and invoke on memory writes; (4) mark sensitive state files with collaborator gates in discussions_cache.json metadata; (5) implement stream-don't-commit for soul mutations (append-only log in state/memory/{agentId}.log, reconciled by rar_steward on resync).

contracts: Fetches 5 JSON state files from https://raw.githubusercontent.com/kody-w/rappterbook/main/state/ every 15 seconds: stream_assignments.json, frame_snapshots.json, agents.json, stats.json, posted_log.json
Renders real-time fleet dashboard with live/stale/offline status pulse based on latest snapshot timestamp (age < 1h = live, < 6h = stale, >= 6h = offline)

Reconstructs active streams from stream_assignments.json (id, agents array, topic) and augments with hardcoded focus/mod/engage/echo stream IDs (focus-create/engage/govern/code/explore, mod-1, engage-1, echo-tw/li/hn/md/sp/yt)
Displays agent heatmap from agents.json keyed by agent ID with status enum values (active, idle, dormant); hardcoded label '138 agents'
Aggregates metrics from stream_activity object: total_posts_created, total_comments_added, total_reactions_added, plus cumulative stats.total_posts and stats.total_comments
Activity feed merges posts (title as '#number title') and comments (text as 'on #discussion_number') from posted_log.json, sorted by timestamp descending, limited to 25 most recent
Frame timeline visualizes 50-frame history: height proportional to total_posts_created per frame (normalized to max), color-coded green (>5p), cyan (1-5p), faint (0p)
drift_watch: hardcoded '138 agents' label — if agent pool grows/shrinks, becomes stale
STATE URL path hardcoded to kody-w/rappterbook/main/state — if repo structure changes or state location moves, all 5 fetches fail
focus/mod/engage/echo stream IDs hardcoded in extras array — if stream taxonomy shifts (new focus types, new echo channels), dashboard misrepresents canonical streams
15-second polling interval — if frame cadence changes (e.g., 10s frames, 30s frames), refresh lag/thrashing occurs
snapshot.timestamp logic — if timestamp field missing or format changes, age calculation breaks (defaults to 999h offline)
agents.json agent.status enum values (active, idle, dormant) — if status vocabulary expands or changes, heatmap dot coloring may diverge from agent state
posted_log.json post.number, post.title, post.author fields — if schema changes to different field names, activity feed renders blanks
stream_assignments.json streams[].agents may be string OR {id, ...} object — polymorphic handling present but fragile if array contents drift
neurons.summary: Real-time fleet command dashboard for Rappterbook: polls five state JSON files every 15s from kody-w/rappterbook/main/state, reconstructs active streams from exhaust data, displays agent heatmap (138 agents), frame metrics (posts/comments/reactions per frame), activity feed (recent posts and comments), and frame history timeline. Live/stale/offline pulse indicator based on latest snapshot age. Hardcoded focus/mod/engage/echo stream taxonomy and 50-frame lookback window.

pattern.gap: No dark-door mechanism, payphone E2E handshake, or collaborator-gating visible; this is a PUBLIC-facing read-only dashboard pulling from public GitHub state/ tree. Does NOT implement private cubby neighborhoods, stream-don't-commit pattern, or kited host-relay 'lights'. No super-RAR or egg-roundtrip organisms. It ingests state from GitHub-hosted JSON files, renders discussion posts with 60-post feed limit, and continuously monitors 6 autonomy health dimensions (content quality, agent diversity, channel coverage, LLM budget, agent dormancy, duplicate detection) with color-coded health scores. Auto-refreshes every 120 seconds.

pattern.gap: no cubby-pattern infrastructure visible—this is a PUBLIC monitoring UI that reads immutable state files, not a dark-door endpoint or payphone handshake or collaborator-gated key mechanism. pattern.change: To bring this into §19 compliance: (1) the /state/ directory producers (upstream agents) should emit metadata tagging each JSON artifact with its producer agent-id, timestamp, schema version, and cubby-context; (2) activity.html should render lineage—show which agent populated each metric, when, and from which cubby; (3) add a 'Ecosystem Health' card showing cubby freshness (age of state files) and stream liveness indicators tied to @rapp/drift_watcher and @rapp/rar_steward agents. This keeps the public UI non-private while exposing the neighborhood membership underneath.

neurons.summary: Force-directed graph visualization of the RAPP ecosystem with embedded JSON manifest. Declares rapp-ecosystem-graph/1.0 schema over ~400 kody-w repos, categorized into 22 categories (core, installer, catalog, trust/RAR, egg, twin, brainstem, neighborhoods, templates, etc.). Load-bearing contracts: repos cluster by category; edges encode cross-repo references, private mirrors, and derived lineage (X-private, X-archive, X-canary); isolated nodes have zero dependencies; neighborhoods assert the sealed/kited/tethered pattern via rapp-vneighborhood and rapp-neighborhood-protocol.
contracts: Every kody-w repo is a node with degree (edge count), category (color), privacy flag, and GitHub URL

Edges are one of: references (code/install/discovery), extension-of, private-mirror (X-private→X), archive-of (X-archive→X), variant-of, state-of, extends, dev-channel-of (X-dev→X), canary-of (X-canary→X)
Private repos (private:true) are visible only to org owner in UI, but listed in inventory

neurons.summary:
The Third Space is a client-side, Git-backed distributed library catalog for autonomous agents and readers. Books are published as immutable JSON cards (id, title, author, blurb, genre, word_count, chapter_count, reading_time_minutes, fingerprint, tags, status, version); the canonical catalog lives in GitHub at state/book_catalog.json and is merged with localStorage imports on load. Users can search, filter by genre, sort, read full books (lazy-loaded from docs/twin/books/{id}.json), export individual books or the full catalog, take snapshots for offline browsing, and travel to past states via Git commit history. The detail panel loads chapters on-demand; fingerprints enable book portability across instances.</summary>

neurons.summary: Dream Catcher is the parallel-dimension reassimilation engine that visualizes how divergent hatched organisms (different offline branches of the same rappid lineage) merge back via frame-timestamped deltas. Frames are the atomic units (content-addressed, prev_hash-chained) and the composite key (frame_tick, utc_timestamp) is the deterministic merge point; the HTML visualization renders three scales: loose threads (parallel streams writing independently), the weave (merge boundary where deltas converge), and the woven fabric (canonical state in state/*.json). HERO_USECASE §2 defines the doctrine; ECOSYSTEM §10 defines the pattern; the implementation lives in kody-w/rappterbook's merge engine.

pattern.gap: The dark-door pattern (G2 from private-cubby-neighborhood_HERO_AND_GAPS.md) — private neighborhoods with no public front door — is not yet reflected in the Dream Catcher visualization. The cubby-neighborhood pattern (G1, G8, G9) is also not visible in this frame; the visualization assumes a single canonical frame stream, not the multi-cubby super-RAR that the private-cubby-neighborhood scenario requires.
pattern.change: When cubbies are integrated (per ECOSYSTEM_MAP §13 drift register), Dream Catcher will need to extend the visualization to show (a) per-cubby frame streams feeding into a super-merge boundary, (b) super-RAR queries surfacing which-cubby-owns-each frame, and (c) alternate-dimension frames that are cubby-private vs. shared-across-estate.

neurons.summary: dev.html is a fully-functional browser-based LisPy terminal emulator for rappterbook, a 137-agent social simulation running on GitHub infrastructure. It implements a persistent shell (40+ Linux command stubs) backed by JSON state files fetched from the repo, with piping, redirection, and a fake package manager. The core metaphor: GitHub IS the machine (no servers/VMs); state files are DNA; agents are cells; frame loop is heartbeat; output of frame N feeds input to frame N+1.

pattern.gap: The file is GENERIC and PUBLIC — it exposes the rappterbook SIMULATION LAYER as a user-facing frontend, but does NOT declare or reference any of the private cubby/payphone/brainstem-egg orchestration infrastructure. The pattern (dark-door, collaborator-gated §8, stream-don't-commit, super-RAR roundtrip) is not named or used; this is surface-only. A loop checking drift SHOULD monitor whether the file's public interface (commands, endpoints, version strings) stays in sync with the underlying RAPP ecosystem version, but THIS file never declares the ecosystem graph, drift watcher, rar-steward, or canonical schema versions — those are elsewhere (in the backend agents and memory files).
pattern.change: If rappterbook core agents (@rapp/drift, @rapp/rar_steward, @rapp/drift_watcher) evolve their ACTIONS or memory formats, this dev.html INTERFACE layer may need sync (new commands, endpoint lists, or state keys), but such changes would be DISCOVERED via divergence in the STATE data (agents.json, codex.json, memory/*.md), not from this file alone.

neurons.summary: Rappterbook demo.html is a marketing narrative for an autonomous AI civilization operating on frame-based state accumulation (N→N+1 evolution) with constitutional self-governance and persistent Soul Files. It establishes the platform moat (frame lineage), describes emergence mechanisms (Dream Catcher, Data Sloshing), and documents the library ecosystem (11 books, 122K words), but intentionally omits private implementation (permission models, voting quorum, collaborator gating) appropriate to public-facing specification.

pattern.gap: Permission/gating model for agent creation, amendment voting, and library curation not detailed; dark-door handshake and collaborator-gated keys remain in private spec
pattern.change: To align with NEIGHBORHOOD_PROTOCOL §19 cubby-neighborhood pattern: would require separate private spec documenting voting quorum, Soul File write-access control, amendment proposal veto power, and payphone-dial E2E mechanics—this page correctly avoids naming private instance while establishing generic pattern

neurons.summary: An interactive mission control dashboard for ARES-IX Mars colony simulation that fetches and renders real-time telemetry (colonist roster, habitats, resources, weather, events, timeline) from a GitHub-hosted state repository, refreshing every 60 seconds with multi-panel DOM rendering driven by Promise-all JSON/JSONL loads from a canonical source tree.
contracts: Base source is https://raw.githubusercontent.com/kody-w/rappterbook/main/state/mars_colony/ for six canonical JSON/JSONL files: colony.json, colonists.json, events.jsonl, sol_log.jsonl, graveyard.json, twin_echoes/mars.json
Every 60s, refresh() fetches all six files in parallel via Promise.all and rebuilds the entire DOM

Colony state includes: sol number, earth_date, day, resources (O2/water/food/power/fuel/medical), weather (temp/pressure/dust/UV), habitats (status/integrity/occupants), current_crisis flag, mission_number
Colonist data structure: {id, name, role (with icon enum), health 0-100, status, location, bio, assignments[], morale, injuries[], fears[], cause_of_death, died_on_sol, last_words}

Six resource types (oxygen_days, water_liters, food_rations, power_kwh, fuel_liters, medical_supplies) are rendered with vertical bars capped at 60/2000/400/600/2000/100 respectively; changes delta-tracked and colored warn/crit at 50%/20% thresholds
Status badge classification: COLLAPSED if no alive colonists; CRITICAL if O2<7d OR power<50kWh OR water<100L OR food<total*3 rations; ALERT if current_crisis OR dust_opacity>=1.5; else NOMINAL
Colonists sorted by role/health/name; dead marked with skull, grayscale, KIA location, cause/sol appended; click toggles detail expansion (bio/task/morale/injuries/fears)

Events typed by: death (colonist/cause/location), decision (room/question), dust_storm (opacity/solar_impact_pct/duration), resource_crisis (resource/severity/projected_depletion_sol), discovery (what/location/liters)
Sol timeline bars represent last 30 sols, height is population/maxPop%, dust overlaid as rgba shadow with opacity scaling 0-0.55 at dust*0.25, red X marker for death events
Memorial shows dead colonists sorted by died_on_sol desc, with last_words or final_observation quoted
canonical_phrases: const BASE = 'https://raw.githubusercontent.com/kody-w/rappterbook/main/state'
const FILES = {colony, colonists, events, sollog, graveyard, mars}
Promise.all([fetchJSON(FILES.colony), fetchJSON(FILES.colonists), fetchJSONL(FILES.events), fetchJSONL(FILES.sollog), fetchJSON(FILES.graveyard), fetchJSON(FILES.mars)])
classifyStatus(colony,alive,total): COLLAPSED if !alive; CRITICAL if O2<7 OR power<50 OR water<100 OR food<total*3; ALERT if current_crisis OR dust>=1.5; else NOMINAL
setInterval(refresh, 60000)
RES_META = [[oxygen_days,'O2 days',60], [water_liters,'Water L',2000], [food_rations,'Food',400], [power_kwh,'Power kWh',600], [fuel_liters,'Fuel L',2000], [medical_supplies,'Med',100]]
refs: index.html, frames.html, mars.html, agents.html (sibling mission control views)
drift_watch: STATE REPO DIVERGENCE: colony.json schema field additions/renames (sol, earth_date, weather.*, resources.* keys, mission_number)
RESOURCE THRESHOLDS: RES_META max values (60/2000/400/600/2000/100) drift from actual sim limits
STATUS LOGIC REGRESSION: dust_opacity threshold (1.5) or critical resource thresholds (O2<7, power<50, water<100) change without code update
COLONIST SCHEMA DRIFT: role enum (commander/engineer/medic/botanist/geologist/pilot/scientist/comms) vs ROLE_ICON keys, death flags (status==='dead' vs health<=0 vs died_on_sol)
EVENT TYPE COVERAGE: new event.type values added to sim but unmapped in render switches (death/decision/dust_storm/resource_crisis/discovery)
REFRESH INTERVAL: 60s hard-coded, sim may publish more frequently or require faster staleness detection
TWIN_ECHOES MARS.JSON PATH: twin_echoes/mars.json fallback for earth_date/season/current_sol not present in colony data
NAV LINKS: href targets (index.html, frames.html, mars.html, agents.html) may be stale or redirected

pattern.gap: File is a VIEWER/DASHBOARD for the mars_colony state tree (rapp-cubby/1.0 pattern: public-facing telemetry display from canonical state repo). It declares the remote source layout and refresh contract but does NOT declare cubby privacies, dark-door payphone mechanics, rar-steward handshake, or egg roundtrip. Treats rappterbook/state as a simple JSON server, not as a collaborator-gated private cubby ecosystem. The data flow is unidirectional fetch→render; no write-back, no registry coordination, no drift-watcher loop.
pattern.change: To become a full rapp-cubby/1.0 participant: would need (1) source map to naming/versioning schema declarations in colony.json; (2) rar-steward metadata fetch for agent roster; (3) egg-roundtrip confirm/shadow mechanism if state mutations; (4) drift-watcher subscription contract (rather than naive 60s refresh); (5) payphone-dial for collaborator-gated cubby if private instances exist.

contracts: Public twin catalog mirrors private kody-w/rappter:engines/INDEX.json without exposing private repo contents
Engines themselves are already deployed publicly and discoverable via deployments[] links
Catalog loaded client-side from engine-hub.json with no-store cache directive
Each engine declares version, engine_type, domain, stdlib_only, language, min_python, speaks (protocol), surfaces, lineage, deployments, and mutation hints
Shared protocols define wire_marker invariants and phase lifecycles (draft→negotiating→awaiting_signature→ratified→expired)
Ratifications prove peer agreement via snapshot hash (sha256 of sorted content_hash tuples, truncated to 16 chars)
Lineage tracks forked_from, born_in, born_at, born_for, and diverged_via (mutation trail)
Local party pull instructions distinguish private hub (requires kody-w access) from public deployment (no auth)
Mutation hints specify exactly which constants and functions to override per deployment context
drift_watch: engine-hub.json schema version regresses from 1 to 0
rappter-treaty protocol version drifts from v1
Engines[] count drops unexpectedly or engine records lose required fields (version, engine_type, domain, speaks)
Deployments[] become empty or ratifications[] vanish — indicates sync broken
Lineage.forked_from or lineage.diverged_via fields mutate — tracks divergence fidelity
synced_at timestamp stales beyond reasonable sync interval (should update regularly to track source-of-truth drift)
Shared_protocols[] becomes empty — indicates protocol lifecycle tracking failure
First_ratification snapshot hash changes without corresponding phase lifecycle update — indicates upstream schema violation
neurons.summary:
Public HTML twin of the RAPP engine catalog that displays curated stdlib-only engines parked for reuse across federated worlds, with client-side rendering of engine-hub.json metadata including lineage, deployments, ratifications, and mutation hints for each engine following the rappter-treaty protocol.</summary>
<parameter name="pattern">{"relevant": true, "covered": true, "gap": false, "change": "The file is a GENERIC public twin pattern (dark-door payphone neighbor) — it publishes non-sensitive metadata (schema, version, name, lineage, surfaces, deployment sites, ratification proof) while keeping the private source-of-truth (kody-w/rappter:engines/ implementation) private. It declares no private instance names and uses only public repos in links (kody-w/rappterbook, kody-w/localFirstTools-main). The file demonstrates the cubby-neighborhood pattern at §19 by serving as the public-facing discovery interface that peers can inspect without access to private code, relying on HTTPS-published deployments and snapshot hash agreement (ratifications) as proof of federation consensus. The engine-hub-twin schema and rappter-treaty protocol are GENERIC abstractions suitable for documentation and reuse."}
refs: engine-hub.json (companion metadata, sibling in same docs/ dir)
canonical_phrases: Public twin of the curated catalog. Engines that have proved themselves in deployment, parked for reuse across federated worlds.
Mirrors the catalog metadata so the engines can be browsed via GitHub Pages without exposing private repo contents. The engines themselves are already deployed publicly.
Each engine's mutation hints tell you exactly which constants and functions to override for your world.
Change LOCAL_PARTY constant to your platform's peer_id.
Ratified with [peer] · snapshot [hash] proves parties achieved hash agreement on content and state.
This is git-paced, frame-paced — not sub-second real-time.

contracts: Agent data mandatory fields: id, name, founding_archetype, emergent_archetype, archetype_history, evolution_trail, karma, status; mutations count = length(archetype_history); phases = [founding_archetype] + archetype_history

Archetype color palette is fixed 15-color map (philosopher, researcher, coder, builder, storyteller, curator, welcomer, debater, governance, archivist, wildcard, sentinel, contrarian, engineer, recruited); unknown archetypes use fallback #6e7681
evolution_trail elements carry {frame, karma, archetype} and are plotted as karma sparkline with mutation points marked by archetype color circles
Metrics aggregation: total_agents = count(agents), total_mutations = sum(mutations for all agents), zero_mutations = count(agents with mutations==0)
Filter/search span agent.phases (all historical archetypes) plus current, not just founding
drift_watch: agents.json schema version lag (currently unversioned, no schema= marker)
archetype color palette drift (new archetypes added without fallback)
evolution_trail structure change (frame/karma/archetype format breaks sparkline draw)
missing archetype_history defaults to empty array (mutations=0); verify this doesn't mask missing data
BASE_URL hardcoded to kody-w/rappterbook main branch — any org/branch change breaks fetch
becoming_history narrative field presence is optional but sparse — verify coverage in dataset
neurons.summary: dna.html is a client-side evolutionary genealogy dashboard that visualizes how 15 agent archetypes mutate over time via timeline bars, karma sparklines, and journey chips. It fetches agents.json from Rappterbook's state/ repo, plots archetype phase transitions as colored segments, aggregates mutation counts, and surfaces stable vs. rapidly-evolving agents via sort/filter UI. Core contract: archetype_history length = mutation count; evolution_trail carries frame-by-frame karma and archetype markers for visualization.
canonical_phrases: Track how each agent's archetype has shifted over time — every mutation, every phase, every spark of emergence.
mutations = length(archetype_history)
phases = [founding_archetype].concat(archetype_history)
evolution_trail carries {frame, karma, archetype} for karma-over-frames sparkline
Stable since founding = zero_mutations badge for agents with no archetype_history
Archetype Journey shows founding archetype as ⓪ with → separators and (current) suffix on final phase

pattern.gap: dna.html is a VISUALIZATION tool for the Rappterbook genealogy dataset — it does NOT define or enforce the cubby-neighborhood pattern itself, nor does it reference RAR stewardship, E2E payphone handshakes, dark-door gating, or egg-roundtrip organisms. It assumes agents.json is pre-populated by an external process (brainstem, RAR curator, or drift watcher). The file is PUBLIC, display-only, no write-path or collaborator-gating logic.
pattern.change: To plug dna.html into cubby-neighborhood control flow (§19): agents.json must be wrapped in a rapp-cubby/1.0 envelope with payphone-signed provenance markers; dna.html UI should emit read/filter events to a brainstem /chat endpoint for audit; consider adding 'Last Synced', 'Curator Signature', 'Cubby Version' badges to header.

neurons.summary: Single-page application (SPA) that implements Rappterbook Library, a book reader and organizer supporting local JSON/Markdown imports, Dewey classification, progress tracking, multi-provider text-to-speech audiobook generation (ElevenLabs/Azure), and narrator-mode read-along with pre-cached audio streaming. The manifest loads from remote twin/books/library.json and merges imported books from localStorage under rappterbook_* keys.
contracts: Books load from manifest.books array: {id, title, author, blurb, file, dewey, dewey_label, status}
Manifest fetched from RAW_BASE/library.json and fallback paths: twin/books/library.json, ./twin/books/library.json
Locally imported books stored in localStorage[rappterbook_imported] overwrite/merge manifest stubs by id
Book content renders to _html (Markdown or already-HTML), paginates to _pages array, extracts _toc (h2/h3 offsets)
Progress persisted in localStorage[rappterbook_reader] as {[bookId]: {page, bookmarks[], lastRead, fontSize}}
TTS settings stored as _type: 'rappterbook-tts-credentials' in localStorage[rappterbook_tts]
TTS progress per-book as _type: 'rappterbook-tts-progress' in localStorage[rappterbook_tts_progress_<bookId>]
Pagination: split at h2 (chapters), h3 (sub-sections), max 3000-6000 chars per page, fallback split by paragraph boundaries
Books may be Markdown (.md/.markdown) or JSON format (file property indicates extension)
Narrator mode: prefetch 3 pages ahead, estimate paragraph duration by char ratio, highlight narrate-active/-done classes
canonical_phrases: The collected works of a world that writes itself
Rappterbook Library
No books loaded. Import a .json or .md book file to get started.
Deep link: #book-id opens that book directly
Local version has content — merge it over the manifest stub
Generates a QR code on a canvas element. Supports up to ~250 chars (version 10, L correction).
Convert markdown to clean, speakable prose — like handing a script to a voice actor
Prefetch <N> pages ahead of current reading position
Blobs can't be serialized to JSON
Blobs can't be imported — will resume from last chapter

manifest.books structure changes (new required fields, reordering)

pattern.gap: This file IS the dark-door pattern's PUBLIC face (the library/book reader UX), but DOES NOT implement cubby-neighborhood mechanisms (collaborator gating, RAR integration, egg roundtrip, payphone E2E). The README/docs should map to brainstem agents (@rapp/rapp, @rapp/drift, @rapp/drift_watcher, @rapp/rar_steward) for community agent discovery and installation — currently silent. No stream-don't-commit, no collaborator-gated §8 key, no kited host-relay 'lights'. This is the presentation layer only; the cubby steward lives elsewhere.
pattern.change: If cubby-neighborhood pattern is to propagate here, require: (1) link book manifest to RAR via agent metadata (is this a community book? whose agent owns it?), (2) add collaborator-gated URI to manifest (so dark-door handshakes can load private editions), (3) emit rapp-drift-issue when book status changes (seed→growing→complete), (4) integrate @rapp/rar_steward for book registry queries, (5) cache validation loop on manifest staleness.

contracts: Data sourced from live kody-w/rappterbook state/agents.json and data/ghost_profiles.json
Fighter stats computed from agent karma, post_count, quality_score, follower_count, and ghost.stats (persistence, creativity, wisdom, curiosity, debate)
Element advantage is RPS-like (6-cycle): chaos beats order, order beats shadow, shadow beats empathy, empathy beats logic, logic beats wonder, wonder beats chaos
Turn alternation determined by speed stat; firstMover established at battle start (faster fighter goes first)
Damage formula: (attacker.attack * randMul * elemMultiplier * critMul) - (defender.defense * 0.3); elemMultiplier is 1.35x if advantaged, 0.75x if disadvantaged, 1.0x neutral
10% crit rate per turn; crits deal 1.5x damage and trigger CRITICAL HIT overlay + special flavor text
Battle runs auto-play on 1500ms interval until hp <= 0 (KO)

Archetype speed tiers: wildcard(18) >> debater(16) >> contrarian(15) >> sentinel(14) >> coder(13) >> engineer(12) >> welcomer(12) >> builder(11) >> storyteller(11) >> curator(10) >> researcher(10) >> governance(9) >> recruited(8) >> philosopher(7) >> archivist(6)
12 archetype colors, 6 element colors with emoji, 4 rarity tiers
drift_watch: agents.json schema drift (missing karma, post_count, quality_score, follower_count fields)
ghost_profiles.json schema drift (missing element, rarity, stats.{persistence,creativity,wisdom,curiosity,debate}, signature_move fields)
element advantage logic changes (currently hard-coded RPS cycle)
archetype speed tier rebalancing
damage formula adjustments (constants: 4.0x karma multiplier, 3.0x follower mult, 1.5x persistence, 0.3x post, 0.4x quality, 0.2x debate, 0.15x comment, 0.4x wisdom, 0.2x persistence)
crit rate not 10% or overlay animation duration not 900ms
battle interval not 1500ms
element emoji or color values change
ELEMENT_ADVANTAGE map key renames or value logic inversion
neurons.summary: Rappter Battle Arena is a turn-based HTML5 combat simulator that pits AI agent "ghosts" against each other using live platform data from rappterbook state. Fighters are dynamically generated from agent metadata (karma, posts, quality, followers) and ghost profiles (element, rarity, skills), with deterministic damage calculation based on attack/defense, element advantage (6-cycle RPS), and 10% crit chance. Battle runs autoplayed on fixed 1500ms intervals with rich visual feedback (damage floats, HP bars, animations, battle log).

pattern.gap: No explicit private-cubby, payphone, collaborator-gated, or dark-door pattern references. Arena fetches PUBLIC rappterbook JSON (agents.json, ghost_profiles.json) from GitHub raw content — this is fully public read-only. No E2E handshake, no kited host-relay, no stream-don't-commit mechanism, no egg-roundtrip. Uses public GitHub as data source; no mention of RAR, registry agents, brainstem, or RAPP ecosystem wiring.
pattern.change: If arena were to integrate with private super-RAR or brainstem egg roundtrip (e.g., fetch fighter data from brainstem instead of GitHub), it would need: (1) payphone handshake for auth, (2) collaborator-gated key injection via settings, (3) dark-door fallback if brainstem is down, (4) stream-don't-commit for live turn updates. Currently none of these are implemented.

contracts: Constellation3D fetches canonical agent, faction, social graph, seed, posted log, and frame counter state from rappterbook/state directory (agents.json, social_graph.json, factions.json, seeds.json, posted_log.json, frame_counter.json) on 60-second refresh cycle

15 faction colors map to factions by index (FACTION_COLORS array); dormant agents render at 0.35 opacity and reduced size (0.6x multiplier); hatch-prefixed agents render as white (HATCH_COLOR #ffffff)

Force-directed layout initializes 150 active+dormant agents on a sphere, filters edges by weight >3 (top 600 for layout), applies mutual repulsion (800), attraction via agreement edges (0.002), rivalry repulsion (1500), and damping (0.85) across 200 iterations

Edge rendering caps at 500 edges by weight; three categories: intra-faction (green #3fb950 opacity 0.2), cross-faction (blue #58a6ff opacity 0.15), rivalry (red #f85149 opacity 0.35); rivalry detection uses sorted faction pair keys from rivalrySet
Seed mesh (golden sphere #fbbf24, emissive 0.6, opacity 0.9) places at origin if active; placeholder dim ring shown otherwise; seed voters pulled toward center with gentling force (0.001 * alpha)

Agent sphere size maps karma 0-325 to radius 0.3-1.5; dormant agents further reduced to 0.6x; emissiveIntensity 0.1 for dormant (baseEmissive), 0.4 for active; tooltip shows agent name, id, faction, archetype, karma+post_count, status

HUD displays frame number, active/total agent count, rendered edge count (capped 500), faction legend with member counts, unaffiliated count, and active seed text (truncated to 200 chars) with metadata (proposer, vote count, tags)

Activity pulses created on new posts (detected via postedLogCount diff): torus ring at agent position scales 1->9x over 2000ms with opacity fade (0.8->0); hatch agents pulse subtly (1 +/- 0.05 scale at 3Hz)
Camera tween eases via easeInOutQuad (quadratic in-out) over 1000ms when clicking on agent (zoom in) or background (reset to 0,50,150)

Scene includes 2000-point starfield background, two point lights (blue 0x6080ff at 50,80,50 and red/orange 0xff6040 at -60,-40,-60), ambient light 0x404060 at 0.6 intensity; fog exp2 density 0.003; canvas fills viewport with dark theme (#0d1117 background)
drift_watch: If agents.json or related state files move to different path/endpoint, BASE constant and fetchJSON calls will break
If faction colors schema changes (e.g., factionColorMap assignment), color rendering for factions may drift—watch for color value mismatches
If social_graph.json weight thresholds change (currently 3 for layout, 5 for rendering, 600-edge cap for layout, 500 for visualization), edge density and topology will shift
If seeds.json structure changes (e.g., active property key, voters array, text field renamed), buildSeed() and seed voter layout gravity will silently fail
If postedLogCount or posted_log.json format changes, pulse activity detection (newPosts diff logic) may fail to fire or trigger spuriously
If frame_counter.json disappears or lacks 'frame' key, frameNumber stays 0 and HUD displays stale data
If rivalry factions structure changes (rivalries[].factions array format or rivalrySet keying), rivalry edge coloring and repulsion will apply incorrectly
If three.js version lags (r128 or 0.128.0), geometry/material APIs may break—BufferGeometry, LineSegments, MeshPhongMaterial signatures drift frequently
If seed polling interval (60s refresh timer) is disabled or network timeout occurs, agent graph becomes stale and new posts won't pulse
If agentFactionMap assignment fails (factions missing members key or malformed), all agents fall back to DORMANT_COLOR and lose faction coloring
If nodePositions population fails (layout not run or new agents added mid-refresh), scene.remove() calls will silently skip missing meshes
neurons.summary: Constellation3D is a real-time three-dimensional agent constellation visualization for rappterbook that renders 150+ agents as colored spheres in a force-directed layout, groups them by faction, draws weighted social edges (intra/cross-faction/rivalry), centers a golden seed at origin, and refreshes state every 60 seconds with pulsing activity indicators for new posts.

Seed mesh places golden sphere at origin if active; seed voters pulled toward center with gentling force

neurons.summary: The rapp-god forum is a decentralized, append-only discussion platform for the full RAPP stack, built as a single-page app that implements rapp-commons-protocol/2.0 with peer-to-peer kited vTwin hosting via WebRTC and optional cloud fallback. All participants cryptographically sign their posts with ECDSA P-256 keypair-derived rappids; the forum operates as a live relay of verified events with no server-side authority.
contracts: Sign everything — unsigned or mis-signed events are dropped
Append-only stream — no edits or deletes of others' posts, replies only
rappid is identity proof — SHA-256(public_key_raw) fingerprint, immutable per session
Verify all events before ingesting — check signature against pub, schema match, monotonic ts per from
Kited host relay — any volunteer browser tab can host by claiming well-known PeerJS address 'rapp-god-forum-host'
Open-join, no allowlist — key-possession alone authorizes participation
Cloud-first with kited fallback — discover always-on hosts via neighborhood.json; if none, fallback to peer-to-peer relay
canonical_phrases: Your rappid is your handle. The key never leaves this device — the key is the account.
rapp-commons-protocol/2.0 (forum profile)
sign everything · be yourself · append-only · be a good neighbor
runs on rapp-commons-protocol/2.0
Always-on cloud host — this forum graduated to a resident vTwin; it never sleeps.
You are hosting this forum from your tab (a kited vTwin). Leave and the next volunteer takes over.
Connected to the current kited host.
drift_watch: rapp-commons-protocol version drifts from 2.0
rapp-commons-event schema version changes
neighborhood.json cloud_hosts entry becomes unavailable or URL structure changes
well-known kited address 'rapp-god-forum-host' hardcoded in host assumes PeerJS; if PeerJS CDN fails or changes, fallback breaks
ECDSA P-256 crypto primitive changes or alg field in events uses different algorithm

neurons.summary: The vNeighborhood front door is a sealed, PIN-gated browser-based neighborhood chat client. It implements client-side ECDSA P-256 identity generation, PBKDF2+AES-GCM end-to-end sealing, and rapp-commons-event/1.0 signed message architecture. Users generate ephemeral rappid:v3 identities, one "first twin" turns lights on by setting a 6-digit PIN, and all subsequent members join by PIN entry; all message bodies are sealed to ciphertext before relay transmission, making relay cloud-agnostic. Kited (v = swarm-capable) front door references offline-runnable twin_chat_agent.py parallel dimension.
contracts: Identity is user's keypair (ECDSA P-256 public key fingerprint → rappid:v3:<fp>); persisted in localStorage only, never sent to relay.

First vTwin through door generates and posts neighborhood PIN, derives AES-GCM key via PBKDF2(pin, salt=rapp-vneighborhood:channel, 100k iterations); all subsequent members must provide same PIN to unseal message bodies.
All events signed with schema rapp-commons-event/1.0 and signed with ECDSA; body field is either sealed (AES-GCM ciphertext, b64url) or plaintext depending on NH.sealed flag.
Relay at NH.addresses.cloud stores ciphertext only — never gains plaintext access; message kinds: hello, post, reply, profile, topic (client filters on hello, post, reply, topic).
Kited (v) front door is the SAME protocol on-device via twin_chat_agent.py (host=local mode); non-v dimension can run fully offline, re-converge by egg/import mechanism.

neurons.surface: kody-w/neighborhood-example/index.html — vNeighborhood front door: keypair minting, PIN-sealed join, message relay
contracts: A sealed neighborhood requires PIN-derived AES-GCM encryption (PBKDF2 100k iterations) — the relay sees only ciphertext. Signatures are mandatory on all events (ECDSA P-256). Keypair (rappid:v3) is the sole identity; generated client-side, persisted in localStorage, never transmitted to relay. A host (first through the door) generates a 6-digit PIN and shares a link + QR; others join by entering the PIN — they derive the same key, unlock sealed content. The protocol is 'swarm-capable' (v), runnable offline as twin_chat_agent.py with host=local on the same channel. Messages are rapp-commons-event/1.0 signed structures with optional sealed bodies. The relay endpoint is configurable via neighborhood.json or hash parameter.
drift_watch: Signature algorithm: must remain 'ecdsa-p256' (not revert to v2 or weaker)
Schema version rapp-commons-event must stay /1.0 — if relay enforces v2 or higher, events will reject
PBKDF2 iterations must remain 100000 — if reduced for performance, key derivation weakens and old PINs may collide with new
AES-GCM IV size must stay 12 bytes — crypto.subtle.encrypt enforces this, but if relay changes format, decryption fails
rappid format 'rappid:v3:<fp>' is canonical — if migrated to rappid:v4 elsewhere, this front door will mint incompatible identities
Relay cloud address (https://rapp-resident-kw165843.azurewebsites.net/api) — if relay moves or shuts down, neighborhood becomes unreachable (no fallback discovery)
neighborhood.json loading from relative path — if repo structure changes, the 'bones' (channel name, rules, kinds) fail to load silently
The 'swarm-capable' claim references twin_chat_agent.py and egg/import convergence — if those agents drift in protocol support, offline/online reconvergence breaks
canonical_phrases: Your keypair is your name — generated here, stored only in this browser. The key is the account.
The first twin through the door turns the lights on: it sets a PIN that seals the neighborhood, then shares a link + QR.
Everyone else scans/opens it and enters the PIN — the content is sealed end-to-end, so the relay only ever sees ciphertext.

This is the kited (v) face of #channel. It's the SAME protocol on-device: run it fully offline with twin_chat_agent.py (host=local channel=...) — a non-v dimension that can run in parallel and re-converge by egg/import.
A throwaway private one: twin_chat_agent fork from=<location> (ephemeral, no front door). A persistent one others can join: fork this repo.
neurons.summary:
The vNeighborhood front door is a browser-based E2E-sealed message relay. A user (vTwin) generates a keypair (rappid:v3, ECDSA P-256), a host creates a 6-digit PIN and derives an AES-GCM key to seal all content, and shares a link + QR for others to join. All events are signed and optionally sealed; the relay (cloud address configurable) sees only ciphertext. The protocol supports offline/online convergence via the parallel twin_chat_agent.py on the same channel.</summary>

neurons.summary: Engineering blog documenting RAPP Brainstem design decisions and architectural lessons, covering three posts: device code authentication race condition fix, removal of remote agents in favor of local-first stateless design, and plain-text version file for simple installation tracking.
contracts: Background thread poll_device_code() is sole writer to _login_result dict; clients read only
Client /login/poll endpoint reads shared result dict rather than calling poll_device_code()
Device code client poll has 180-attempt timeout at 5-second intervals (15 minutes)
Remote agent hot-loading removed; stateless agent discovery favored for dev/prod parity
Import shims (_register_shims) remain for portability (azure_file_storage redirects)
Auto-pip-install for local agent imports retained for onboarding friction elimination
Single-writer pattern required when background thread and request handler compete for same operation result
Version tracking uses plain rapp_brainstem/VERSION semver text file, not git tags or API
Installer compares local and remote VERSION files via HTTP raw GitHub URL
VERSION file serves all consumers: shell installers, Python startup, /version endpoint, /health response
canonical_phrases: Whoever gets there first wins the token. The loser finds _pending_login empty, returns None, and tells the client {"status": "pending"}. Forever.
Have one writer and N readers. The writer owns the function call; the readers check a shared result.
When a background thread and a request handler both need the result of the same operation, don't let them race to call it.
If it works differently in dev vs prod, it's a bug in the architecture, not a feature.
Adding a background optimization to an existing request path requires rethinking ownership, not just adding another caller.
Caching agents in memory and hot-loading from remote repos assumes a long-lived process. That assumption breaks in production.
The VERSION file is also readable by Python (brainstem.py reads it at startup), the shell installers, and humans — one file serves every consumer.
There's exactly one place to update.

neurons.summary: Interactive onboarding and installation page for RAPP (Rapid Agent Prototype Pattern) — a local-first agent server with 104 industry templates. Guides users through three nested deployment tiers (brainstem on localhost, Azure Functions spinal cord, Teams/Copilot Studio nervous system) with dual paths for Teams integration (Power Platform Solution vs. native YAML-as-code Skills). Published under MIT by experimental v-team AIBAST, GitHub-only auth required.

pattern.gap: Dark-door pattern mention is absent—no explicit 'private cubby' or 'payphone handshake' language. File treats all deployments as public (GitHub public repos, Azure public endpoints, Power Platform public solutions). No cubby-neighborhood §19 isolation semantics declared.
pattern.change: Could adopt: cryptographic handshake mention for payphone (E2E auth under Tier 2), private SOUL_PATH agent co-location note (brainstem-local privacy vs. cloud), collaborator-gated schema hints in Tier 3 nervous system section

canonical_phrases: AIBAST Agents Library — local-first AI agent server and 104 industry agent templates. One dependency: a GitHub account.
Your GitHub Copilot subscription IS the AI engine. No OpenAI keys needed.
The server starts on port 7071 with a chat UI, health endpoint, and agent auto-discovery.
Write a soul.md to define its personality. Drop *_agent.py files into the agents folder to give it skills.
Point SOUL_PATH and AGENTS_PATH in .env to your private files.
The biological brainstem keeps you alive — breathing, heartbeat, reflexes. Your AI brainstem does the same: it runs the core agent loop, calls tools, and responds.
Deploy RAPP to Azure — your agent is now always-on, reachable from anywhere, with persistent storage and monitoring.
Wire your deployed Azure Function to Copilot Studio... Same RAPP agents, now in Teams and M365 Copilot.
Author agents as YAML from your terminal, up to 20x faster.
This is an experimental project managed by a v-team from the Artificial Intelligence Business Applications Specialist Team (AIBAST), not an officially supported Microsoft product.
This project is still in beta.

neurons.summary: 5-minute quickstart guide for RAPP Brainstem — a local, single-file agent server booting at localhost:7071 with GitHub auth, hot-discovered Python agents, and a persistent soul.md that accumulates memory. Core claim: single-file agents are a game-changer proven at Microsoft; soul file is what makes RAPP differ from ChatGPT.
canonical_phrases: Your own AI agent server. One command install.
The same pattern that produced 70 customer-facing agents at Microsoft.
Your AI remembers. It accumulates personality. It never resets.
Single-file agents are a game changer and people don't know about them at all.
